user_comment: Good points: Very deep black (and they come with a warning that the colour might rub off on lighter textiles!) VERY stretchy if that's what you want Nice and long in the legs. I'm 5'11" and could wear boots or heels with these Modern boot cut (more on that later) Drawbacks: The FIT: I always wear a 12 in Lee jeans and these were a bit tight. I could wear them if I didn't mind the muffin top due to the fact they are smaller than any of my other jeans and the "midrise" is very low (to me, at least). If you are looking for mid-to-high, really, like me, do not buy these. The STRETCH: VERY stretchy. The kind of stretch that snaps in to hug your body no matter what. And, I don't know why, but that sort of pants always seems to get folds in the backs of the thighs (the front, too, if they are loose, but these are not). They are also very stifling in the heat. If, like me, you live somewhere where it is 30 degrees Celsius much of the year, do not order these. You're welcome! (Looked it up, that's 86 in Fahrenheit.) The CUT: As I said, these are modern boot cut. Maybe I am really looking for flares. 20 years ago, what we called boot cut flared out right below the knees and looked great. These grab me right down to mid-calf (so everyone can clearly see how knock-kneed I am - if my legs were straight, I'd be even taller!). Then they flare waaaaaay out. I find that pretty ugly on me. I guess they could work with boots if you have small calves (I do not). They are indeed huge through the ankles, but I think they would be pretty snug over boots in the lower calf. Also, they are on the low side of midrise, which is another huge negative for high-waisted me. Hope this helps!

I love the high waisted style - the loops are large enough to fit a nice belt and ALL pockets are actually pockets ☺️ I have to hem them a little since jeans are usually long on me which I don’t mind. I like how you can get a more loose flaired end at the bottom or cuff them for a more tapered look (see images). I appreciate the looseness of the legs while still making my behind look good and not frumpy. Just know that these don’t have any stretch to them, and if you have lower belly fat like me, this won’t do a great job of flattening/hiding it.

I like how you can get a more loose flaired end at the bottom or cuff them for a more tapered look (see images). I appreciate the looseness of the legs while still making my behind look good and not frumpy. Just know that these don’t have any stretch to them, and if you have lower belly fat like me, this won’t do a great job of flattening/hiding it.
Great jeans! Love the flair that’s trending. I’m 5’6” and i can wear with a heel or some platform sneakers.

Haven’t worn jeans in years, instead ankle-length, moderately supportive athletic leggings had become my mainstay. I am 5’2”, approx 115 lbs with tummy and let’s just say not massively firm from tush through to thighs — despite being a casual 3x/week runner... After trial and error, I have determined I am a 2S... Any bigget or longer and they would not hit my ankles in the perfect spot, and the waist and butt would be too loose... These things are A-MAZING... they provide support, smooth out my abundant lumpiness, keep my muffin top in check and feel great! I have yet to see how they stand up to linger wearing, whether they stretch out and wash well, but will back with an update once I do... While I certainly have obvious issues and a body befitting a 50-something, somewhat active woman battling the UNIVERSAL challenges of a changing physique (ahem) I am pleasantly surprised EVEN PLEASED with the magic of these jeans... I kinda feel like I am misrepresenting myself to the world (but in a good way, lol) .... Love these so much that I have been wanting to order them in GALA, but they are not available... So as a back up I ordered another in Cape Town— the wash highlight on my first pair was perfect and happily, the over all tone is darker than it appears in the product pic. But the second pair ordered was visibly larger in the thighs and lighter in color overall and so I am returning them and hoping to re-purchase one that fits like the first and will wait patiently and hopefully until they offer the GALA in 2S. If you’re on the fence I highly recommend you take a chance and order these… I would also recommend Levi’s gold label modern skinny Jean — very similar feel and fit and currently available in a very dark wash called immaculate… I have already purchased two of those and I’m very pleased… I never thought I’d have a jean wardrobe: I am thrilled! Thank you Amazon! Than you Levi’s 👍🏼🙏💕😎😍

Having packed on the Covid 15, I was scared to get my actual size. I went with a 22, but now wish I had gone for the 20. At 6 foot, 265 pounds, it’s nearly impossible to find jeans long enough. I’ve been looking for the perfect pair of jegging‘s for over a year. These pants are thicker than leggings, but terrific. They don’t lift my bum, but at 55 I don’t care. They are super stretchy, Maybe if I had gone a size smaller, they would hold my stomach in more, but they don’t feel super strong. They are very comfortable. AND LONG ENOUGH! I will be keeping these for my fatty days, and will order another one in size 20, hoping for more tummy support. I love that they are truly high waisted (over my belly button), have four real sized pockets, and come below my ankles. I highly recommend. Don’t judge me on my pics, LOL.

Here's my wife for this review:I wanted a pair of jeans that I could be comfortable in and look a little up to date with the trends and I didn't want them to be blue necessarily. I saw these offered in a few colors and loved the olive green color. I also loved that these are high waisted and have a loose fitting to these. The straight legged barrel look is popular right now and honestly it's a comfortable style to wear. I ordered them in a size 10 which is my go to size. I'm 5'6 and 160 pounds. I think they could be a little baggier but they fit me in the waist nicely. They're a LOT of stretch to these jeans which adds to the comfort. I can roll the pant legs up to create a wide hem which is in style today or I can leave them long which fits me like a good hemmed pair of jeans would be. I do have shorter legs than most people so perhaps this would be above the ankle on most people which is also the style today. I also love that you could just cut the hem and leave it raw which is also the style of today!I haven't worn them for a long time so I don't know how the stretch factor is. If I see an issue I will back but for now I really am enjoying these jeans. They're assembled nicely. I do see a little hesitation in the zipper when I zip them up, but I believe it's because of the stretch in the jeans. The zipper lays fine and doesn't gap or anything. For the money, style, and comfort, get yourself a pair of these.

Herseas Sweatpants are a, "wear all the time, everywhere, comfortable sweatpants". I'm not usually a huge polyester fan, but these pants are beyond comfortable. Easy to wash and wear, I washed them in a cool regular cycle with a medium heat tumble dry and they came out great. No shrinkage, stretching, or weird static feeling. They are soft, not clingy, and are the right length for my longer leg. I am 5' 8" weighing 195 lbs, I have a hard time finding sweatpants that are long enough in the leg. I got a XXL that fit perfectly. I highly recommend and want more of these!

Hey Amazon. This is an entirely unbiased and unpaid for product review. I got these for a cosplay I was doing (Pearl from Steven Universe). I originally meant to cut these into shorts however I kept them long because of weather. These came within a day with my student Prime which was excellent. The product was very well packaged and immediately I noticed how soft these were. They were the texture of a very thin velvet almost and the color stayed saturated even at my joints. Trying them on they fit all the way down to NY ankles. I'm 5'2" so I didnt really have any concerns, however, I believe a taller person would have problems with leg length because even for a short person these didn't go awfully long. Now although the material was very very soft it was also very thin. My butt was covered in my costume however I wouldn't recommend wearing these (at least the light colors) by themselves. Wearing standard underwear the pattern and crease of the underwear was very obvious and looked pretty bad. I think even with a thong it'd be way too obvious. So if you plan to wear these by themselves get a darker pair because the lighter pair won't satisfy your needs. With them being so thin they didn't keep in heat very well either and I was pretty cold even inside. Also, I developed a small hole near my crotch after dancing in them for a while, probably due to constant stretching. Overall I believe these are decent quality. I would consider buying a black pair as leggings if the material would be made a little thicker. If you need something for under a skirt or shorts then these are absolutely perfect. TDLR; Soft and well made but too thin

Hey there, so I don't usually buy clothes online because they simply don't fit; but I have learned to buy larger then I typically would need and it works out. In this case I ordered XL and they are a very comfortable fit. I am 5 ft 6 in and about 170 lb; I'm a little "hippy" at 41" and that's where jeans usually don't work for me. But these are a little loose, which I like, and stretchy; so I have mobility, which is another important factor. I did immediately notice that the open stars on the jeans look like little stickers you would just peel and stick in your notebook, but I have washed and dried them and they stayed on. I would imagine they won't last forever, but they would look fine without them, or I could put something else in their place. The material underneath the holes is like a microfiber, so it's thin, breathable and stretchy. I will be ordering some of the other designs for sure.

Hi! So okay, I have had a few pairs of these “soft, boho pattern flare pants” throughout my young life. I remember when they peaked back in early 2010s! So, if you see my photos, I already owned a few pairs in Red/Pink and Black/White. These pants are my favorite. They are so soft, such good quality. They feel like cotton, they’re pretty thick (and don’t show panty lines!), and they’re the perfect length on my 5’7 self, they are truly the best. I really wanted some in Blue, but I couldn’t find any in my area. So I decided to give Amazon a shot. I’m a bit disappointed. You may not be able to tell from my photos, but these pants are… different. The pattern is clearly laser printed. It doesn’t look as ‘real’ as the red and black ones. Also, the quality is much worse. It doesn’t feel like soft cotton, it feels more like polyester. The pattern does look good, but it’s just not the same. The fit is fine, but it’s more lightweight than my genuine boho pants. It’s also more see through!?! That’s not supposed to happen, IMO. Overall, they’re thinner, not as soft, and really cheaply made. But still, If you’re just going for a boho look, then go for it. I’d say, if you can, try to find the real deal boho flares in discount department stores.

I absolutely loved these jeans. Was a little skeptical because I'm 5 9 and 220lbs and it's hard to find jeans that fit my butt and thighs or long enough. These were comfortable and cute! Definitely a good addition to any closet!!!

I absolutely loved these joggers as soon as I opened the package - as per previous reviews they are incredibly soft, the length was perfect. I ordered the grey camo print and the color was exactly as displayed on my monitor. I normally wear size 0-2 and ordered Medium in these pants as most of the reviews mentioned to size up; that didn't quite work for me - they were too baggy in the waist as well as hips most likely because I'm not that curvy (waist - 25" and hips - 36"). I am exchanging them for size Small - I will update the review when the new pair arrives. UPDATE: After receiving the new pair in size Small I can honestly say that these are my favorite joggers ever (I'm going to order more in different colors and prints and I won't break the bank doing so :)). The fit is great : not tight, not baggy, the fabric is thin, buttery soft and washes well (the pants kept their shape after a few washes). The waist band is very elastic and the drawstring is easily removable if that's your preference. The one thing I would mention about these pants is that on my frame (5'4") they sit above my belly button, which would make them high-waisted; if you're much taller than me, that may not be the case for you.

I absolutely loved these pants while I was pregnant! I’m normally someone who wears a small but I did gain 50lbs during pregnancy so I ordered a medium. I’ve put them on since I’ve given birth and they are big but still fit. They are super stretchy and a soft athletic type material. I’m 5’2” and normally 120, most pants fit me long but these weren’t too bad. Overall, I highly recommend
I absolutely loved these pants. I’m 4’11” and 175lbs i bought an XL, tech could have gotten a large but was happy with how they fit!
I absolutely loved these pants. They have a flair leg and a stretch waist band. I just wish the were slightly longer. I am 5’9 and they hit slightly shorter than I would like.

I absolutely loved this dress. It was super comfortable. The light blue jean color was absolutely perfect! I am 5’9 and weigh 159 lbs, so I went ahead and bought a size large. I always like thinsg oversized rather than fitting, but I could’ve easily gone for Medium as well. You could dress it up or dress it down. I bought this for an event and have worn this probably way more than I expected to because it’s just that cute. Any type of jewelry goes with it and shoes you could put boots with it or like I did a pop of color sandal. Buy the dress, you won’t regret it!

I accidentally ordered Large (I typically wear XL) and the waist on these look so small. But, I tried them on and they fit perfectly! They are very comfortable and the material is very soft and stretchy. The only complaint I could have is the length. I took a chance hoping the true inseam would be short than as listed on the Size Chart, but the size chart is accurate. I'm just a short 5'4". The waist size is also accurate, but the material stretches very well without looking or feeling tight. I really appreciate the pockets and they are sewn flat so there's no added bulk and looks is sleek. These make great leisure-lounging pants.

I actually liked the fit of these, I’m 4”11, 143 lbs and purchases a size 6. I actually really like these pants , lots of stretch and good length. They are a bit too big on me. I’m usually a size 13-14 and I went with a size 14. I’m 165 pounds 4’2 . I will return for a size smaller.

I actually really like these pants! They look good with a heel. Yes, they are straight leg but a heel could help to make it look tapered. It makes me feel less of a frumpy woman. The only thing I’m not sure I like as much is the poochy tummy, but that might be my own belly making it look that way. The pants are overall nice and not scratchy. I adore the length and style of these slacks; they are super cute. I am almost 5'8", and they fit just like in the picture- they have a slouch if I wear flats and show my toes if I wear an inch heel. They look fine, I won't bother trying a medium, but I wonder if they might have suited me better.

I adore these overalls! They are the exact fit and style I wanted because they are wide leg and it has great adjustable straps. For sizing I usually wear a 6/8, am a 34C, about 5’5 and around 135lbs, and these fit me perfect!

I adore these overalls! They are the exact fit and style I wanted because they are wide leg and it has great adjustable straps. For sizing I usually wear a 6/8, am a 34C, about 5’5 and around 135lbs, and these fit me perfect!

I ADORED these pants when they first arrived. I’m 5ft 4.5in tall, 185 lbs, and ordered the XL. They were the perfect, breezy, floor-length pants of my dreams. They skimmed my problem area (my postpartum belly) gracefully and made my stomach look flatter. I washed them once on the cold delicate cycle and hung them to air dry and they shrank at least 2 or 3 inches. They used to touch the top of my feet, and now they are above my ankles. I’m not sure if I’ll wear them at all now - instead of lengthening my proportions, they make my legs look shorter and stubbier. Extremely disappointing and a waste of money. Save yourself the heartache! Unless you’re exactly 5’2, in which case they’d probably end up being perfect lol. Ladies, know your fabrics. Anything 100% rayon or viscose will shrink. Look for polyester options instead. Check my other reviews if you’d like to see the brand I found that worked way better for me.

I always get nervous ordering pants online because they never seem to fit right. I'm very happy with these. The material is stiffer than leggings, so they look like regular pants, but they are stretchy and comfortable like leggings. The waist is a little tight compared to how much room I have in the lower waist/belly area, but it's still fine. I would say I usually wear a size 8-10 and I ordered a Large in these pants. I'm also 5"3" and they are not too long on me, which was a relief. And I love the army green color. These pants will look great with boots in the fall. I would recommend.

I always have trouble finding the right pair of shorts because I'm short and thick, but these fit perfectly. I have ordered these shorts in gray and black about a year apart, and the quality was still the same. Not tight on my thighs, but still fit my butt nicely. Definitely not inappropriately short or oddly long (since I am 5'1"). The top comes right under my belly button. I always buy a size larger than my jeans so they don't squeeze my inner thigh or my stomach.

I always hesitated to get pants online, as you never know how they end up looking and fitting. Dresses and tops are much easier. I really wanted a pair of wide leg pants though, and saw these through the Vine program. Read the reviews and pretty much everyone who got them seemed happy with them, so I pulled the trigger. Happy to these really do look and fit very well and the white is not see through. Which was surprising, as the fabric is a fairly light (but not very thin) polyester. I am not a fan of synthetic fibers and would certainly prefer these in a linen blend, but I must say I do appreciate how they fall because of the material and the feel is not bad at all either. They're quite soft and silky. At this price, I think they're a great value and totally recommend them. Might get them in another color too. For reference, I am 5'5, 130 lbs and got an S.

I always look for good quality black leggings because they are basic and practical to have; you can wear them with anything. I ordered these to try them out. And I really liked them; I weigh about 155 pounds and I'm 5.5 feet tall. I ordered them in size S and they fit me perfectly. I love the way the color and the material look. I recommend them 100%.

I always thought this design kinda looked like a waitress’s apron. But, luckily, in person these shorts are actually pretty amazing. Good thing they’re so stretchy too, or I probably wouldn’t have fit in them. I’m 5’7”, 115lbs, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, and got an XS because short legs and hips are always super wide on me. Should’ve gotten the Small. The waistband is very tight, causing spillage when I hardly have any meat on my bones anyway, and the skirt panel pulls at the button. BUT, I still think they look incredible -I just won’t wear short tops. The front skirt is so uniquely stylish, flattering, and balances out well with the super short and tight back. I love all the pockets and, overall, the shorts seem very well-made. Also, they’re surprisingly comfortable. This is a great choice of shorts with fashionable flair. They will look fabulous on you. Just, go with your true size.

I always wanted a pair of these but was scared to order because some jeans fit me too loose in the thigh and it is not flattering. These are perfect. They flatter my shape and aren’t too big. At first they were tight, but they loosened up nicely after a few minutes. Now they are comfortable. The jeans are of very good quality. Not thin at all. But there is no stretch. The color is a nice medium denim color. For size reference I am 5’3 152 lbs with 41 inch hips. I have a meaty belly. And I got my usual size 29 (8) If I had gone up a size, they would have been unflattering throughout the thighs. Length is ok. Not too long but I did have to cuff them a little. Nothing crazy. I really like these jeans and I plan to order more colors.

I am (barely) 5’3” and 118 lbs. What I like: Soft fabric with good stretch. Fits everywhere but not so tight you can’t move freely. No waist gapping - love the inner elastic waist. Nice color. What I don’t like: Irritating tags sewn into the waistband. I am very sensitive to fabrics and textures and had to cut them out with my seam ripper. A minor inconvenience for jeans that fit so well, but that’s the reason for 4 stars instead of 5.

I am 13 weeks pregnant and already showing. My regular jeans are too tight so I wanted to find jeans for work to grow with me. I am 5'6 and 125lbs prepregnancy as well as a solid size 2 in jeans. I first ordered a small in the regular jeans and black jeans based on reviews however the legs were pretty baggy and I had to keep pulling up despite the belly band and waist being ok. I decided to return and get the xs. The xs fits soooo much better and I'm really glad I exchanged. These are super stretchy and comfortable just like my usual jeans and I am really hoping they last me through my pregnancy and post-partum journey. If you are hesitant on size, I would ask yourself first if you have bulkier legs, if you do, order normal size. If you want these to fit like true skinny jeans or jeggings, go with a size down
I am 130 lbs and bought a small and they fit great! The elastic waistband is very forgiving. I wish it was 100% cotton, but it is 97% cotton & 3% elastane.

I am 158lbs @ 5'3", measuring 36DD-32-42, and I normally wear a sz 12 in jeans. I bought a sz 10 in these pants based on other reviews saying to size down, and I loved how comfortable they seemed at first. As someone who has a small belly, I wasn't bothered by the waistband being slightly large, and the waistband didn't roll. They are very soft, not too thick or thin, have good stretch, and fit comfortably everywhere else. I did wish that they had real front pockets. I was ready to keep these, but as I wore them while getting ready to go out I noticed that they just didn't look right with any of my tops, and gave the appearance of being slightly baggy in front. I also had the worry they would loosen more while wearing so I'm returning them. It may be worth it to mention that they didn't come with any brand tag or price tag, so it's possible it was a previously returned item.

Also, in the summer heat, humidity and polyester don't mix. These were returned.

I am 175lb and 5’10” and I chose a size 10 long. IF THE OTHER COLORS HAD 10 LONG I WOULD BUY THEM ALL. Seriously, I hate work pants so much that I wear skirts and dresses in the winter! I am 180 and 5’3 1/2”. I love these jeans soooo much. Comfortable and fits perfectly in waist, butt and hits right at the belly button. The only thing that bums me out is the pants are like an inch or two too short in my opinion. My ankles are not covered.

I am 5 foot four, 115 pounds for reference and thankfully the length on these jeans is just right. I was worried they would be too long and might have liked if they were maybe a half an inch shorter, but they definitely work pretty well for me. They have a nice soft stretchy feel to them, but almost a tad too stretchy, especially when it comes to the waist and down the front button and zipper. It protrudes a little bit on me and there is this little extra pooch of fabric at the bottom just below the zipper, which I also noticed on the model in the item description photos. I would like it if they sucked me in a tiny bit more in that area, but I will be able to work with it, depending on what kind of top I wear. The only actual issue I have with these jeans is that they have that kind of musty smell right out of the package. I immediately put them in the wash, hoping it would just go away, but unfortunately, it is still there. I guess it isn’t horrible, but it still bugs me and would obviously be more ideal if they just smelled like nothing! Overall, cute for sure though, and I will definitely get some use out of them.

I am 5' 10'' tall, I weigh 175lbs and ordered an XL...fits great. I typically wear size 16-18 up in pants but closer to 16. I love how the legs are pinned up to show the fray at the bottom. But if you don't like them pinned up you can take them down easily. The bottom of the pants are pinned up by only a couple/few stitches in four places on each leg. These overalls have five pockets-two roomy pockets on each side in the front, two smaller pockets on the back and one large pocket (shaped like a back pocket) on the front. And did I mention these overalls have STRETCH? Matter of fact, there are four buttons on each side to undo to assist in putting these on but with so much stretch in this fabric I didn't need to undo even one button. Although these overalls don't look quite as sexy on me as they do the model, I do like them. And I plan on getting a lot of wear out of them.

I am 5' 3' and I ordered these light blue straight leg jeans in my usual size of 16. I am 5’ 3” and weight 135lbs with an hourglass shape. I ordered an 8 since my waist is about 28 inch and my hips are 39 inch. If you’re short like me, either wear some tall heels or hem the pants. I expect to take off a good 4 inches off of mine. The material is cotton and upon first wear is stiff. I expect them to soften with use. The pants are nicely dyed with good contrast for what is a cow print and come with deep pockets! I can fit my entire hand down the front pockets and the silhouette remains flattering. I think the sizing is true to size just not accounting for petites. The pants take a nice fit to my bottom so that’s a win in my book. The rise of the pants is around the waist which many women like for the tummy control. Overall, would recommend for ladies 5 ft 6in and taller.

I am 5’ and I weigh 97-100 pounds it just depends! These pants around about 1 inch about my belly button and they fit like my Hollister (00) vintage mom jeans. Everyone said go up a size but I didn’t do that and I stayed my usual size which is a size 25. I was honestly thinking of sizing down but the reviews made me second guess everything but if I would’ve dozed down, they would’ve been too tight because these pants have little stretch. My Hollister jeans don’t really fit my waste fully fitted, but I don’t mind any of that because I do like to wear belts. I think if your pant size is your true pant size, for instance you’re a size 26 but you can only fit into a size 26 and not a Size 25, then maybe you should size up because the pants are were thick and aren’t very stretch at all. Also, these pants are VERY long but that was no surprise for me. It took me 6 rolls just to have it fit the leg of my length but you can always trim those down but of course the taller you are, the less amount of rolls you’ll have to do. But other than that, I do like the fit! I’m happy with the sizing I got and won’t be sizing down or up. I really hope this helps you!

I am 5' approx 125lbs. These fit so perfect! I got 5 colors, ALL IN SMALL REGULAR. I got the: Off white thick Black thick Khaki thin Pink thin Black coffee thick I'm glad I went with my gut/some reviews & didn't get 'short'. They all fit the same for the most part. Perfect around my waist with comfortable stretch left, but it wasn't loose it was fitted stretch left if that makes sense? Length was good on all of them except for the black coffee, that one needs to be hemmed and is super long. The rest fit perfect WITH heels on, which was my intent. If I want to wear them with any other shoe I will need to get them hemmed 100%. As far as thick vs thin goes, they feel like regular pants, neither thick or thin, I would have to wear the opposite to tell the difference. Although, I did wear the 'pink thin' out and you could see my cellulite LOL so maybe because it's thin. The black coffee is thick, the only one that does feel a bit warmer to me. The rest I would consider regular pants. The red heels in the photos are kitten heels, so not that high and the pink pants were above the ground. I tried to be very detailed! I hope this helps you! I definitely recommend so classy and chic!!

I am 5'1, 150 pounds, 36 weeks pregnant and I purchased a meduim. I have bought so many different jeans/pants between this pregnancy and my first. I cannot recommend getting these jeans enough. The belly band is super comfy and unlike all the others I have tried, these pants stay in place. I am not constantly adjusting or pulling them up. The pockets on the front and back are both real. Also, the booty doesnt have the ugly 'maternity' look. They look just as good as nirmal jeans!! So glad I made this purchase. I wish I would have gotten them sooner. I am 5'1" 105lbs for reference. First impressions, these pants are AWESOME. I have a ton of harem and boho type pants but non that are this kind of material. It is almost like a cross between jersey and bathing suit material, is pretty thick compared to similar type pants and has a ton of stretch! The pattern/colors are exactly as shown in the listing. I absolutely LOVE these pants but alas! I cannot give them 5 stars, They are very long and are also too big. The elastic on the waist band is not very tight. The bottom seem where the band meets the pants is the tightest part of the entire waist band and even that to big to stay up. The first photo is where I think and would like the pants to sit, however, the second photo is where they fall. I cannot keep them any higher. They fall down when I walk, not completely, but enough where I caught myself constantly pulling them up. The third picture is me on my tip toes. You can see how the length is pretty perfect at that height but you can see by the other photos how it sits just standing regular. Because of the length, I was also constantly stepping on the bottoms which also pulled them down as well. I am washing them now so I will see if they shrink at all. Hoping they do but I have a feeling they won't due to the material. As is, I really need an XS or XXS in these pants to wear comfortably. If the waist band was actually tight and held them up securely, I could def wear a size small out of the house. But, as is, I can't see myself being able to wear these pants outside due to the constant falling down. So if you are more petite like me, I would pass on these unless they add smaller sizes.

I am 5'10" tall, 150 lbs. My waist is 31, my hips are 39, my upper thigh is 23.5, my knee is 16.5, my calf 14.5, and my inseam is 34.5. I ordered an 8 long. (My legs are large, so these are the closest I can realistically get to a somewhat skinnier, non-baggy look.) This is online shopping, ladies: let's help each other out with stats! My local stores don't carry tall jeans, so I can only order online, and I need all the help I can get choosing! Buyers of this Levi style say the denim fabric is thin - it certainly is - and it is probably the reason it does not cut into my waist like the Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda classic straight jeans, which I wore in my 50's, do now. I am switching to this Levi style now I've entered my 60's; my waist is bigger and more sensitive to pressure from stiff and heavy fabrics than it was in earlier stages of my life. For someone my height, the pair I ordered in 8 long covers my ankle bone, so my socks won't show when I wear shoes. This is all I was looking for in that regard. Bottom line: Yes, the waist runs big - hallelujah! - and there is some stretching out, but, I personally have never ever owned a pair of jeans that hasn't stretched out over a day of wearing, so this is not a deal breaker for me. Yes, the denim is thin, so if I want to wear these and be warm here in Michigan in winter, I will have to wear tights under them if I spend any significant time outside, which, I rarely do. I also wear a very long coat, so I expect to be fine regardless.

I am 5’11” and about 165-170lbs with long legs and ordered juniors 13 Long. First pair I ordered 13 long (I have a 35” inseam) that was in May 2022. The seam down the side showed where the inside got pinched with the sewing process but, I was using them for work, so it wasn’t a problem. Second pair I tried on and noticed a worn hole on the butt cheek and some other sewing irregularities. I sent them back and ordered another pair. Third pair came and I tried on and they were a bit tighter and NOT LONGS 😤 they said long but we’re not! I was going to send them back and order another… they are out of my size 13 LONG and now on sale. I’m kinda upset at this since I paid more and they can’t get it right. Or maybe these are all irregulars and others are sending back too? I really tried making this work because I like how these jeans hug around my body not making them feel tight but more like leggings. and now it looks like I’ll be keeping and maybe adding denim to the bottom so I don’t look like pee wee Herman. If the company would like to still correct I’d like them to reach out but, consistency in the jeans is key! I may still return just for the credit because I can’t see spending money on a pair I’m not satisfied with. I hope this helps others.

I am 5’2 inches tall with a 27.5 inch inseam (crotch to floor). I ordered size M because sizing chart referenced inseam at 27in. When I tried on the pants, they dragged the floor (even with heels). Confused, I check listing (again) and measure myself (see pics) to discover the inseam measurement is 29.4 inches. Color is a very nice (classic) deep navy blue, the high rise waist measurement is accurate and very comfortable. Well positioned pleating allows for classic and traditional trouser fit (not too full in buttock/hip). 100% poly fabric is cheap, very lightweight and retains static electricity (clinging to legs and attracting lint and hair). It is disappointing that the mfg and vendor failed to communicate and ensure sizing chart reflected accurate information to the end user consumer. The trousers are low priced reflective of their low value and unworthy of the time and money it would take to hem. I will be returning.

I’m considering ordering another pair before my upcoming trip! These will travel so well, I can already tell. In a nutshell- yes, buy them.

I am 5’3”, 155lbs. This pair is a size 8 (I am always able to wear a size 8 in Levi Signature - as they all run big IMO). The waist measure 15.5 inches flat, the inseam is 27 inches (ankle length, if you are taller than 5’4 these are gonna be short, almost a perfect length for me at 5’3”), 11.5 inch rise, the leg opening is 14 inches. They are a mid-weight cotton blend with plenty of stretch. The rise is long, maybe a little too long, so the crotch hangs a little low if you don’t keep them pulled up. They are a nice slim straight style, that are a pretty classic cut that will likely always be “in-style” They are comfy. I have washed themed and they really didn’t seam to shrink and they have done a decent job of holding their shape throughout the day. Overall a solid pair of not expensive jeans, great for wearing around the house, but definitely don’t do anything to enhance your figure; like you don’t put them on and think “wow, these look great” it is more like “wow, these are a perfectly serviceable pair of jeans”. I mean for $23 you can’t go wrong really.

I am 5'4", 120lbs, and usually a size 4 in pants (though I can sometimes be a 6 since I am curvy). I bought these in a 28 and they are comfortable but snug. The brown color is nice, and I like the button, the hemming, and the high waist, which works well with crop tops. The length is fine, and about as short as I would go in pants. The fabric registers as a very 70's, thin polyester. VPL is a real issue, so exercise caution with your choice of undergarments. You may also want to size up for a looser fit, as these are very fitted and stretchy. I took two stars off because for this price (about $35 at the time of my writing) these are not cheaper than what I might find at a store specializing in casual basics, and those stores would have higher quality materials and craftsmanship. The tag on these says "do not put in the dryer" so they seem like they won't hold up.

I am 5’4”, 140lbs and I got this in a size medium. My legs are about 34 inches long. My curves the way it’s supposed to unlike most jeans and it doesn’t have that weird gaping space in the back. I could wear these with or without a belt. They’re comfortable and stretchy. I’m posing funny in 2 pictures because I wanted to show how much they easily stretch. I got them in four different colors, but I don’t recommend the white pair if you’re a perfectionist. The white pair collects too much fat is and hair to it. It happened to me at work with a scarf. That’s why I gave it 4 stars. If you go by the size chart, then it is true to size and like I said it stretches so it has gift to it. Not trying to be rude but whoever said they run small was in denial about their measurements. They’re as comfortable as my cotton jeggings from Uniqlo. I recommend these jeans especially if you’re curvy like me.

I am 5'4", rather athletic build, and not much of a booty.😭 I hover between 145-150lbs. I bought a medium, which was what was suggested. I wear anything from a size 6 to a 10 in bottoms, although 8 is my overall average. These are VERY high- waist on me, but I have a short torso so that might be why. They aren't as loose fitting at I thought they would be, but they aren't tight. The waistband is very stretchy so don't panick when you first pull them out the bag. I thought they were child's pants at first! I bought the army green color and it is much darker than what is usually considered army green. It's almost like what would be called a "forest green". The pockets are fine although they land lower on my butt than I would like. Note that the little front patch is just that--a patch of material that is sewn on, not an actual pocket. The material is similar to a lightweight chino or khakis, good for fall and spring. With some leggings underneath you could wear them in the winter as well. Overall, they are very comfortable, I just am not sure how to style them. They aren't tight at all, so if you like how they look on me, get your regular size, but double check the size chart measurements.

I am 5’5” and 127 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 or 27 in jeans, medium bikini bottoms, and small in shirts. I was really torn which size to go with and decided on the medium recommended by the size chart. Let’s just go ahead and get the negative out of the way. When I think of “boyfriend jeans” I expect straight and loose legs but fits at the waist. The legs of these jeans are not baggy enough to be considered true boyfriend jeans. This pair in particular is quite a bit more fitted than the model photo. I would classify this style as distressed relaxed leg. Now let’s move on to judging the material. These are soooo stretchy. I wore them for a full day of running around town and they never lost shape or loosened up. They look tight in my photos but in real life the waist line does have some gapping at the spine and is barely touching my abdomen. Even though the size chart recommended a small, I am extremely glad I went with the medium. I think these jeans are stretchy enough that the small would have fit but they would have been super tight skinny jeans. The material of the light blue is slightly scratchy before a run through the washer. I don’t trust them to survive many rounds through the dryer but they did survive a tumble on the low heat/delicate cycle because I was trying to soften them up a bit. I will probably hand dry in the future to keep the fraying in tact. The paint splatters on the jeans are white and very much paint (not bleach) so they could flake off over time. Recommendation: Cheap enough to be worth a shot. The material is thin, stretchy, and ripped so these will make great summer jeans. Don’t expect them to be really loose unless you are willing to size up but keep in mind that the waist may be too big for you if you do size up. This pair of jeans just became my go-to for weekend road trips because they are stretchy enough for the car but also cute. I was comfortable and cool enough wearing them for a full day in the Texas heat.

I am 5’6” inches tall, 123 pounds on an empty stomach, and a consistent US size 4 in pants and jeans. I ordered size 4 in olive from Khaki and Blue and they do fit true to size. The waist is low-rise. The material is stiff and even makes some noise when I walk. No stretch whatsoever so size up if you are between sizes or don’t like things digging into you while seated. After sitting for long stretches, the lack of stretch really starts to dig in around the seated section of my lower torso and upper thighs. There were some steam marks upon arrival on the back of the pants but they came out in the wash. All 4 pockets are deep but the pants are a little too tight for me to even be able to use them for much. I’m afraid the size 6 would be too loose in the waist but probably would’ve been less intrusive for my desk job. However, I do like the style and look of these pants with the flaring leg and hip hugging. In conclusion, the stiff/thick material with no stretch will keep this olive pair out my regular line-up but the design and style will draw me, when I’m feeling peppy and can stand up a good portion of the day. Definitely a high quality pair of pants!

I am 5’7, 155 pounds and I ordered a size Large in the color Black. I go back and forth typically in sizes medium and large in pants. Sometimes I am a size 6 but often I am a size 8. The large fits me in the waist extremely well. These pants are made out of 80% Nylon and 20% spandex so they are very stretchy and soft all over. One of the comfiest black dressier pants I now own! The waist is elastic and although it looks like it buttons up in the front, it is only there for visual purposes. These fit me tighter at the waist and hips and then are a straight leg with the slightest gradual flare going all the way down to give a tiny amount of wideness at the bottom. I wouldn’t consider these wide leg pants though. The only thing I wish I could change about these pants is the length. This is a normal problem for me though. The length is perfect if I wear a flat shoe but anything higher they will be too short. I like to wear a chunky block sandal or something with a heel when I wear black pants like this and unfortunately I will not be able to do that. Other than that, these are extremely comfy and I love them!

I am 5'7" and 160lbs with a 31" waist I normally wear between a size M and L, I got this in a size L and it was slightly oversized.This gave me Free People vibes and looks to be a dupe of one of their other shirts but I really wasn't impressed with how it looked on or the quality. The edges are unfinished and did have a small line of stitching to prevent further unraveling, but the unfinished edges didn't look intentional...they just looked sloppy.The fit to this is weird. The arms were really long and there are no buttons or elastic so you can't roll them up and have them stay up. The material has no give to it, so you can't push them up your arms either. The cut was very oversized but strangely short in length and the slits up the sides were oddly placed. I just thought it was a very unflattering and non-functional piece.The material is a think but strangely stiff denim, a stiffness I might expect out of a thicker denim but not out of a demin this thin. It was uncomfortable to wear and unflattering. I won't be keeping and would struggle to recommend this, especially at this higher price point. This looked and felt cheap to me.

I am 5'7" and 160lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and L. I got this in a size L and they fit great around my waist, butt, and thighs. They were really short though, I know they are supposed to be cropped but they were ridiculously short on me. To add to that the two legs were shaped slightly differently and uneven, it was really odd.These were also really weird feeling. They had a greasy feel to them that left my hands feeling slippery, I ended up trying them on over leggings because I didn't want to get the residue on my legs. I have never had a pair of jeans have this kind of greasiness to them.I was really excited to try out these barrel leg jeans and very disappointed in the quality. The greasy residue that they seemed to bathed in was a huge yuck factor for me but might have washed off in the wash. The fact that they legs weren't the same length was just poor manufacturing. I didn't keep these and can't recommend them.

So excited to have a jean option like this to buy on amazon!

I am 5'7" and these pants reach the ground from my natural waist, if you are shorter you should be prepared to have these hemmed. I am 5'9" 180lbs athletic build and these fit me perfectly. I wear a 10/12 usually in pants, it depends on the cut and type of material. I got these a bit longer then normal at a 36L I would usually have a 34L, this was done so I could wear tall heels with them yet cover the shoes with jeans.Anyway, the denim is soft yet durable, feels like old school wranglers that have been washed a hundred times. The pockets are wide in the back and the liner in the front is deep enough to hold a phone. Also, the stitching pattern on the back pockets is the W of old, and it is done in great neutral colors. The boot cut doesn't flare out a ton so even with standard heels these are very nicely cut. The belt loops are standard size and these have a heavy duty front zipper. I washed / dried like normal and had only the normal snugness of jeans out of a dryer feel no major shrinkage.These jeans are very comfortable. If you like your waistline extra loose buy a size up. These fit me and don't dig into my skin, any middle padding I have doesn't bulged over the sides of my pants (no muffin top) however, if I wanted these loose fitting I would by a 12 not the 11. These are snug on my butt and fit nicely on my thighs. Overall a great pair of jeans

I am 5'9" and a pretty skinny 126 lbs. I bought 2 pairs in the exact same size - Red, Brown, Medium. I returned the red. They did not fit the same neither were they both of consistent quality. The red fit smaller & were narrower/tapered in the legs. The material was poly/cotton & thinner. The brown was a great fit, long enough and thick, comfy material. They are 65% Rayon, 35% Spandex! That means they will never shrink in the dryer. I don't detect any stretching from the Spandex content, which is odd, but for me, not a bad thing. Haven't washed them yet, so we'll see how they hold up otherwise. Maybe these pants were made in different countries, but now I'm looking to get a different color that is exactly the same as the brown pair. If you have bought other colors & you see Rayon/Spandex mix, post it in your review. I'll def order that color. It seems that I can't post 2 different reviews, Amazon keeps giving me the first review to edit for the second pair. So I've combined both reviews here. 2 stars for the Red, 4 stars for the brown .... Guess I'll average to 4 stars so the browns don't reflect badly.

I am a pretty true size 8 with short legs (30” inseam). 5’7” 145lbs. I’m pretty curvy and work out a lot so my legs and rear are a little thick. For me these pants are perfect. I am a short person (4’11) and have a short torso on top of that. Not to mention I am also currently a bit overweight and carry it in my midsection from previous pregnancies. Therefore, when I wear jeans that require a belt I end having to hike them up above my belly button and my belt bulge looks ridiculous under my shirt and makes me feel uncomfortable and more self conscious. I saw this product and thought I would give it a try. It’s really neat because it is stretchy and adjustable so it is able to fit a variety of different sizes with just one belt. That way as I lose weight I can just tighten it up, rather than having to get a completely new one. One downside to the elastic type material is that when you undo your belt it tends to “snap” back and it will come out of at least one of my belt loops. This is a bit annoying when I’m trying to go to the restroom and attempting to keep my belt from coming out of my loops all together. Lastly, the buckle itself can be a bit tricky. It does have a way about it to get it just right. It took me several times until I finally figured out the placement of pressure to get it to release easier. And sometimes it releases when I don’t quite want it to if it’s being held at a certain angle. But, at the end of the day this is still an awesome alternative to your typical belt. It’s also super comfortable to wear. Most belts are painful for me to keep on when sitting or driving, but I haven’t had that issue with this. I definitely recommend.

(No having to pull them up when I sit down so my kangaroo pouch isn't squeezed in half) There is a little bit of "puddling" at the ankle, but I'll usually be wearing boots with these so I don't care. And there's pockets! Good ones that I can actually put my hands all the way in! (The back pockets are not real pockets, but who cares when you have real front ones!) And although I haven't tested them to see if they are 100000% opaque, I'm pretty sure they're going to pass a lot better than some others I've tried. 1000/10 recommend.

I am always dubious about getting clothes online but I am so happy with this purchase! I got these pants for work and they are perfect. I am 5' 2 1/2" and 156lbs and these are great for me! Not too long, but not high waters. It's a good fit without being too tight or too loose. There is room to tuck a shirt in if you need to. I feel like I can move and bend in them just fine without restriction. The full leg picture is me bent half over.

I am always on the hunt for comfortable work pants that are flattering to my figure. I love the color, the cut and style are cute and flattering. Unfortunately, these fell short for me for a few reasons. First of all, size wise these fit as expected and are flattering my to curves. I wear size 8 pants normally and the 8 fit good, except, there is no stretch in the waist band, which I would really expect in a pair of pants with no zipper/button. There is almost no give in the waistband, so it makes it awkward when pulling on over the hips. Second, the material is pretty scratchy. The picture looks like it would be a softer material but it's really more of a stiff polyester. And finally, this is more an issue I have with my shorter stature (5'5") these pants are too long. I would need to get them altered, which, with pants at this price, not really worth it. Again, cute pants, flattering cut...but the comfort just wasn't there for me.

I am amazed by these jeans! I bought size 12 (my normal size) and they fit like a dream, so comfy! I'm 5'8" 165 lbs for reference. I'm getting more pairs in different colors soon, but I think I might try size 10 depending on how these ones wear over time. Even new I do feel like they slide down ever so slightly as I walk around and move in them. If they stretch out even a little bit or I lose weight (which is the goal) they may be a bit more relaxed looking than I want. But I'll just have to wait and see. Anyway, I highly recommend them!!

I am around 120 pounds and 5'6 and usually wear 0-2. I ordered a size small and there are several holes still available and it fits great. However, I am concerned about the longevity of the material. It is not as high quality as I expect from Calvin Klein. This is made of split leather and PU leather. In concept, the PU leather coating would make the split leather a little more durable. However, the belt began to show signs of wear after trying it on. The metal bracket of the belt is coated and I can see the joining seams. It still has a nice appearance. The brown side is very dark and almost black in appearance. I am not confident in the longevity of this belt. I still like the belt but have not had it long and hesitate to recommend it fully.

I am at a point in my life where I want comfort and luxury or at least look and feel more luxurious! I have had 4 abdominal surgeries/Csections (5 kids) and just had a hysterectomy. I have been looking for clothes that make me feel good and look better than typical sweat outfits, etc. with a flattering effect or piece. I am pleasantly surprised by this outfit. It is a little see through for the white one, but I seriously would be surprised if it wasn’t see through (duh, it’s white) . Maybe it’s the white color that makes it look a little fancier? Not sure. But I do like it! The texture is soft (I’m a seam and texture type). I just tried it on and I’ll be sure to wear it for a day indoors! I’m a klutz in white! I’m 158lbs, 5’4” and one week post surgery (filled with air) - I think it’s fits nice now and I only expect it to fit even nicer once the bloat goes down.

But they are much better than Lee version of pull up jeans (which are OK-ish), and much much better than Levis pull up jeans (which are awful).

I am currently a solid Women’s size 8, Juniors size 9 (W29.5, H36, Ht 5’3.5) and wear the Hybrid & Co super stretch twill pants in Medium. After reading the reviews for the Indigo Jeans I purchased one size smaller in a 7 and they fit perfectly (these are offered in Juniors sizing, so that translates to a Womens size 6). I have a 29” inseam and these are long enough, and more than reach the top of my vans. They button at my natural waist and fit nice and tight everywhere without pinching or causing “muffin top” My only two issues are: 1. Although they fit snugly at first, they don’t offer much shaping or hold. They are very comfortable, but stretch out pretty quick in the hips and knees just from driving. I cannot image I would fit into a size 5, so I don’t think this can be remedied by sizing down again. 2. Indigo is advertised here as a much darker color, and most denim companies market “indigo” as their darkest shade before black. This Indigo denim is not dark or sleek unfortunately. It is a medium, work-a-day blue. I like it enough to keep them but have posted pics so that others can decide before buying 🤗🙂

I am curvy. And picky with jeans. I am about 190# and 5’10”. My hips are 45 and waist around the belly button is 36. I bought 12 long which is the size I buy in American Eagle. There is no gap in the back. Tight in upper thigh but they are stretchy I can do squats without thinking they will rip!! These jeans are great!!!! I will buy again and all the colors.

I am extremely impressed with these pants. Many faux leather pants make this annoying swishy noise when rubbed together (aka walking) but these just glide smoothly with no detectable noise. The waist band is stretchy which makes it so comfortable throughout the day - you can eat a large meal in these and not need to unbutton yourself. There is no gap at the waist either, which is difficult to find when you’re more well-endowed. They’re very trendy and I really couldn’t be any happier. For reference, I’m 5’7” about 180lbs and bought a size XL.

I am extremely small pre pregnancy I was 95-98lbs currently I’m 25 weeks and 113lbs I have not gained weight anywhere except my belly so small in maternity is still too large around the hips but these shorts fit amazing for reference I got Xsmall

I am impressed with these jeans! The color is great, and the fit is size accurate according to the sizing chart. They definitely are high waisted. The pants have a nice but slight stretch. The inside of the legs are super soft and the pants definitely keep you nice and toasty. I definitely recommend if you are going to be outside in the cold, say, for a night football game. They may be a little too toasty in a crowded place with heat, unless you just are cold natured. The pants are an average length (they were long on me as I’m 4”11) but will pair nicely with boots. You can’t really roll them up at the ankle as the inside lining shows. I do wish they left a little room without lining so they can be rolled.

I am in between sizes and bought a medium. I’m 5”3 and 140 lbs and sometimes need a small and sometimes need a medium. The medium fits great! Lightweight romper and it could easily be dressed up or dressed down. Elastic on the sleeves and ankles and comes with a removable belt. Highly recommend!

I am in love with these green palazzo style pants! One, the buttons on the side of the pocket are are cute little detail that make it stand apart from the usual dressy/casual pants. The fabric is thicker than some of the other similar style pants I have which is nice because they are a little warmer and they have a heavy (but not hot) quality fabric feel. I love the elastic waistline because these have quickly become my "I'm having two desserts today" pants. Perfect for a long day of eating! I recommend these.

I am kind of in between size, medium, and size large, and I ordered the classic blue. First of all, the color is darker than the picture shows. Both legs are exactly the same shading under the pockets, but the positioning makes my saddlebags look wider than they are. I may have to get some sandpaper or pumice and try to add some more to the front and the back. I guess when you pay less money, you’re not getting an individualized shading, but all of the pairs will be the same. The rips are at the knees and nicely done. It seems like the fringe on the bottom of the right leg is about a half inch longer than the left leg also. They are very comfortable, and I suggest if you are in between sizes, maybe go with the smaller size. I feel like these are going to be a bit baggy after wearing them for a while.

I am middle-aged and all my 9/10 Old Navy jeans are too tight. I have extra weight around hips/tummy that I want to loose (hopefully), and I am not ready to give up. So meanwhile, I thought I would try a size 10 Long in these no-zip Amazon jeans. I am 5'7", and as you can see from the full leg picture, maybe I did not need the "Long". But I just now pulled them on and have not washed them yet. I would rather have a little bit of bagginess by the knees then too short at ankles. I am very satisfied with the feel and fit. It is a decent thickness of fabric --not super thin, but not thick and heavy. These are very comfortable everywhere on my crotch and legs, including the waist. The waist band seems midwaist and sits about 1.5 in below navel. Not highrise or lowrise or hipsters, etc. Some of my belly is inside the jeans, some above, but nothing feels constricted. Snug but not too much. Super comfortble when sitting and bending/squatting and my crack does not show. Hope this review helps you. I would try them out at least, since Amazon Prime gives you time to try them within the return window. I only bought one pair for now, but wow! I am excited about these. Have a blessed day.🙏💖

I am mildly obsessed with this dress and absolutely cannot wait for it to be warm enough to wear! It is just about the cutest style ever, made even better by the lightweight but traditional, no stretch acid wash denim. This material means it holds its shape without clinging. The style is extremely flattering, it’s like it’s made for beer belly days;). The top and sleeves are cut in a way to still show a bit of shape so you aren’t totally shapeless.The material is not soft but I think if I put it in the dryer instead of hand dry, it’ll help that. I am a true medium and so is this dress, so very true to size. Nice length for my height and preference.

I am nearly 5'11, 200 lbs. and am a size 14, which is a Large, but I ordered an XL based off reviews: It just literally arrived and I will be returning them after I send this review. I was disappointed upon seeing and feeling them, but to be certain I briefly tried them on- being extremely careful not to snag any of the overexagerated cuts with my toes. It fit well and had a good stretch too them, however the cuts aren't reinforced properly and on the right leg knee cut it was already dangling- no thank you! In my opinion these are way overpriced! If you are curvy and have thighs that rub- I can predict they will leave additional holes in the inner thigh area with normal use in minimal time- just my assumption based off the material. For the price- buy yourself some trusted Levi's, Lee's, Wranglers, Old Nay, Gap- even Lucky Brand (on clearance😜). If thinner, more slender, then, yeah, these can work if you're wanting extremely ripped up Jean's, and don't mind paying too much for them... your preference is your own.

Around my waist, the belt is at the last notch and around my upper body (think, sinching at the waist dresses, etc), it fits me better. The material is durable, and I think this belt will hold up for a long time.

I am normally a size 4 and ordered the small with hesitation. The size chart recommended the small but I detest overly tight waist bands. I am 5’6” and 128 lbs and I am pleased with the fit. I am not a tall person by any stretch of the imagination -- I'm 5'3" on a good day. I ALWAYS have to order short/petite pants or hem up regular pants by several inches. Having seen the reviews on these saying that they shrink, I went ahead and bought the regular length ones. Goodness, am I happy I erred on the side of caution. They started with a 29.5" inseam (see photo). After two or three wash and dries, they've shrunk 2" up to a 27.5" inseam. They're nearly too short for even me, but they'll work. That aside, they fabric is thick, soft, and high quality. It's perfect for work, lays flat, doesn't wrinkle, and it gives a nice silhouette for curvy hips/bum. They're also pull-ons for extra comfort, and they have front pockets. I really do like them and I'll be getting several more pairs for work, but I feel bad for anyone taller than me.

I am really impressed with these jeans! I am truly shocked at how much I like them! I did cut the bottom as they were slightly longer than I like, but now they are perfect! I already purchased the other two colors! Some thoughts below: - Runs large. I usually wear a size 6 or 28 in jeans and I went with a 4 in these and they are PERFECT! I am 5'7" and 135lbs for reference. - The length is a bit longer than I like so I cut about 1.5" off the bottom. The photo in heels is full length and the other two photos are cut. I might cut a bit more off but have not decided yet. Even with the shorter length, they still look cute with heels or flats! - The material is somewhat soft but still holds its shape. It has a bit of stretch to it, but not enough that its going to stretch out after one wear. - Nice and high waisted which is what I think makes the wider leg work. - These are tight through the thigh but wide through the calf, they're perfect! Overall, I would size down one size, especially if you are between sizes. Expect the length to be around 29" inseam but cutting them is super easy if you want them shorter and they still look cute! Definitely recommend.

I am really surprised at how good these are! They fit me (almost) perfectly and they look super cute! I have them on straight out of the package in the pics so they are holding some wrinkles still, but overall, excellent pants and I will be ordering in more colors! Some thoughts below. - Fit is true to size with some flexibility. I am 5'7" and 145lbs and holding on to some extra holiday weight! I ordered my true size small and they are slightly tight but its only noticeable at the pleats since they are not laying flat!! Otherwise, these fit perfectly! The back of the waist has elastic which makes these flexible in terms of sizing. I would say order your true size. - The material is not the softest but its not uncomfortable either. I am in Michigan and they don't have a ton of warmth so not sure these will see much wear until it gets back into the 40's or 50s around here. - I LOVE the jogger look for a business outfit. It adds some fun to the outfit and they are flattering! They could easily be worn with sneakers for a more casual but chic look too! - Love that they have pockets and belt loops! Overall, these are shockingly so good. I will be buying in more colors. True to size.

I am reviewing this as a mom with a bit of a fluffy body (I am EXTREMELY self-conscious about my waist and belly so no photos of the waistband on), 5'5"ish and usually wear an 8/10 -- so definitely not young and cute like the model. I ordered a Large in these pants and I am very pleasantly surprised.First, they are a cotton-twilly material that doesn't feel particularly soft initially but actually moves pretty well when wearing the pants. They have a boxy feel -- but that is typical of the cargo look (and my lack of an actual waist). I give the waist band 100 stars -- it is a mix of elastic and button/zipper so it has some give, doesn't compress on my belly or slip below a muffin top and it and is really super comfortable. I won't show any photos, but the band comes right above my belly button and securely above my hips and covers the small of my back completely-- so they cover everything and sit in a very comfortable spot. Even when I sit they are very comfortable and stay in place above my belly and hips. There are belt loops too just in case you need them, but waist wise the size is perfect for me, esp with the elastic!Length wise, these seem to run a bit long for me -- however I actually really find the rolled-up style with the sandals cute. For tennis shoes I rolled the hem up once and I think they are okay. The boots photos have no roll. The sandals photos took 3 full wide rolls on the hem. These could easily be hemmed, but I often like my pants on the longer side so I can wear them with different types of shoes and in different season -- so I am good with these. But, yes, I would say they run long.Lots and lots of pockets -- 2 back pockets, 2 hip pockets, and 4 cargo pockets. The cargo pockets are real and not just decoration. The top ones just have a flap, the bottom ones have a button with the flap. So lots of storage if you want it.I actually surprised myself and like these. I am always nervous about ordering online because I never feel anything fits me in a flattering way, and I second guessed my order a million times. But now that I have them on and see how incredibly comfortable they are on even with my body style, I am really glad that I pushed order. These are a keeper and I would definitely recommend.

I am satisfied with my purchase. I am 4'11 & 143lbs and it's so hard to find clothes that look decent on me. I got these in size 6 petite size and they fit perfectly. Just know they do not stretch at all.

I am shocked at how much I like this belt!! There is nothing country about me but I really like this! It has some country vibes but also with a more modern feel. It’s so fun! Perfect for summer concerts with shorts. I followed the size chart and went with the small (28” waist) and the fit is good for high waisted pants. But if I want to wear something lower waisted, it might be a bit too small. So keep that in mind when ordering. Otherwise, it seems very high quality and well made, especially for the price! Definitely recommend!

I am short (5’0) and was afraid these would fit me too big. They can be adjusted at the bottom leg. They’re very comfy windbreaker style pants. They do make quite some noise when I’m walking like the fabric rubbing against itself. But other than that, they’re cute workout/walking pants! They have a reflective stripe on them as well for nighttime visibility.

I am short and thick so I kind of expected these to be tight on me. I was a little disappointed at first because I wanted them to fit me like the model but let’s be real these look soooo good!! They are a little on the shear side but they’re white so that is expected. Wear nude or no undies and you’ll be good!! I am 5’1 and with a little heel these were just the right length for me. The material is very soft and comfy. They have a little stretch to them which is a plus for us fluffier girls. The back of the waste band is elastic which also helps them fit better in my opinion. They did come with a ribbon like belt but I took it off because it was too short for my waste so it looked funny tied. I love these so much!

I am short.. 5foot2...but these pants make my legs look long. I felt absolutely amazing wearing them last night. The material is thick and good quality.. light stretch and absolutely hugs my curvy hips. I am so in love with the pants. I wish I could find pants made of this material... looks great on... buy them...don't hesitate!

I am size (US)4. Normally I will order a small, and once in awhile my voluptuous bottom is a little too big for a small. But with these cargo pants it seems they are quite baggy and loose on me overall. I think there is definitely room if you order a small and you're somewhere between a small and medium. A medium would have been way too big for me. I'm 5'6" and these are almost too long for me, so if you're shorter than I am, you will probably need to roll them up. They are very well made from very durable cotton. I love the huge side pockets. The front pockets are also decent in size. The zipper works smoothly and the closure button is solid. Overall, I'm pleased with them, especially since they're 100% cotton.

I am size 16 (fighting the battle of the mid-life weight gain at 195#) and struggle to find jeans that fit my shape. I guess I am apple or rectangle with a flat wide butt. I like the rise on these and they have some stretch but thankfully feel like nice quality jeans. These aren’t that thin super stretchy cheap fabric, they have substance which is nice. I bought the short length based on reviews (I am 5’4”) and they are still a bit long. Wearable for me but some may want them shorter. Overall I think they will be good. I did the 7 day try on so I will walk around the house and see.
I am small but I decided to go Xs because I like it tight not loose. So fresh and comfortable. I am 5.3 ft tall 120 pounds .

I am so disappointed that these pants are too short! They are thick and see like good quality. They are super comfy but they are way too short for me. I should’ve checked the inseam. I am 5’7”. If these were longer they would look perfect.

I am so disappointed. These pants are adorable and are ACTUALLY a good length for me, which is hard for me to come by in cropped style pants, however they are HUGE! I am 5’7 and 126lbs with long legs and unfortunately I cannot make these work…I ordered the smallest size available (4), knowing a 4 is typically too large for me and hoping for the best. If you’re a skinny Minnie like me and don’t have curves, steer clear. They don’t make them small enough for us. 😭

I am so glad I read the reviews before I purchased! I am 5'7 116lbs and I was going to get a size 2 but saw to size up so I went with a 4 and they fit! Yall have no idea how proud I am walking around being able to say I'm wearing a size 4! I love the quality and fit of them as well. When I initially ordered them I realized after placing the order it says midrise and I was a bit disappointed because I was looking for a high rise and I had overlooked that fact but when I put them on they definitely fit me like high rise so I'm very happy with them!!

i am so grateful to have found these jeans! i’ve struggled with jeans shopping all my life. i am 5’3 and 145lb. I got size 6 with 27in length. they fit perfect! super soft! they fit my short legs and thick thighs. plus they make my booty look great - with no sagging. i have to pull them up above my double belly button piercing when i sit down, so they do the weird “i have a d!ck” thing (see photo) but most jeans do. no complaints there really. they stay form fitting all day - even after 3 hours at the skatepark. i don’t find myself having to pull them up or adjust them at all but still feel stretchy and comfy 🙌🏼 i usually cuff my jeans but i don’t HAVE to with these - they’re the perfect length and tightness. but the ankle still has enough give to cuff them if i’d like. the waist is perfect- not too low but not too high [you can still see my top belly piercing :)] overall, recommend - i actually have these in 3 colors now

I am so happy I bought these jeans! I am 5'8" 144LBS, 3 months preggers and the small fits perfect! They are not too tight and the belly area is very comfortable! Hopefully they hold up over time!

I am so happy with my purchase! Ive been eyeing it for awhile but hesitant bc of all the mixed reviews.. i got the light blue one and Large I am usually a XL in shirts/sweaters so I downsized since it naturally has the oversized look and I am glad I did. I loved it so much I bought the dark grey one right after in large as well! The material is good quality, its relaxed wothout the super “sturdy/boxy” feeling some jean jackets give but still has a relaxed yet firm shape/feeling to it. I was hesitant bc of the length and I was happy bc it does go below my hips, it covers half of my behind. Some reviews were saying it is too short ends right above the waist but that wasnt the case for me and im 5”8. I was pleasantly surprised the inside had two deep/secret pockets as well. I loved it and highly recommend! The one snall thing that its missing that would have made it perfect was a little hook thing they put inside of jackets at the neck/collar unfortunately didnt have that but that is just something small.

I am so happy with these jeans! I'm 5'6" 135 lbs and normally wear a 6 but I got these in an 8 because that's what Amazon recommended and they fit perfectly. They are truly high-waisted to hold in the mom tummy, great amount of stretch. Great length for flats or a low heel.

I am so impressed with the softness and stretch of these shorts!!! I'm in love, but I needed a smaller size. I'm 5'1, 120 lbs, waist 33", hips 38", and I bought a large. Too big. Ordering a medium. Obviously, I'm rolling the waist down until the medium (6-8) comes in.

I am so in love with my new pants! I have always have SpongeBob waist 😅 but these pants makes me look like I have a smaller waist. They are so comfortable. I bought them for my job but I will be using them for everything haha I would definitely buy them in other colors. I bought M size and it was perfect for my 5’1 and 150 lb body.

I am so incredibly happy with this product, I had to take pictures and do a review. I don't do this for every purchase (I couldn't possibly) but when I have an experience this positive, I have to acknowledge it so I give credit where credit is due and also provide other shoppers with information to help them with a decision. I bought these pants because I was searching everywhere for pants to match this bright purple blazer I had bought and it is just impossible. I saw these, and I knew it was a gamble, but they showed up in the mail the next day, and it was exactly the same color and hue as it was pictured on the internet. It is a perfect match! Not only that, but the fit is absolutely perfect. I think these are quite possibly my most comfortable pants in my wardrobe now. They are stretchy enough to not need a zipper or button (the ones you see are fake) but the pockets are REAL! All four pockets are nice and deep and functional. The fabric is firm enough so that it doesn't wind up looking like leggings (they look like fancy jeans.) They are formal enough to wear with professional attire. They are the perfect length for me (5'2") and they do not fit too tightly around the waist (a problem I find with way too many pants and skirts. They fit just like a glove. In fact, I was shocked at how perfectly they fit. They were almost out of my price range, but I am very very glad that I sprang for a few extra bucks and I may purchase a few more pairs in different colors now that I see how great a product this is. For your reference, I am 5'2", 122 lbs, 31" waist.

I am so so happy with this denim skort. It is extremely soft and fits so well. I am 5’4” 130-135 lbs. the medium was perfect. Wish I had a better picture but this is the only full body picture I have so far. I will be ordering more colors.

I’m so over skinny jeans but have struggled finding a style like this that didn’t make me look bigger than I was. These are so perfect. They seem durable yet stretchy and soft.
