I am typically a size 12 but I ordered a size 12 and 14 because of reviews. I'm 5'6" and around 150. I'm also not as young as I used to be. That being said, I found both pair of pants comfortable but would probably have gone with the 14's in case there was any shrinkage. I DO NOT like skin tight jeans. I ended up returning both pair as I didn't care for the faded jean look (Sea Ranch). I will however order the Point Bonita when they are available in my size. I would recommend these jeans for the comfort and leg length option which is very hard to find.

I am typically a size 4 - I’m 5’3 and 129 lbs. I should have ordered the shorter inseam and down a size 2. It all really depends on how you like them to fit. I personally like my jeans a little on the snug side. With this order, my typical size, I had room in the front and back (see picture). I do like the high waist. It fit right about my “love handles”. They are super comfortable too. Since the pants sell at such a low price, I’ll keep these and order a fitting set.

I am typically a size 8/10 and I ordered the L. 160lb, 5’7”. These pants fall about ankle length and I really like the fringe on the bottom as well as the length. The mirror doesn’t do them complete justice. They also fit me very well in my hips and bottom being tighter there and loose down my thighs and legs. I really like the way they fit and they are extremely stretchy and comfortable to wear. I am able to actually pull out some of the material around my waist as you can see. They do stretch out a little as you wear them but nothing abnormal, and they don’t fall down as you wear them. I put these in my washer and dryer and they didn’t shrink or have any type of seams that fell apart after washing. They are a very good quality jean and I would 100% recommend to any middle size woman who has a little bit of a belly and loves leggings and doesn’t want to wear jeans. These jeans are still very comfortable.

I am updating my review! I love the color and style of these. They are also very comfortable. I am 5'11", 175-180lbs and got 15longs in Jenna at first and they were too big, then I tried 13long and finally 11long. I have a 29 inch waist, 22 inches per thigh, and about 41 inch hips. I have a booty and struggle so very much to find pants and wear sizes ranging from 8 to 12 depending on the brand and cut. None ever fit though except Silver pants (which are expensive). I carry all my weight in the thighs and hips plus I lift weights, which doesnt help find pants. These fit perfect in the thighs and I loved the length. The waist to hip/thigh ratio was what I really needed to be dramatic and the 11longs came super close. I was nervous the thighs would be too tight but the stretch in them proved how adaptable they are. I have a tad bit of room left in the waistband but its as close as Ive come to perfectly fitting jeans! I definitely reccomend.
I am usually a 10/12 US in pants. The medium fit well on my hourglass/ pear shape frame. For reference I am 5'4 and the length was perfect.

I am usually a size 2 in almost every style and brand (5’6” 120lb) but I didn’t want these to be at all tight or “skinny” looking so I ordered a 2 and 4 to see which one worked best. They’re both going back. The 4 is too loose, which was probably to be expected for me. The 2 was a bit snug around the rear and hips but the fabric is stretchy enough that it would have worked. However, they rode up in the crotch really badly. So uncomfortable. Also the fabric is very thin. You can see in the photo that you can see the tag right through the fabric. And there’s a decorative little silver thing on the back pocket that I think is hideous. So they’re not going to work for me.

I am usually an xl or between a size 10/12 I ordered the 12, and although it is just a smidge loose on the waist area, i simply did my safety pin trick for the metal clasp and its perfect! I am 5’4 180lbs and this was so flattering around my mommy tummy!

I am usually seldom able to get pants to fit my petite size (5'2" 108lbs), but these denim pants to my pleasant surprise fit me pretty nicely! From the waist to the bottom to the legs, all fit! I ordered size 0 Short following the size chart on the product detail page, which turned out to be exactly true to size! The fabric has a nice texture, it has enough thickness, but not stiff at all, it has a soft touch, which I really like. I can bend very easily. The style looks really fashionable and casual. Wow! I just really really love it! Would recommend to anyone these nicely made pants!

I am very happy with this belt! It ios simplistic but chic and fits perfectly. I ordered the 105cm with a 28" waist and it works great. I have a handful of rivets to make it larger or smaller. It stayed in place all day and looks really nice. I love the brown color, it doesn't have that orange tinge to it. I am very happy with this!

I am very happy with this denim jacket. It is very comfortable and lightweight denim material, not stiff and heavy. I will wear this as a light jacket on cool days this spring. It could be worn as a casual shirt, with a t-shirt underneath or just by itself. I am 5 feet tall and weigh 130 pounds, I ordered a medium, I wanted it to be oversized, and it fits perfect. I could also wear it as a jacket over a sweatshirt, I would have room and be comfortable. I usually wear a small if I want the top to be more fitted and a medium if I want an oversized fit. This denim jacket is made of quality fabric and the details are really nice too. A nice feature are the two front pockets that you can use for your phone, keys, or small items. The pockets are finished with an integrated back. They lay flat and do not bulge. I’m very impressed with this jacket, it washes and dries with no wrinkles and did not fade or shrink. I would give this 10 stars if they were available to give.

I am very petite and muscular (Height: 4ft 8in, Weight: 108lbs, Bust: 32D). I was trying to find a size that would fit my biceps & shoulders. First I ordered a Medium, but was way too big. (6 Total Pockets- 2 chest, 2 side, 2 inside) The inside pockets can fit an iPhone X with case.

I am very pleasantly surprised by the quality of these pants. Very soft, comfortable, do not make noise when I walk. I am 5'3" tall, my inseam is 29", 135 lbs. I am very pleasantly surprised by these jeans. They are extremely comfortable and they look great too! I think jeans can be tricky to get right. They’re either too tight or too baggy or they’re too long or too short but these are “Goldilocks jeans” because they are just right 👏. The material is soft and stretchy so they have some give in them. I ordered a small and they fit perfectly around the hips and waist without feeling tight. The product description said the inseam was 30” and I knew this was the exact length I needed when wearing boots with a heel. I’m 5’4” but I do have short legs so although these work great for me, they may be too short for many people of the same height. If I had any criticism, it would be that the stitching wasn’t great in some areas but I’ve been able to tidy it up using a pair of small nail scissors. Overall, I couldn’t be happier with how these jeans look and feel! And, you certainly can’t beat the price point because jeans can be expensive. I highly recommend 😊.

I am very pleased with the feel of the fabric and the fit. Love the smooth, stretchy fabric. Very comfortable!!! 5’ 2” 128 lb Purchased Size 8. The waist is a slightly big but a fancy belt would fix that. Length a bit long but I like that they don’t ride up past ankle when sitting. Legs comfortably trim and not tight. Excellent value for the price. Wore them straight from package after warm tumble with wet towel. Will amend after washing if needed. Also bought in navy. Thanks for the special gift included in tan pair.

I am very pleased with these jeans. They are modern, comfortable, and the perfect length. It gives it a straight Jean look. I bought xL and it’s true to size.

I am very pleased with these jeans. They’re super soft and stretchy. The elastic inside the waist makes them so comfy without squeezing your mid section. I’m super long-waisted and they come up high enough to avoid muffin top. I think they would sit closer to the belly button (if not over it) on the average person. The length is perfect too. I’m 5’6”- 130lbs, normally a size M or 6/8.

I am very skinny (5'8", 114 lbs, size 0) and struggle to find skinny jeans that are tall and don't give me wedgies or feel like they are cutting off blood flow at the knee. These jeans are very comfortable. They smelled weird coming out of the packaging but the instructions advised I throw them in the wash so I have yet to wash and re-wear them. It's nice to find something casual that doesn't break the bank. I am very happy with this purchase!

I am very thin (5'5"/ 103 lbs) and the XS-S fits great. I will say that there seems to be no difference between back or front so I can't speak to how the fit would be on a fuller back side but they feel like they would stretch a little without pulling down. These rest perfect at my natural waist and don't slip down, there is no weird crotch fit and they aren't sheer for me. I love how they have the extra piece to go over the foot to keep them from riding up. The material is smooth and there is a sheen to them, my sweaters / shirts don't cling. The ankle does have a small band and for me there is a little bunching because I am small, but I always wear shoes or boots that go higher than the ankle. I am always cold and at 67 degrees in the house, I am warm. They fit under my jeans but it's snug, haven't tried with super skinny jeans and don't want to. They are definitely warmer than typical leggings if going out. If you're always cold, they are not miracle leggings - nothing is for me, lol! If I was a normal weight, I would try the next size up. They're nice, comfy, keep me warmer than usual, and I will buy more.

I am: 125 lbs 5'3" 28" waist 38.5" hips I ordered a size 7: Front rise 9" (it fits like a high rise) Hip front 18" (18x2= 39") Length 31.5" Fiber/material content: Cotton 58% Rayon 21% Poly 18% Spandex 3% * Remember it will stretch and seem a bit bigger due to lots of poly and rayon; i like the poly and rayon, but will have to order a 5. * In my experience about 1/5 inch larger than a 7. * Prob won't shrink much due to lower content of cotton * I REALLY like the feel...due to lower content of cotton * If you like a soft feel, look for rayon, ramie, poly content * If you like a thicker feel, look for high cotton content. * In pictures shown here, I am wearing a thick fleece under the jeans. Only practical if I plan to wear thick fleece all the time. Even so the waist on the back side was loose. * Really like the pocket detail stitching.

I appreciate reviews when people give their body stats and and the size of the garment they ordered. It makes it so much easier to figure out what size you should order. So I am 5' 1 1/2" tall and weight is 156-lbs. and I ordered a large which fits perfect. I am very short waisted and these sit just slightly below the belly button on me. So I don't know that I would consider these high rise. The fabric has a tiny bit of stretch/give to it but its definitely what I would call a cotton twill. The color is a nice Army Green, lots of nice deep pockets and very comfortable. The washing instructions say to hang dry, but I will probably put them in the dryer on the lowest setting just to fluff. Also my inseam is about 30" and the length is perfect, if you have longer legs these would be too short. I hope this helps.

I bought a size Small, Short in Black. 𝗙𝗮𝗯𝗿𝗶𝗰: I didn’t particularly like the feel of the fabric, but the fabric is quite thick and suuper stretchy. Owing to their thickness, they might not be the best choice for warm work environments; but, probably offer some decent coverage for your everyday Normal Human lines, lumps, & bumps, as well as not being see-through. I say “probably” because I only tried them on briefly and (unfortunately) knew right away I’d be returning them. 𝗪𝗮𝗶𝘀𝘁: These size small pants have a waistline that rests without stretching on an ~25” waist. I’m sure they could comfortably accommodate waistlines up to at least 27”. side note: the area below the waistband is cut wider, so they fit just a bit awkwardly in that area. 𝗟𝗲𝗻𝗴𝘁𝗵: I ordered a “short” size, as petite size inseams are usually for heights down to ~5’4”, and short inseams usually accommodate ~5’3” and shorter (I’m about 5’1”). The size charts say the inseam should be 27”. These pants are labeled ‘29” inseam’ (printed into the inner waistband). Even 27” wouldn’t give me the cropped look in the product photos, but 29” is unwearable without alterations. 𝗖𝘂𝘁: I’m pretty lean with plenty of muscle. My legs measure roughly 13 1/4” around the calf and 18” around the upper leg, at about halfway between groin and knee. Unlike the model in the product photos, my legs were swimming in these pants. I measured the width of this product’s legs. The 1/2 width is about 7 5/16” across. For comparison, I also measured the leg width of a pair of my skinny jeans at right about the same spot. The 1/2 width of the skinny jeans is approx. 5 7/8” across. So, if you’re rocking some gorgeously thick legs, these might be your perfect skinny pants. If you’re sporting slimmer stems, definitely look elsewhere for that ‘skinny’ fit. 𝗜𝗺𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝗮𝗻𝘁 𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗲: The back pockets are fake; the front pockets are real and quite generous compared to many pockets marketed to women. 𝘁𝗹;𝗱𝗿— I’m giving the pants ⭐️⭐️⭐️✰✰ + super fast shipping + waistline measurement accuracy + fabric thickness + front pockets I can put my whole hand into - lower waist cut/tailored a bit awkwardly - length inaccuracy compared to size charts - leg width/cut description & images vs reality - fabric texture - fake back pockets

I will definitely be buying a few more.

I bought these because I am 5 months pregnant and cheap. I don't ever want to spend a ton of money on pants I'll only wear for a couple of months, also, just because I'm pregnant why should I have to sacrifice my style? I bought these in a medium (I am 5'3 and normally an xs/s) and they are perfect. They are really long (great for taller people), extremely soft, and not see through at all even when completely stretched out. They are also high waisted, which I love. I will definitely be buying a few more.

I bought these because I thought they would look more like a dark jean. They are much brighter than pictured and it’s obvious they are leggings in my opinion, but they are so stretchy and comfortable and true to size I’d definitely buy them again! I might grab a different color next! For reference I’m 33 weeks along, 5’5” and about 170 lbs.

I bought these because I would be traveling in a car for several days and wanted to be comfortable. I was very comfortable. I wore them for 4 straight days. I know that's a long time, but no one pointed to me and said " lady do you ever change your pants?". Very comfy walking on the Appalachian trail ( gotta brag as I'm 62yrs. young). As you see in photo the thread is a little loose. I'll just put a drop off clear nail polish or frey check on it for now instead of returning them. I did wash them when I got home inside out and air dried. They look fine. Size was good. I'm five' 5" feet and 130 pounds. I wore them with sandles as I was traveling from FL. to CO. Savannah, GE was wonderful!! If you go stay on the river in the Marriott with the balcony! Any way, these jeggings were great for travel. My daughter said they are good for old people like me. I took her out of my Will. Try these, you won't be disappointed. Update! These jeggings hold up to falls!! I was running on loose rocks when my daughter's dog ( who is no longer in my Will ) cut me off and I fell. I was wearing my jeggings at the time. I landed on one knee and wrist very hard while landing on my side. My knee did not tear the jegging material yet my knee was cut and bruised. I fell hard, broke three toes, and injured my shoulder, but the jeggings are fine! There is just a little place where my knee hit the rocks that made the fabric a little lighter blue but not noticeable to not wear it again. I'm wearing an orthopedic boot for my broken toes for a few months, but I think the jeggings will look cute with the boot when I go to Maine to hike a little bit on the Appalachian Trail. I have good insurance. So again, I would recommend these, although I wouldn't recommend running on rocks with a large dog thinking it's playtime. And I question why would a husband ask "Are you O.K." when finding you bleeding and laying down on rocks?

I bought these because reviews said they were lightweight and I was looking for comfy lounge around pants for summer. I was not disappointed! Love the pockets, love the weight of the fabric, love that they’re soft. They were a bit stiff when I first got them but after one wash they softened up quite a bit. I ordered up a size because of reviews that say they shrank a bit in the wash. I recommend you do the same because they do, indeed, shrink in the wash. I’m 5’4” and about 130. I ordered a large and after washing, they’re perfect. You can’t beat the price so I highly recommend that you get a pair while you can. I originally ordered light grey and came back today to order some additional colors only to notice that many of them are no longer available in sizes I would buy :( I’m hoping they’ll restock because I could absolutely live in these. VERY IMPORTANT UPDATE!!! I received the black and white buffalo checked ones a few days ago and they are a completely different fabric. The grey ones that I fell in love with are 60% polyester/35% cotton/5% spandex. The buffalo check ones are 95% polyester/5% spandex. They are neither light nor are they breathable. I ordered a large again because I thought they’d be the same as the light grey (silly me for thinking that ordering the same pants in the same size but in a different color would result in getting pants made of a completely different fabric.) They obviously did not shrink when washed and I’m swimming in them! If you order that color, you may want to size down BY A LOT.

I bought these belts because my pants were always falling down and I’d have to yank them up. I had a belt with a buckle that really hurt my stomach. I found these and the difference is amazing! I wear a size 22/24. I’m also 5’11 and weight about 280. I got the pack with 1 black and 1 brown belt. They are so comfortable without a buckle. It does come with a hoop if you do want a buckle. I give these belts 5 stars, two thumbs up and 💯 recommend!

I bought these belts because my pants were always falling down and I’d have to yank them up. I had a belt with a buckle that really hurt my stomach. I found these and the difference is amazing! I wear a size 22/24. I’m also 5’11 and weight about 280. I got the pack with 1 black and 1 brown belt. They are so comfortable without a buckle. It does come with a hoop if you do want a buckle. I bought these first in white in a 6, and LOVED THEM, then they became baggy as I wore them (in a cute way though). So I bought the grey and red in a 4 and they fit perfect. I normally go between a 4-6 in normal pants so I'd recommend sizing down because of the stretch. These are my new favorite pants. I have worn them the last 4 times I have gone out. Perfect for any occasion, and great with a bodysuit. I have a small waist and wider hips and the 4's fit perfectly, no belt needed.

I bought these for a 70’s costume. I am 5’4, apple shaped body. I usually wear a size 16 and I bought the XXL. I definitely could have sized down one. These particular pair run a little big and have some stretch. They have a large flare at the bottom. They are really long. In this picture I am tall wearing platform shoes and they are still pretty long. I will be keeping them and I’ll wash and dry to try to shrink them a little. Overall good quality.

I bought these for a 70s party and ended up keeping them for casual wear! I have recently gained some weight and my nutritionist suggested I get comfortable with the weight I am, and buy some clothes that feel good. These fit that bill. And as a bonus, my husband thinks they're pretty cute too. I am less than 5ft tall, so just cut 3 inches off the bottom so I could wear them with flats. I might need a few more pairs.
I bought these for a 70s party, but I will be wearing them for way more than just that! Super cute. I’m 5’7” and the size 10 fit me perfectly, including length wise.

I bought these for a 70s party. Fit was great. I ordered a small, the length was perfect, I wore them with short (2-3in) heel clogs. I have a small waist and thicker thighs and bottom. 5’6, 34/36 bust, 28 waist, 36 hips, 150lbs

I bought these for a casual wedding; they are lightweight and very comfortable. They are a bit more flattering than some other palazzo-style pants because of the top layer, which flows nicely. They are advertised as capri pants, but they are full-length on my 5'4" frame. The pants are not a bright white, but they are pretty close. The blouse that I'm wearing in the photo is bright white; the color difference isn't that noticable.

I bought these for a concert and I have to say I'm very impressed with the quality! First thing is they are long. I am 5' 6" and they covered my feet I wore platform shoes and it was perfect. Second thing they are not itchy! They have a nice inner lining but my legs do rub together, thicc thighs and all, and the sequins caught a few times but that was after I had them on for a few hours. Third they are a bit big at the top but my high waist is smaller than my hips I would size down next time and get a slimmer to wear under them. I will definitely be buying more pairs in the future they are fantastic!

I bought these for a family photoshoot and I’m obsessed! Love love love!!! They were stretchy enough to not feel constricted but still kept my figure! Not bulky and frumpy! 10/10 recommend! I am 155lbs 5’6” and got the size medium! ☺️

I bought these for a themed birthday party. They are light weight and very comfortable! I didn’t get hot and the waistband stretches to give you extra room if needed! I am 5 foot 6 inches tall and 150lbs for reference. I bought a size large. Since I do not really wear this style of pants for my day to day activities, they will become lounging pants for the house as they are really that comfortable to wear. I recommend!!

I bought these for a trip abroad. They are very stretchy and I likely could have sized down. However, they were cute as is, and went with all my tops. The big pockets in front were a large plus, and they were flattering and cute. I tried to buy some from a bigger brand and they were stiff, uncomfortable, and too small despite getting my “size” and advertising stretch. These were much better. I’m taller (5’10”) and sizing was accurate if not a bit big.
I bought these for an upcoming cruise. They're lightweight and comfortable. Long waisted. They are a bit long for me (5'4"), but I will tie the ends when I'm not wearing heels.

I bought these for an upcoming trip to Nashville and they were absolutely perfect. I am also 6 months pregnant and love that they have the perfect amount of stretch around the belly area. I'll be able to wear these post pregnancy as well! I am 5'8 and ordered a Large. They were the perfect length with my platform converse. Not sure how these would be for shorter people, might need to get them hemmed, but totally worth it. Love Love Love

I bought these for family photos and I was very pleased! They look a little more cranberry in photos than burgundy but they still worked perfect. I’m 5’7 and 140 pounds. I bought a size medium and they fit fine! There was a bit of room in my waist but I don’t feel like a small would have been flattering at all. With the belt, you can also tighten it a bit more. They are quite long. Being 5’7 and wearing flats, a bit touched the floor so shorter ladies may want to wear a heel of some sort. I will probably buy again in a different color. Worth the money FOR SURE.

I bought these for family pictures and they are super stretchy, super comfy but they are a little thin and I feel like you can see any imperfections in my legs through these, granted they are white so I'm sure on a colored pair you may not see those imperfections as much. All in all I really like them and I can't complain for the price point. For reference I am 5'7 around 200lbs and I ordered a large

I bought these for my 60’s themed birthday party. I bought 2 pairs in 2 different sizes & brands. These I liked best cuz of the red embroidery detail. They were comfy & no problem with length. I’m 5’7 & 130 pounds for reference & a size 8. I ordered these in the 8 & they were fine. The only downside was I really needed a belt. I kept having to pull up on them all night. They seem a little long in the crotch & I’m long waisted which is weird. Anyway, I like them so well I will be wearing them after the party! I washed them in cold & dried them on low. We’ll see how they fit now.

I bought these for my daughter who is 17 weeks pregnant. She was outgrowing her regular jeans and needed something comfortable. They come down to her ankles which she likes. And they fit snuggly uncomfortably. She currently weighs around 122 pounds. And she is 5 foot five.

I bought these for my daughter who is 17 weeks pregnant. She was outgrowing her regular jeans and needed something comfortable. They come down to her ankles which she likes. And they fit snuggly uncomfortably. She currently weighs around 122 pounds. And she is 5 foot five.

I bought these for my daughter who is 6 months pregnant. She loves them and they look fantastic on her. She is 6 feet tall and the.length is perfect for her. She bought a medium. They were a perfect fit.

I bought these for my daughter. She loved them. She is 5’9” so I was afraid of them being too short. I figured I could add material if needed, but fortunately they fit perfectly. She wears a size1-3. I bought her a size 2. They are stretchy and soft. I would recommend 100%.

I bought these for my granddaughters Lainey Wilson bell bottom themed party!!💕. I’m short so I thought they’d be way too long! I bought a size 14. I’m 5’ and weigh 160 with a big booty! I could have gone with a 12 as they were stretchy! So fun to wear!!
I bought these for my mom in size 18, and she loves them. She is 5'5, waist 38inches, and 209lbs. They are comfortable, and the length is perfect.
I bought these for my trip to Japan. it's soft and comfortable, I stayed warm outdoor all day visiting temples and fish market. I'm 5'6" 130 lb and got size 6. It fits perfect.

I bought these for work, but they may be better suited for more casual wear. The material is comfortable. 95% polyester, 5% elastane. I had to spend quite a bit of time ironing them on low to get the deep creases out from shipping (and that was after trying washing and drying on delicate and wrinkle-removing spray). They're still pretty wrinkled in some places. For reference, I have a 33" inseam, so these pants may not work for shorter people.

I bought these for work, but they may be better suited for more casual wear. The material is comfortable. 95% polyester, 5% elastane. I bought these for work, where I am required to wear khaki pants/shorts. The fact that they have functional front and back pockets was a HUGE selling point for me. I am 5'3 and between 125-130lbs. My waist was 30" at my time of purchase so I could not decide between small or medium because according to the size chart, I am at the upper spectrum of small (25 - 32) and at the lower spectrum for medium (28 - 38) and I really do fluctuate between those sizes depending on the brand. I ultimately went with small and they fit nicely with absolutely no squeezing of my body. I am so comfortable at work and because of the texture of the fabric, they look like form fitting pants rather than leggings. As you can see in the photo, the pants are too long for me, so I will stick to the capri style. I've worn them once a week for the past month and although I have followed the directions for washing and drying, the fabric is starting to look like its going to pill so I am not sure how much longer they will look nice. If I order more to wear outside of work, maybe I'll try hanging them to dry and see if that helps.

I bought these for work. I am in love with them. Very flattering. I bought a size small. I am 127 and 5 foot 2. I might need them hemmed. I am short and deal with that normally. I want some for every day of the week :)

I bought these for work. I love them for work! I needed 100% cotton jeans and these did the trick. They’re not super flattering but boy are they comfortable! So great for lounging, running errands, they’d have FANTASTIC for gardening, and they are great contract/construction work. I wore them when I visited power plants for work and I will wear them again for gardening and other outdoor adventures when it’s hot out and I need long pants. I love these pants!! I wish they were a little more stylish so I could wear them more often... I mean... I could... but I don’t have that lien or courage... yet. BUT, I will wear these every opportunity I get and maybe I can find a way to make them stylish. I think it’s my body. Some women could definitely make these stylish. I’m a little shorter and more petite. I think for these to be stylish you probably need longer legs. I have to to roll these at the bottom and I look like a little girl. I also got the smallest size they offered and they get a little roomy... they may be made to be that way though. Anyway, I seriously do love these. Comfiest and most breathable jeans that I have ever worn. I want MORE 100% cotton jeans. Please!! They truly are amazing. GET THEM. They may look great on you, just not as much on me. If the way don’t look “cute,” keep them for fun and rugged activities/gardening. P.S. I got a size 4. This was the lowest offered when I bought them. They stay up because of where they sit on my hips though. I’m something like a 27in waist, 34-35inch hips?? Waist size doesn’t matter as much for these... or they didn’t for me... I’m 5’3”

I bought these in a size 6. Size 6 women’s at stores like JCPenny normally are my ideal size. The waist on these is absolutely Too Snug and tight , it is very uncomfortable. Normally I’m either a size S or M depending on the brand. These pants are 14 inches across the band when I measure them with tape (per see the picture attached), which seems like it could fit especially with these being advertised as stretch, I don’t think the waist is stretchy at all for this type of pants, I would probably have to size up to an 8 but I’m not sure I will be as these I am going to return due to the band being non stretchy imo. For reference right now I’m weighing around 130 lbs , my height is 5’1.5 inches my waist measures approximately 28-29 inches and my hips are 40.5 inches, athletic build.The material is smooth and pretty thick and feel comfortable on the skin as the fabric is 53% cotton 39% nylon 8% spandex , so material feels professional and soft. Front pockets are real and back pockets are fake which I think looks good because it doesn’t look bulky on the backside but smooth and sleek on the bum. Overall these pants could be great , and the inseam options are very good for different heights, but with 8% spandex I would expect way more stretch and these don’t have that. I hope this helps someone as I have a hard time buying clothing. On my experience stretch pants usually can accommodate 8-10 lbs loss or gain weight and these just aren’t forgiving and won’t stretch to your size with weight fluctuations.

I bought these in an attempt to not buy another pair of super stretchy skinny jeans. Was hoping to get a different look. Unfortunately they were not for me. I am 5'10" typically wear a size 14. I bought an XL based on the reviews but these were not flattering at all. The length was about two inches too short. Not very surprising I guess based on my height. But the tightness on my hips and waist were. Typically high waisted jeans fit me just fine but these were not made with pear shapes in mind. Returning and won't size up based on overall fit.

I bought these in black, bungalow khaki and indigo rinse, all size 6 short. They are incredibly comfortable and flattering but the lengths are all different. I bought the black pair first and loved them as they were perfect length for me (I am 4’11”). Since I loved the black pair I bought the khaki and indigo colors and they came way longer. Usually I have to wear heels with “short” pants but I didn’t have to with the black pair. The khaki were like half an inch longer so not terrible but the indigo were much longer. I returned the indigo and khaki and kept the black. I love them and wish the lengths were consistent because I’d buy every color if they were.

I bought these in blue and love them. Very stretchy and comfy. I’m 5’7 and 155lbs and purchased a medium. There is still a lot of room if my body decides to grow, but they did not stretch out after a few wears without washing. I am disappointed that the second pair I ordered in the Pecan had white lines on the top. I think to make them look worn but it doesn’t look natural. In the ad photo the lines are not noticeable. I will be returning. Would love them if they didn’t have the lines. Very pretty color.

I bought these in denim and liked them so much, I also bought them in white. I'm 5'3" and about 150 pounds, so I got a medium. They fit fine in length, but I like to cuff them (as shown in the photo). The fabric is substantial and not sheer, so they look like skinny jeans. These jeggings are a wardrobe essential and can be dressed up or down.

I bought these in light grey medium for myself. I am 5’ 3”, 155lb, carrying weight in my mid section. First these are weirdly long. They are all bunched up around my ankle and baggy from the knee down. I usually wear a 28” inseam. They fit fine up top but they have a drawstring that I was unaware of when I bought them. Second they are thin. They have that fleecy feel on the inside but I wouldn’t call them “lined”. They are one thing layer. Overall the light grey were sub par. I won’t wear them out of the house. Extra note they have a small mesh key pocket inside the other pocket. I bought a black size xs for my daughter who is 5’ 2”, approximately 100lb soaking wet. She’s a long, beautiful bean pole. Long legs. The black were a completely different material from the light grey. Much thicker and definitely fleece lined. They just feel more substantial. They are still long which works for her long legs. No drawstring in the black pair. Not sure why they were so different.

I bought these in silver first. Turquoise is my favorite color. Ended up buying them in both colors. The silver has a white background. The blue have a blue background. Wore them to a friend's 60th Birthday, was comfortable all night. I don't notice if any sequins have fallen off. Got tons of compliments on them. I wore them with silver booties they are a little long but I just pulled. them up. I am 5'6' and wear a size 4 pant. I ordered the small. I'm tempted to get the red pair as well ;)

I bought these in size S/M but I think I could've gone with a Large very easily. I'm normally a size 6/8 and small in women's clothes, medium in anything"trendy" (like forever 21, H&M, etc.). I'm 5'8”, measurements 34-24-38, and most pants are a little short on me, these were no exception. I was expecting to probably have to exchange these for the larger size, especially right after I took them out of the package. They look like clothes for a ten year old, but these leggings stretch an absurd amount. I would not suggest a S/M for someone who has any extra "love-lumps" going on because I feel like these will role down in that situation. They're high waisted enough to feel really comfortable and they're not the warmest leggings but it's not like I can feel a breeze when I'm wearing these (like some other leggings I have, looking at you Target). Would buy again. My biggest suggestion is that, if you are trim and slim, buy the S/M. If you are anywhere else on the spectrum, buy the Large.

I bought these in the black, green and gray and I love all 3. They fit perfectly. I’m between 5’3” and 5’4” and they’re the perfect length.

I bought these in two different sizes, so I am going to have similar reviews. The fabric on these is very nice, with a soft, smooth finish. They have a good amount of stretch -- better than the PU leather type pants. There was no strange smell to the fabric. The XL and XXL both fit my girlfriend well. She is 5'10" and an average sized American woman. The sizing chart on the product page appears to be accurate. These are very shiny and do a good job of giving the leather look, while still being comfortable and breathable. On the XL, there were some loose threads. I was able to cut them without damaging the integrity of the pants. On the XXL, all of the seams and stitching were perfect. My only other issue, other than the loose threads is that the product description appears to have changed since I ordered these. The product page now refers to these as "jeggings". This are not jeggings, they are leather look leggings. You can see this clearly in the pictures, so I don't know why the page changed. I would recommend these.

I bought these in white first, size M and I’m 5’9, the fit was amazing and just the perfect length both with and without heels; it went all the way to the floor. I decided to get another pair in blue because I loved them so much, but these were a disappointment. I think it was worn and returned because out of the bag, it smelled like laundry detergent and the tag shows wear and tear (I’ve attached a picture). The fit is much tighter and it’s much much shorter, I thought maybe I accidentally purchased a Small but that wasn’t the case. The material also feels different — more rough and itchy than the white one. I think the person washed them and they shrunk and altered. I’m returning.

I bought these Jeans size XXL based on the sizing recommendations, my daughter normally wears size 11 or Large she is curvy so that's why we got XXL when they arrived since it was a xmas present we had to wait until after xmas. Once she opened them tried them on they were too loose from the waits as well as the hips/legs. Glad to know we could return them, as soon as I got the email that about the refund I order another one but this time we ordered size Large it arrived fast and she tried it on and it fits just perfect. she is 5'3" 155 lbs she has sort of hourglass body shape with just a bit more on the hips. (hope that helps you when deciding on buying this Jeans). The picture is just to reference body shape so it helps you.

I bought these jeans based on my waist. They fit perfectly. I'm 5'2, 165, and I bought an XL. I may need to use a belt at work. The hands down best part about these jeans are all the pockets space, especially the front pockets. They do bunch up around my ankles a bit, but not enough to cover my platforms, but my converse look very cute.

I bought these jeans because I like my jeans a light-blue color. I took this pictures fresh out of the package. These jeans just look worn out, not at all like the pictures advertised. I do love this brand however because it's the only brand that fits me at 5ft. tall.

I bought these jeans for Chris Brown concert and I’m so glad I did. The stretch was PERFECT and for sure worth the price. The many pockets came in handy since I did not want to carry a purse. I normally wear a size 11-13 depending on where I’m shopping and I bought a size large in these. I’m 5’7 190.

I bought these jeans for my kids. They like trendy clothes, they wear jeans with holes in them, and I thought these are a shoe in. The jeans arrived yesterday, and surprisingly my girls weren’t too interested. I think because it’s summer and they’re thinking shorts and mini skirts rather than jeans. Big win for me! I cannot believe how amazing I look in these jeans. Now, I’m not the type of person to compliment myself pretty much ever. I’ll be the first person to point out my wrinkles, jowls, saggy bat wing arms, and lumpy cellulite allll over. But these jeans make me look like a movie star. I don’t know what kind of magic was sewn into the fabric, but they’re comfy with just the right amount of stretch, they stay on all day… So they’re stretchy, but they snap back where they need to, they aren’t low rise, but they don’t go up to my chest. I’m telling you, these are perfect. I think the only people who might not love these jeans as much as I do are taller people. I say that only because I have a 30 inch inseam… a lot of my friends have a 32 or 33 inch inseam and these jeans would be a little bit too short for their liking. Some women prefer the ankle length look, and this would definitely work for that. These jeans are absolutely perfect on me and I’m going to be looking at some of the other colors as soon as I’m done writing this review.

I bought these jeans for my kids. They like trendy clothes, they wear jeans with holes in them, and I thought these are a shoe in. The jeans arrived yesterday, and surprisingly my girls weren’t too interested. I think because it’s summer and they’re thinking shorts and mini skirts rather than jeans. Big win for me! I cannot believe how amazing I look in these jeans. Now, I’m not the type of person to compliment myself pretty much ever. I’ll be the first person to point out my wrinkles, jowls, saggy bat wing arms, and lumpy cellulite allll over. But these jeans make me look like a movie star. I don’t know what kind of magic was sewn into the fabric, but they’re comfy with just the right amount of stretch, they stay on all day… So they’re stretchy, but they snap back where they need to, they aren’t low rise, but they don’t go up to my chest. I say that only because I have a 30 inch inseam… a lot of my friends have a 32 or 33 inch inseam and these jeans would be a little bit too short for their liking. Some women prefer the ankle length look, and this would definitely work for that. These jeans are absolutely perfect on me and I’m going to be looking at some of the other colors as soon as I’m done writing this review.

I bought these jeans for myself and I love them!! I am 5'3 and weigh 102 pounds, my waist size is 24 inches and hips is 35 inches, so I bought a size 1. They fit perfectly and bring out my curves. They are very high waisted and sit right on my belly button. I HIGHLY recommend these jeans if you want a perfect high waisted Jean. They are very stretchy and long so it is also good for taller people. It depends on how much you weigh really for the sizes, a lot of people around my height say they got the size 3 but they weighed more so their Jean's fit them, but if you are around my height and weight a one will do you wonders.

i bought these jeans hoping they wouldn’t be too long, i’m short 5’3” and if paired with a 2inch heel boot (give or take), they are amazing!! super soft and stretchy on the legs, i’m 175lbs and got a size 12 and chef kiss 😘. and not to mention the deep pockets in the front and they aren’t too thick of a material, very flattering

I bought these jeans in a size small, and the length is perfect. I'm skinny, and I don't have thickness in my legs or my rear. So these were loose. I'm 5'6" and 110lbs I'm normally a size small for everything. I recommend them, the jeans are little high waisted for someone that doesn't have a rear, really black, just keep in mind they may be a little loose when you buy them.

I bought these jeggings 2 months postpartum in hopes of wearing something other than leggings and pajama pants. Im 5’9” and 165lbs. I purchased a 10 long. Pros: the length was perfect. I like the fit, they we’re not too tight to creat a muffin top yet tight enough to feel supported. The material definitely feels more like jeans vs leggins. Great price! Cons: i wish the waistband went higher than my belly button. I think this would solve a lot of my issues. Because it hits right at my belly button i still have some extra belly that needs supporting and creates a small muffin top when i bend over. Also, minor issue but the top butt area doesn't provide support for me. My butt kind of bulges out and gives an unflattering extra curve. In summary i would definitely recommend these jeans for any mom post partum or Similar problem in the belly area. I definitely think the price is right for what you get. I am a happy customer because i didn’t over spend on jeans that may be a temporary solution to what i need. I plan on losing more of this postpartum weight and improve my diastasis recti so down the road these jeans will look better on me *crossed fingers*

I bought these July 29, 2023 and loved them so far, but (as of September 16, 2023 - less than 50 days) there's a finger sized hole that just keeps getting bigger over the left back pocket! I've only washed them 2-3 times tops, so I am very disappointed. They always get washed cold and delicate and basically only get worn to and from work. I bought a size 12 in color "One Tone Dx". I am 5'6" and roughly 175lbs, typically wearing a large. These pants are comfortable and stretchy and for now I can live with the hole because my shirt typically covers it, but you expect a lot more from a brand like this.

I bought these last year and never got around to wearing them until the other day. They were great at first but after 1 wash they shrank about 4 inches. I washed them very gently and they still shrank up horribly. I bought a large knowing they'd probably shrink a bit based on reviews. Im only 5 foot so it wasnt a big deal. They were dragging the floor at first so i just hiked them up a bit on my waist and after one VERY GENTLE WASH they're too short to wear. I bought them a while ago so the return window is gone so I basically paid $40 to wear a pair of oversized pants once. My advice is to find another pair made with different material because these ain't it.... unless you like to throw away money- then go for it.

I bought these leggings for a trip to Iceland this fall to wear under snow pants. The leggings are very nice, warm and soft. My stats: 53 y/o fit, 5’3.5”, inseam 28.5, waist 28”, hips 38.25”. The smalls fit perfectly. The black pair are water resistant as long as it is not a lot of water. But they fit great and are very warm! I highly recommend these leggings.

I bought these leggings for a trip to Iceland this fall to wear under snow pants. The leggings are very nice, warm and soft. My stats: 53 y/o fit, 5’3.5”, inseam 28.5, waist 28”, hips 38.25”. The smalls fit perfectly. The black pair are water resistant as long as it is not a lot of water. But they fit great and are very warm! I highly recommend these leggings.

I bought these leggings to wear with my concert tees for a rock concert I was going to. They are so comfortable and soft. They are a high rise fit so they would be perfect with a crop top. They aren't too thick or thin. These are now my go to concert leggings! I am 5'5 and 118.5 lbs and a small was the perfect fit. They held up well after washing. I always line dry my leggings so they keep their elasticity. I would buy these again!

I bought these Levi's hoping they were half as good as the reviews said and they Really, Really are! Long story short, I went off my diet and I'm feeling blahhh! I had to go to a school function last night and "be seen"... so I pulled these out of the drawer, took them out of the bag and hoped for the best. I was alittle worried because they have a "pull on" waistband, no zipper or buttons and I thought they might look alittle "old". But as long as you wear a shirt, tunic, sweater that comes down long enough, your good. The inside tummy panel really works great. I went with the 12 Long (5'10 185lbs was 180lbs) and the fit & length are perfect. They're were alittle tight going on and it was a questionable fight for a minute, but I won. I'm wearing the Harmony style.. These hide any bit of muffin top you might have. If your a bigger gal like myself, I highly recommend trying these. They are flattering, the style looks good on and the price is right.The Measurements For 12L:17" Across Waistband,10.5" Front Rise,32" Inseam. They are exactly what I needed. I paired these with a layered tunic and ballet flats and I felt good and was comfortable all night. Definitely Buying More.

I bought these looking for a more affordable alternative to the Aritzia ones that I have and love and these are pretty similar it’s just that the crotch area is a little long. I’m 5’2 - 145 and I got a small but could’ve gotten an XS if it was available. This shade is a little transparent but if I wear white or nude undies I’ll be fine. Def will be buying in more colors though!

I bought these on a whim because the price was right. I love the gray color - it's dark enough to serve as a black without attracting all of the hair but actual black jeans would. For reference, I'm 145 lb 5'5, athletic build, and the size 10 fits the way I like. If I wanted a slightly more standard fit, I would go with my regular size 8, but the 10 gives me room to do anything I might want to do around the house or out. I actually ordered another pair in a more traditional jeans color.

i bought these on impulse, hugely discounted, during the cyber monday deals. i’d never heard of the brand but read the great reviews and thought i’d give them a try. from the reviews, i ordered a size smaller than i would usually go (i’m 5’4” about 115lbs and ordered the 26x33). when they came and i tried them on i was worried they were too tight and there was no way to exchange them, only returns. it turns out, after wearing them for just a few minutes, they became the most comfortable jeans i own. the length is a bit long for me but works great over cowboy boots or with heels.

I bought these overalls for gardening so I can stop mooning the neighbors. I'm 5'7" 190lbs and size XL is comfortable and roomy to move around. My phone fits in the pockets and the material feels like a real jeans. Cute and stylish Soft Sky Blue demin overalls. I highly recommend these! They're very flexible!

I bought these overalls for gardening so I can stop mooning the neighbors. I'm 5'7" 190lbs and size XL is comfortable and roomy to move around. My phone fits in the pockets and the material feels like a real jeans. Cute and stylish Soft Sky Blue demin overalls. I highly recommend these! They're very flexible!

I bought these pants because of the comments of them being tall girl friendly. I'm 5'7, 145 lbs but have long legs and a short torso. I am used to most pants being ankle length or shorter on me but these were great, I could even wear heels. The color is vibrant, I received so many compliments throughout the day. I plan to buy a few more pairs and hope they fit the same

I bought these pants for a Caribbean vacation and they were really nice. Comfortable to wear and the tie waist helps to make sure it fits well. As you can tell, they are not cropped on me. I am 5'5 and they come right to the top of my feet, which was fine with me. I would definitely buy again. I may actually buy them in a different color.

I bought these pants for a Caribbean vacation and they were really nice. Comfortable to wear and the tie waist helps to make sure it fits well. As you can tell, they are not cropped on me. I am 5'5 and they come right to the top of my feet, which was fine with me. I would definitely buy again. I may actually buy them in a different color.

I bought these pants for hiking, herping, and just being outdoors. They will be perfect for that. I'm 5'1" and 120-125 lbs. I normally wear a size 4, but after reading the reviews, I decided to buy a size 2 and they are still a little big around the waist. Nothing too bad, i have to wear a belt (which is also too big for me 😒). I'm hoping after a run through the laundry, it'll shrink just a little because I hate when my pants constantly fall. The length was ever so slightly too long which i prefer anyways and the amount of bagginess in the legs is perfect, I have no issues moving around which will be perfect. Update: Did not shrink after laundry, still big, but i still love them.

I bought these pants for my wife. She says they are very comfortable. They are just stretchy enough to fit comfortably, but not so stretchy that they get loose during the day. They are sized correctly, and are a good length. She is 5’5”, and 245 pounds, and the 18 fits her well. They are high waisted, and the material is sturdy denim, not something thin and too airy. There are belt loops, decent pockets, A button and zip closure at the front, and pockets in the back also. Very flattering also.

I bought these pants for professional headshots. They fit great. Reference, I’m 5’5, 140lb, athletic build with curves. Super cute, flattering, and comfortable! I have a 4 star because for the seam to line up straight on the backside, the front hook is off centered. Just going to move the hook over.

I bought these pants for travel. I love traveling in beiges, ecru, etc and this color was a perfect addition to my travel wardrobe. I wore it 3X just for boarding three (3) different flights on one vacation and to be honest it didn’t even get dirty at all, despite the color. The quality is fair, after ironing it, with starch it looks even more chic. I’m 5’ 6” .. waist 21” hips 45” so I purchased a MEDIUM and it was PERFECT .. I’ve gone ahead and also purchased the khaki to add to my travel wardrobe. Overall I say .. BUY IT …

I bought these pants for work and I was not disappointed! I followed the reference and got a Small/29” Inseam Petite based off of my height 5’3/5’4 and they ended up fitting perfectly. They are very comfortable and stretchy. Perfect for work attire, but also comfy enough to be worn at home.

I bought these pants in another color, 31" inseam, and LOVE them. So I bought a second pair in Heather Grey and they arrived 2 inches shorter than they were supposed to be. Contacted Amazon who sent a replacement, another 29" pair came marked as 31". So I reached out to G4Free customer service who apologized and said a 2 inch discrepancy shouldn't have happened and gave instructions on how to measure an inseam just in case. I've written multiple polite responses saying that I followed their directions and the pants are 29" but marked 31" and they have ghosted me. So, if you get the size you order, they're amazing. But their product inventory apparently is inconsistent and customer service will leave you hanging. I won't be ordering more unfortunately.

I bought these pants in order to create an outfit that I saw online. The entire outfit as pictured online was around $200 and I was able to get the jacket and the pants for around $50. I am over the moon with this look. I’m 5’7” and I have a long inseam of about 33 inches and I’m wearing a small wedge sneaker underneath, so they are long wide, legged, palazzo pants! Perfect if you’re on the taller side with long legs!

I bought these pants to take with me on a long vacation. They were so amazingly comfortable that I was about to order a second pair. However, when I took a closer look after wearing the pants for about the 10th time, I had noticed pilling in the area near the crotch on the pant legs, and upon closer examination the fabric was thinning. By the end of the day a hole had appeared in the area. Due to this I will obviously not be getting a second pair, which a shame because I really did love them. Maybe if you don't have thigh that rub together when you walk they will be fine, but if you have thick thighs like I do, be warned.5'6", 165 lbs, Size Large - Fit was great (loose and comfortable)

I bought these pants to wear as a costume for our royalty group to wear on a float decorated in the Mamma Mia theme. According to the sizing chart, I was buying up in size than I would normally wear (typically, I could buy a size 1x - I am 5'10", 220lb). This product, I purchased a 3x and it was still tight in the thighs and hips area. It is stretchy material, and will stretch out a bit for me to wear for the event. They are very cute, well made, and come in so many color options! I do love how they feel otherwise.

I bought these pants to wear in a production of Pippin, in which I had to do various types of dance, cartwheels, and climbing (it was crazy); these pants were terrific! The material is very comfortable and I had no problem wearing them for multiple rehearsals and performances. Personally I was worried all the wear would lead to rips in the pants or they’d become less opaque but that wasn’t the case! I’m about 5’4” and when I wasn’t wearing heels the pants did end up on the floor a little, but it wasn’t bad at all! I highly recommend these pants for any occasion.

I bought these shorts after having a baby, and they are incredibly comfortable! The fabric has a good amount of stretch, and the high-waisted design is both flattering and supportive. They do run a little bigger, but not by much. For reference, I'm 5'5" and 150 pounds, and the size 8 fits me well. These shorts have quickly become a staple in my wardrobe. Highly recommend for anyone looking for comfy, stylish shorts post-baby!

I bought these slacks for work. I needed a pair that were not skinny. They are comfortable and professional looking, but needs ironing after washing. I’m 5’7”, 130 lbs and size 8 fits fine in length and hip area. I do need a belt due to the waist being a little too largeUpdate: Be careful ironing! When I placed the barely heated iron on the pants, it burned the material. Now the pants are useless!

I bought these to use as part of a Rodeo Queen Halloween costume. I’ve never owned wranglers so had no idea how they’d fit but after reading several reviews decided on the 7-34. I’m 5’8” 140lbs and they fit perfectly. And though I normally wear a 32 inseam, the 34 length was perfect for my high heeled boots. Super happy with them!

I bought these to wear for work. I was touring a new cruise ship and needed something stylish but easy to move around in. These hit the spot. I do agree with others that the waistline is tapered but, for me, they worked ok. I have an hourglass figure so my body tapers at the waist naturally. If you have an apple shape, you will need to size up. I’m 5’4”, 160 pounds and the medium fit well. If I weighed a smidge over 160, they wouldn’t work. The fabric is soft and wasn’t see through with my skin tone underwear. The pockets sit high on the front and while functional, are a little awkwardly placed for truly holding items. There are pockets on the back that work great for normal use. This will be a staple in my wardrobe. I really enjoyed wearing them.

I bought these to wear to an Elton John concert. The fit was great, except they were a bit too long for me as I am only 5’4”. That happened a lot with pants for me and NOT a manufacturer’s issue. They looked like the photo when they came, and I was happy about that. Perfect for the occasion!

I bought these to wear to the Justin Timberlake concert. For reference, I’m 5’4”, 143 and I purchased the medium. They fit perfectly as I wanted them to. I wore a platform shoe and the length was perfect. The denim is on the thinner side but that made them very lightweight. I’m not crazy about the button fly but I loved this candy pink color so I made it work! Overall so happy with the fit and color!

I bought these to wear to work (because real jeans are not to comfortable to sit in all day). These stretch jeggings are super comfortable and the material is very soft and stretchy. I didn't feel they were that flattering on me because I purchased a Large, one size down from my usual... I think they are true to size and being 5'8" & 180 lbs, I should have just went with my usual XL size. I'm still happy with my purchase because I bought them for comfort. Good jeggings for the price.

I bought these trousers to wear to work and wear with a black top that I had just purchased. The trousers fit right at my ankle. I am 4 feet 11.5 inches tall and 144 pounds I usually wear a size 8. I’m a full size 8 presently so I got the size 10 and they fit like an 8 they are a little snug in the midsection but they work. They sit above my belly button. I don’t consider them a skinny pants they are more like a straight leg pants like someone in a previous review had mentioned. I like the style and fit enough to purchase another color at some point.

I bought these when I was 26 weeks pregnant. This was definitely a must-have. My regular ones even with the elastic band is just not comfortable whenever I sit. The downside to this is there's not enough pockets (all the pockets are on the right side)and it does not come in petite. So I need to hem the bottom. But this is very comfortable.I'm 5'2, and now 153lbs. I fit a medium with room to move. I had it for a two weeks and already notice there's a wear in thigh area. So I bought another one to alternate.

I bought this belt not only for the look, but also because grommets can add extra support to the belt. The grommets don't have a back connection so they're not really grommets in the traditional sense. They pop out easily with the slightest pressure because there is nothing supporting them on the back side. They should not be relied on for actually holding pants up. The photos (and probably the positive reviews) show people who are basically just wearing this for show. If that's what you're looking for, they are certainly cheap, so one start for that. The other is that despite no grommets on the sizes I needed, they held up my pants for a year, so one star for that too. But you can see how much damage happened over one year. I'm relatively slim man (6' and between 32' and 34' waist) and I don't wear heavy jeans or work in a physically demanding job. Buyer beware!

I bought this for a 70s themed dinner and it was perfect. Comfortable, flattering, and the small was a great fit for my 5'3" 130 lb self. I had concerns over the quality after reading reviews but I liked it so much it went from my backup option to my first choice.
I bought this for my daughter for our family pictures. She’s 5’6” 130 and athletic build and it fit her perfectly!

I bought this for my wife, she is 5'10 and 205lb, usually wears a US 12 or L but we order a 1xl and it fits her well, she wants this pants to wear while pregnant, stretches enough for her belly, now she's pregnant her hip is 46 "and her belly 45. It can still stretch a couple of inches

I bought this for the Western Wear portion of a pageant and OMG this dress was not only flattering, but so comfy! The buttons were nicely attached, and the sleeves were the right length as well as the skirt. If this item is in your cart, this is your sign to purchase it! I promise you won’t regret it! (For context, I ordered an XS and I’m 5’1, 115 lbs.
I bought this jacket to embellish it. I absolutely love it. This $50 jacket now looks like a $350 jacket I love it I am 5”10 and the jacket hits right around my knees

I bought this jean jacket for a concert I went to and it was perfect! I was scared of what size to buy but glad I got the size that I did. It fits just right. Not too small and not too tight! I am 5’8 203 lbs with a bust size of 43 and I bought a XL.
I bought this on sale and thought pink would be a fun summer color. I am 5’1 and even in the small it was long on me, so I ended up cutting them to give them a more cropped look.

I bought this outfit for graduation and a few other outfits and I absolutely love this one!! It is so comfortable and flattering!! I am 5’3 and weigh 195 and an xl was perfect!! I recommend this outfit to everyone!!

I bought this size Medium in 2 colors - I am 5’6” and 150 pounds and the fit is perfect. I bought them in hopes to “dress them up” as I’ve seen other reviewers mention, but I definitely won’t be able to wear them in my office setting workplace. The side seams (see pic) look like leggings/athletic wear which unfortunately won’t work for me at the office, but I will definitely get use out of these! They are a high rise (almost too high for me) with a loose fit thru the legs. The flare is gradual and flattering, and they hit right at the top of my foot (almost too short with shoes). They don’t do anything for my fluffy middle section, but I usually wear a longer top to cover that. It washes well - I did not dry it but the tag says dry on low. I got the taupe and cameo colors, and they are both nice. They are a comfortable addition to my athleisure wardrobe which seems to be growing lately!

I bought this skirt to wear to a concert and I love that the back are shorts so I can dance and sing as much as I want and don’t have to worry about keeping my skirt pulled down. I ordered a medium and it fits perfect. I wear a size 6 shorts. I am 5’3” and 131 lbs.

I bought this to decorate with patches and hotfix rhinestones, which requires 100% cotton to work correctly. I kept the jacket even though I knew it was going to be more work than I had anticipated because the fit was exactly what I was looking for. The fabric has a nice weight to it without being overly heavy. To note, I added shoulder pads for a little more structure. For reference, I am 5' 4", 140lbs, measurements are 36-30-38.

I bought this to wear to the Zach Bryan concert. It is just what I was looking for! TTS (I am 5’10”, 160 lbs, ordered a medium). It is comfortable. Can be worn with a tank or tshirt or long sleeved shirt underneath. The denim is not stiff. Length is perfect.

I bought this when it was on a lightening deal. I like it, and I’ll probably buy more colors if they go on sale again. The brown color is perfect for fall, though and will be very versatile with lots of outfits. It’s kind of a spandex material but it’s soft and has a little warmth to it. I love the neckline! I purchased a small. It is definitely tight through the midsection area and I may have liked a medium better but I like small enough to keep it instead of deal with a return. I’m postpartum and my size has been changing a lot but I typically wear a small-medium so I’d say it’s true to size. 5’4”, 130lbs, 27” waist.

I bought three of these all in size 18: dark, medium, and light washes. The dark and medium fit perfectly. But the light wash is both tighter and shorter than the other two. Luckily they are easy to return. Otherwise, the medium and dark washes fit perfectly and are a great length! I'm 5'4". The material feels durable but I'll see how they hold up after a few washes.

I bought three of these all in size 18: dark, medium, and light washes. The dark and medium fit perfectly. But the light wash is both tighter and shorter than the other two. Luckily they are easy to return. Otherwise, the medium and dark washes fit perfectly and are a great length! I'm 5'4". The material feels durable but I'll see how they hold up after a few washes.

I bought two different pairs- an xs and a s. The XS was more "fitting" but then the waist was more snug than I would have liked. The S was a looser trouser look. Also, the coloration from the images isn't exact especially for the brown, it's much more "desaturated." They are stretchy and soft. Almost like fancy sweatpants. I have yet to wash, but due to their composition, I assume the will have the tendency to shrink. The pockets are deep, the quality seems alright. It's an easy to pull fabric, so gentle cycles. No issues with the zipper and button. Opaque, decent length, lightweight. I am 5'6, 128lbs and they sit 2-3 inches above my ankle - I'm pretty used to that. I'll update my review after a few wears.

I bought two of these, different shades. I like the dark blue better than the denim blue. Had planned to return the denim blue jacket. However, missed the return window. I love this jacket because it stops right below my butt, which is exactly what I was looking for. I am 5' 7-3/4 " tall. Also, love the two inside pockets. Carry your cell phone? You could carry a sandwich in there. The only problem for me is the jacket is too small in the front. The panels don't meet across my breast so that I can button it. I was sort of expecting that. All XXLs are not created equal. My size fluctuates based on item and designer. I wear 1X (not so much these days), 2XL, 20 and 20/22. But summer is for dieting!

I bought two pair; pink and navy and they fit exactly as I had hoped. I/m about 5'2" 150lb, bought XL. Then I bought another pair in black, in the same size, and the cut was off (see images). I'm short waisted so it made the fit not as comfortable as the first two. I also didn't like that the black had a gray drawstring rather than black. But I really like these pants, they are soft and very comfortable for lounging around. I have even worn the navy and black out for daily walks.

I bought two pairs of these a size 8short and a 10short. I am 5’3” and a half and the length of the short size is perfect. I’m not telling you my weight, sorry. I normally buy size 10 jeans at Old navy and in Levi’s. While both sizes fit me (8 and 10), the size 10 is much better for work. The waist is a bit tight on both sizes, but this loosens during the day. The pockets are functional: the two pockets in the front and the pockets in the back. This is a big deal because way too many women’s pants assume we don’t need pockets which is so annoying. I bought the size 8 in dark gray and the 10 in tan. I’m pleased because while I can wear jeans to work, it is always safer to wear actual pants. These are not too stuffy, cheap enough to buy multiple pairs and I can just wash them with everything else. The material isn’t too thin. These are pretty nice. I wouldn’t Hesitate to buy more when I stain these.

I bought two pairs of these jeans for my daughters. They both fit great and were comfortable. I love that they're 99% natural fiber. I was also so glad to find 30" inseam here on Amazon because I couldn't find that short of a length at any of our local western/farm stores. All in all, great jeans and so super cute.

I bought two pairs to try on. My body: 5’2”, 130lbs, 29” inseam, Typically Size 2-4/XS-S. Pair 1: Army Green, Size S, Regular Length, Thick Fabric. Pros: Standard “dress pant” thickness, great length for me to wear with either flats or small heels/wedges. Beautiful green color, nice earth tone. Cons: Tighter in the center, felt like they hugged my body between the inseam and belly button tighter than the Khaki pair. Only two front belt loops, no back belt loops. Button front closure, not a “hidden button” like the Khaki pair. Would prefer to wear a belt with this pair to disguise that. Pair 2: Khaki, Size S, Shorter Length, Thin Fabric. Pros: Loved the thin, moveable fabric. Pockets and elastic waist sat flatter than than on the green pair. Hidden button closure looks better than the exposed button. Stitching looks better quality. Cons: Too short for me to comfortably wear with the wedges. Khaki color is not as flattering on me as the green. Overall: If the Army Green color was in stock in the thin fabric in my size, I’d order that instead. I much prefer the hidden button closure of the thin fabric and the structure seems to be better quality. They also look like a higher end product in person. However, due to the length and color not being right for me, I am only keeping the Green color and wearing those for my event instead of the khaki. I would recommend these pants overall and they are a great item to have in the closet! The biggest downside is that there are only two front belt loops. For the thin fabric with the hidden button closure it is less of a problem, but the exposed button really could use a belt to pull the outfit together. Overall I didn’t notice a size difference between the two, but the thin fabric felt like it was more flattering. I’ll probably wear shaping shorts under these just for my own comfort. Definitely need to wash (instructions say cold, gentle cycle or soak for a short time, hang dry) and steam out the wrinkles from the packaging before wearing!

I can't say enough good things about these jeans! They look and feel like real jeans, not like jeggings. They have a tummy control panel that comes down about 3 inches from the waistband. It completely flattens my fupa and lower stomach pooch!! And I can still comfortably eat in them. I will say, the more booty you have the more difficult it is to pull them up bc of the stomach compression part. I have 42 inch hips and thick, relatively muscular thighs. I got a size 8 in these which is average for me. I'm an 11 in juniors sizes, if that's useful. It takes some effort to get them over my butt, but once I get the compression fabric up over my thighs and butt I only have to do the *jumps while pulling pants up* once to get them the rest of the way up. 😂 The waistband is smooth and invisible under a thin t-shirt, whereas most jeans cause weird lines where the button and zipper are. They're stretchy enough that there's no gap at my lower back. They do have to be pulled up pretty regularly for me when moving around. I think I might also get a pair in size 10 to wear on more active days.

I should have gone with a size XXL, as I am using the 6th and 7th belt notches. I would order one size up when ordering this belt, but not two sizes like I did. For reference, I'm 5' 5" and 197 pounds and wear a size 16 in bottoms.

I completely love these vintage jeans! I try to keep my expectations at bay when ordering jeans online because finding the right fit can be challenging. These far exceeded my expectations! I ordered my usual size and they are a little bit big but based on the fabric content, I expect that they may shrink a little bit over time so I think I still made the right choice. I will say that these are wider in the leg than expected based on the pictures but I think I actually prefer them this way. The waist is high, yet comfortable with a good amount of stretch when sitting. It did roll on me a little bit after sitting for a long period of time but I suspect that is because they are a little loose in the waist. I'm 5'4" and these are just a tiny bit too long but perfect with a bootie that has a smaller heel. A taller person might find these to be ankle-length. The zipper is sturdy, the pockets are nice and the stitching is of good quality. Lastly, these are soooo comfortable. I'm a happy customer!

I look forward to wearing them many more times this summer. If you are 5'9" or taller, you'll be hard-pressed to get them to be simultaneously high-waist and long enough! It's very close.

Now that I see that pic, maybe a little baggie in the butt but I don't want skin tight jeans. But now look at the front. What are those bulges on the side? Really out there on the right side of the pic. Not as bad as the person who called them jodhpurs but I'm not having it. My husband said I was being too picky. He thought they looked okay. I guess if you want to buy these, it is your call. I am 5'2" and weigh between 124-128 and the length was perfect. If not for those "wings" I would have kept them.

I decided to try out baggy high waisted cropped jeans for a change. The leopard pattern is great, subtle, still cool. I like the fabric a lot, it has no stretch to it, but it is soft enough, really sturdy, thin, structured. The pants are a bit too cropped for me, but I am tall with long legs (5’9”), so that is expected, and with high heeled pointed boots would look elegant. The problem for me is the top. To look dressy and elegant, these pants should be high-waisted, and they hit at the top of the hips giving an overall boxy shape. For reference, I wear size M (6-8, waist 27” or 28-29” when the pants are mid-rise).

I do not like trying on pants or jeans I avoid it at all costs they are very tricky to fit on my curvy very short 61 year old body. However these were a winner ! I purchased the F'AB BODY SCULPT SKINNY style. These jeans have lots of stretch, the waistband in particular is so comfortable and it is high enough to cover muffin tops. They are intended to be ankle length but I have to hem up about 2" as I am 5'2". The sculpting darts and elastic panels really do work ! I am size 8 but originally purchased size 10 based on some reviews and I was nervous that an 8 would be too tight. The 10 fit but after wearing a few days the waistband loosened up and I was constantly hiking them up. I switched that out for a size 8 . I recommend trying your usual size in the SCULPT SKINNY style.

I don't normally leave reviews, but I absolutely had to for these pants. I am a server at a very busy restaurant, in Florida where it gets hot as heck. I was out of work for 2 months thanks to covid shutting down all our restaurants, and well I gained some weight while sitting at home doing nothing 🤣🤣🤣. I was called to return to work on Memorial Day weekend and of course, my black work pants would not fit over my newly found, voluptuous body. I spent 2 days running from store to store, fitting rooms were closed and I was getting desperate. I bought and returned no less than 10 pairs to various stores. I found 2 pairs that fit decently, but I absolutely hated because they didn't fit right. I have a small waist, big hips, big thighs, etc., so I need more of a stretchy material. I gave in and hit Amazon figuring if I had to buy and return multiple pairs, I could at least do it in the comfort of my own home. Well, this was the 1st pair i ordered. I can absolutely say, i LOVE LOVE these! They fit perfect, stretch in all the right places, they are light enough so they aren't hot, so soft, unlike starchy, rough dress pants. If you have a job where you are required to wear black dress pants, I highly recommend these. For reference, sizing is spot on. I am 5'7", 165 lbs and typically wear a size 11 juniors/8 or 10 womens. I bought the Medium 31" inseam and they fit perfectly! I ordered 2 more pairs right away!

I don’t think anyone is loving the elastic band on these pants, but that is my only complaint. I like my jeans tight around my butt/thighs, which is hard because I don’t much have a butt or thighs. But I love this fit and they feel like wearing comfy sweatpants. I’m 5’7”, 115lbs, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, 18” Thighs and got an XS. There’s a bit of a loose area in front, but it doesn’t look weird and makes space for more curvy figures. The fleece does add a scotch of bulk, but I need all the bulk I can get on my skinny legs. The cut is tighter in the lower legs than the seat and thighs. Probably not great if you’ve got larger or athletic calves, but the stretch is fantastic. I put these in the dryer on low to tighten up just a little, no problems. The fleece isn’t terribly thick like another pair I have, but is still warm and super soft. The blue is a nice wash on the less faded side than some. I very much recommend these fleece jeans to pretty much anybody.
