I expected these to fit like sweatpants. In the front they kind of do but in the back, they have a very slim fit. I thought this made them hang really nice, mire like dress slacks, but it might be challenging for someone who is bigger through the hips and buttocks region. Despite being a knit fabric there’s not a whole lot of stretch to the material. The material is very soft and comfortable. The pockets are amazing. There are six packets including two cargo on the sides small pockets on the back and deep pockets for your hands upfront. I washed these and they didn’t have any color fade or pilling. Seem to be wearing well. For the right figure, I think these are an excellent find. They were long enough for my 5 foot seven frame, and it would be ideal for someone who is slimmer through the hips and butt, frustrating if you’re bigger in those areas. A little poochy in the front like a sweat pant but overall very nice. I think they will be a comfortable option for travel- look nicer than sweatpants, have pockets for storage, and will be comfortable.
I feel beautiful in these pants. 5’1”, 125 lbs, 31 years old and I bought the 2 Short.

I feel like a frumpy old lady wearing these pants. They run large. My hip measurement is 40" and I got a size 8 (sizing down one because I read they run big) and they are still kinda loose, even after washing and drying. They are well tapered to the waist for me though, and my natural waist measurement is 25". As I walk, the pants bunch up, pulling away from my ankles. I'm 5'-5" and if the pants didn't bunch up, they would otherwise be the perfect length for wearing with flats. I think they are too short to wear with heels. The pants are extra loose at the knee. I would describe the fit as similar to relaxed fit jeans. The fabric is very stretchy, but also very thick yet lightweight at the same time, and prone to wrinkles. The fabric feels cheap. The pants are reasonably comfortable though.

I feel like a good review is so much better than pictures. With that being said I’m about 5’4, 195 lbs and I ordered a size large. I carry most of my weight in my mother’s belly. THESE PANTS are everything to me! I love the flared legs because that’s how I used to always wear my jeans. They do touch ground just a bit but also, that’s how my jeans always were and I don’t mind. The biggest most amazing part for me is the FIT! The top of the pants come up enough to hold in my flabby baby belly WITHOUT digging into my love handles! They fit like a glove in my thigh area and flare out enough through the legs for flowy comfort. The pockets are big enough to hold a range of phones and also stretch a little bit still lay flat with nothing in them. Oh and did I mention my butt?? I don’t think it’s ever looked so good. Seriously these pants are SO flattering. And another great feature for me is not only the stretchy material, but the wipe away effect when it comes to Siberian husky dog hair! It literally just wipes off. I’m so in love I can’t wait to buy another pair and I’m about to find out if they come in other colors! 5 star review, these just came in today and I put them on and have been working around the house. No idea how they withstand in the wash yet but I’m completely sold on these!

I feel like some kind of crazy mad scientist in this shacket. I expected it to be a little bit big seeing as I'm typically an XS and this is a small (I'm 5'3, 100 lbs.). But it's also super lightweight and wrinkles so easily. The white one doesn't really look like the listing photos- I'm not sure how they edited them, but they made it look super defined and darker around the seams. In reality, it's a bright white and I had a really hard time styling it to make it look good and no matter what I did, I couldn't get the collar to lay down. This just isn't for me, but it does look cute in the other colors!
I feel like this is probably a very universally flattering dress. I got a large and I’m a size 10-12 US. 33 inch waist and 43 inch hips

I feel that this runs a little large in comparison to the size chart. I just ordered my usual size instead, M, and overall the fit is ok and matches up to a typical M, I am just a bit too short & chubby for these to fit right. I am 5'3", 38-33-38 and the hem is about 3 inches too long, rise is too long for my taste (definitely a high waist on me when I would have preferred mid-rise). This is a very fine-boned corduroy fabric (see pic) and it does not stretch a lot apart from the elastic waist, so it's more like real pants than stretchy leggings. You may possibly want to order up and think of it more like baggy comfy pants? Or it may fit better on someone with a trim body. These won't fit well if you have large thighs (mine are 20" and I feel that they're about the max) or bootie, I think they will drag down on you. Overall these will probably work the best on someone with a 30-32" inseam & a slim-rectangle kind of body type. Looking at other reviewers that seems to match up with who was more happy with these pants. Really nice color selection (I was so tempted by both the greens but am surprisingly lacking in plain black pants) & they look good but they're a bit ill-fitting for me on comfort.

I fell in love with these funky checkerboard jeans the minute I tried them on. I’m 5’9, 160 lb, 29–30” waist, and I normally wear a size 10. Looking at the size chart, I decided to order size 12, and it was a perfect fit. I need a 32” inseam minimum for flat shoes, and bless these pants, because they have a 32.5” inseam. They have a ragged hem but are stitched so they are durable (see picture). If you want a shorter hem, it would be easy to cut them; if you do, I’d either stitch the edges or use iron-on hemming tape. They have a little stretch and are comfortable and hold their shape all day. I got the black and white, so I wasn’t worried about washing them in the machine. They have laundered well on a cold, gentle cycle and hang to dry.

I felt compelled to write a review because there were none in my size range. I usually wear a 6R at 5'7" 140ish lbs, which is what I ordered. Something I noticed before ordering was that the measurements on the size chart for sizes 6 and 8 were the same. What? So how do I know what size to get? I usually get a 6, but knowing this brand is usually for younger people it felt like a risk. As you can see from the photos, it's a really snug 6. I did try these on at the end of the day when (for me) pants are tightest because of gravity 😂 I think that after some wear and washes, they will get softer. The material is a bit rougher than I usually like, with less give, but you get what you pay for. They will be good for cool weather. I also like the wash, I don't like huge lines on my hips and these aren't too in-your-face. (I would have opted for a darker wash but it wasn't available in my size.) So overall pretty good, I'll throw them in the rotation and pair them with a thrift store flannel to keep the dream of the 90s alive 😎

I figured these would be excellent pants to wear for work when I have to dress business casual. They are extremely comfortable and very well-made. The corduroy is excellent. My only issue is that I am only 4 foot 11 and I have a rather large pouch of fat in my lower stomach area (fupa) And I noticed that these pants seem to sort of accentuate that quite a bit I’m not sure if I wear some extra tight shape wear. It will help even it out and look a bit better. On another note, I am also short in the torso area so my pants tend to come up above my belly button even if they are not meant to be ultra high-rise so I do believe that has a lot to do with the issues on how these look on the other than that these are very great pants and I love the cinched part at the bottom of the pants so I don’t have to worry about them being too long like most others
I finally found a jacket I love. It hits right at the hip and the arms aren’t tight. So happy with this purchase. I am 5’8 and 160 ordered a Large

I finally found a pair of jeans that fit me. It has been hard to find jeans to fit my thighs,hips, and bottom. These jeans are very comfortable, and I love that they have the warm fleece liner inside to keep you toasty warm. These jeans have a stretchy waist and they have the zipper and button that actually works to make these even better. Pockets are good size and fits your hand nicely. These look great and feel great all around. My new number one pair of pants now.

I finally found some black levis that fit. These are the blackest black levis I have ever seen. They are soft and stretchy and they fit great (My butt is lifted up). As I mentioned in another review (Levis 311 skinny plus, kabekona camo), I currently wear an 18 in Womens Levis 505 which run larger than most levis so I sized up to a size 22 to be safe. I probably could have gotten away with a size 20 with these but they are so comfortable (I don't want to take them off). However, I did not have to hold my breath to button them or wiggle into them, they just slid on, so I'm happy. I will also have to wear a belt with these which is always the case for me. For reference: I am 5"5 with thick hips, butt, and thighs and currently weigh around 260 and my stomach is not flat but it is smaller in proportion to the rest of my body.

I finally had an opportunity to wear this very cute, wide-legged jumper. Although the picture is not very flattering, it really is a cute & stylish piece. I wore it to my nephews graduation & got many compliments about my "on point" outfit. The wide-legged design sets it apart from other standard jumpers. I wear a size medium & it was true to size. I'm just about 5'7" & this fit well. I had to shorten the straps a bit, so if you are taller, this would work well. If you have a more petite frame, this may not work for you because the adjustable straps may not adjust to be short enough. This is a great option for anyone who loves to rock jumpers!

I first bought a pair in size 12 according to the measurements given. They were pretty big & long. But I loved how the style was & they have a great stretch! So, I ended up purchasing a size 8 & they fit perfect! I usually wear size 10 I am 5’3 & have 30” waist

I first bought a pair of these pants in the color "blue ice." Size small. (First picture) They fit so nice! I was like, "Yeah mama!" My butt looked awesome, unlike most maternity pants that kind of give you that saggy mom butt. I liked them so much that I bought another color, "flip sig." Also size small. Now these were too small and tight. As you can see in the second photo, it was like some crazy muffin top and not flattering at all. So I exchanged them for a medium and they fit great! Just like the first pair did in small. So I would say there are some size discrepancies between colors.For reference I am 5'7", 130ish and size 4 pre-pregnancy.

I first got these pants in size 6, which is my usual size (I'm 5'8", 120 lbs.); they fit, but I wanted them a little looser for comfort (I'm 71 years old and don't like them "skin tight"). I returned them for a size 8. They fit perfectly! Very sleek and sophisticated looking! I love the longer length also! The quality of the construction, sewing, etc. is exceptional, as is the quality of the fabric. The colors are great also! I purchased the pink and navy too (would have got more, but I restrained myself!). Overall I'm VERY satisfied with this purchase and will look to buying more from Amazon Essentials in the future!

I first ordered these jeans in my normal size (4-6), but although I am 5’8” tall, I am short-waisted, and I did not like the high-waisted fit on me. I returned the original pair and re-ordered a new pair 2-3 sizes larger than my normal size. I also ordered it in the color “Blue” instead of “light blue” because they were lighter than the photo. Now I am super-happy with these jeans and love where they hit my waist! The fabric is soft and comfy, and I’m so glad I gave these a second chance! I hope this is to you in achieving the fit you’re looking for. :)

I first reached out to the company on the 6th of January, to see if this could be rectified before I left a review using the email they had on the happy/unhappy card with my purchase. No reply. I bought 3 pairs, all the same style in a size 1. So, the very first pair I received are Amazing! The fit was excellent, the length, love love loved it. Those were the medium blues. Was so stoked to get the other two being delivered the following day. Thought I had finally found “the jeans” you just love to wear. Wow, just completely shocked at the inconsistency in sizing. The dark wash was fitting like a 0, a bit tight on my hips but I could still put them on. The light blue acid ones. Omg. I couldn’t even get them past my hips. It was like trying to put 00’s on and I was a bad can of busted biscuits. I’m 5’9, and 114lbs. You can see each measurement just getting smaller and smaller. Each stating they are a size 1. No, honey they are not. I now believe the other reviews. It’s just incredibly frustrating and annoying that even though they are the exact same style, just different colors, none of them are the same. I’ll update my review once they finally contact me.

I found these on tiktok. I got a 5/34. I am 5’3 so definitely need a shorter size but I will also roll them on the bottom. I might get a bigger size just so I have that comfort in the waist. But overall these jeans are so cute and great quality.

I freaking love these stretch pants. They truly look like jeans but wear like a second skin. In my opinion these are spring/fall type stretch pants. Too hot for summer but wont keep you warm in winter. They are kinda long (I'm 5'4" about 170lbs) and they bunch at my ankles and sit high in my waist. BUT- 1. They're perfect to wear with calf boots!! 2. If you want to wear flipfloos or sandals...they easily fold up to look like a cuff. And 3. Even though they are high on my waist, there's no seem or zipper bulge; so no funky lines under a t-shirt. But best of all, it doesnt matter what color undies I have on. I can bend over and it doesnt stretch so much that my undies peak thru the fabric. Worry free wear! (And I have hips and a booty, so they definitely have to stretch!- lol). I would recommend.

I frequently have trouble getting pants to fit right. I ordered a medium. I'm 5'6 and about 135 lbs. The waist is a little tight and in the back, the pants are slightly baggy. I guess that's what they mean by loose. We know that fashion will dictate that it's tired of skinny jeans and pants. These pants are right on trend, as the legs are a nice stovepipe width. For me the length is good as I'll wear flat shoes or sandals. Unless you have shorter legs, the pants are a little short if you want to wear heels. The only thing I don't like is that there are belt loops on the font, but none on the back. If you want to wear a belt, there is nothing to hold the belt in place. That's why I rated them 4 stars instead of five.

i get these pants for my morning yoga classes and i have to say, they’re quite nice. here are my thoughts on it: advantages: fabric is so soft, i feel like i’m wearing a cloud. very stretchy, so it move with me while i do my exercises, no tight feeling at all. high waistband is great, it hold everything in place even when i bend or stretch. love the wide leg style, give me relaxed vibes, also make me look taller (even though i’m not). cons: length is a bit tricky, the shorter size is still little long for me (i’m 5’4”). after one wash, i notice tiny bit of shrinkage but still okay. overall, these pants are comfy for lounging and also good for yoga or even quick grocery trips. i would recommend if you looking for something that’s flexible and casual.

I got a size 6 and the fit is pretty good. I typically go back and forth between size 4 and 6 on jeans depending on brand. Since I never owned this brand before, I took a shot at a 6. The fit is fine except the crotch depth and waist height is a little too deep for my figure, but that is typical on me with other brands where I get a size 6. All that said, I would guess the jeans are close to true to size.I am 5’8” and the jeans are long enough to wear, but only with flatter shoes. They fall to the back of my heal. I prefer a jean that almost touches the floor in the back, so these are about an inch to 1-1/2” shorter than I like. Once again, I find this problem with other brands so I don’t think this is a sizing issue, just a figure issue for me. (I’m kind of short waisted and long legged).The jeans are made well and I like the fabric. They feel like “broken in” jeans from the moment you put them on (a big plus for me). They feel nice on the skin. The fabric is also a nice weight - not too thin or too thick. I found them comfortable to wear. The waist stayed in place and they didn’t “bag” in any areas after a day of wearing. The color is perfect. I do like the straight leg cut of the jean. To me it’s much more comfortable than skinny jeans.I have never owned jeans with cargo pockets before, so this is something new. I have mixed feelings on the pockets. I think it’s just something I need to find the correct top to go with them and they will be fine.Overall, I think these are a great jean and run true to size. Five stars.

I got a size 7, but I probably could’ve gone another size up since it’s tight around the waist. I do have a scar on my stomach, but the button doesn’t pinch it so it’s not painful (I don’t know my hip size, but I’m around 120 lbs), but for someone else who has bigger hips or stomach, I would recommend going 2 sizes up. Keep in mind the waistline will be the tightest part compared to the rest of the jeans. Overall, the pants are actually fairly comfortable and the material isn’t scratchy at all. It’s somewhat stretchy. Some jeans can be irritating if you don’t shave your legs, but this pair is comfy regardless if you shave or not! It’s only been one day, and the zipper keeps riding down when moving around. I don’t know if it’s because the waistline of the jeans is too small for me or if it’s just a general issue, but it isn’t a big deal. Jeans are a big long too, since I’m 5’3 and it kind of scrunches up around the ankles, but that’s an easy fix! I don’t have hardly any curves and my butt is flat as can be, but the pants did give me some of a figure (not by much haha!). It also didn’t slide down while bending, so that’s a plus. I never wore high waist jeans before, but this might turn me over.

I got mine just delivered and tried it on and so far, I like it. I was looking for some really comfortable pull up jeans that I could slap on the go. My regular jeans were suffocating me so I was really going for comfort. I was scared to get it because there were some splitting reviews. I got the size 4 and inseam 30. I'm 5'4" (163cm) and 130lbs (~60kgs). They are about the perfect fit and size for me, a tad bit loose on the calves so giving a loose skinny look. (I prefer the slightl loose compared to the skin tight jeggings look) They do give me a bit of a muffin top but I don't really mind as long as they don't either slip down as I walk around or squish me breathless. No slipping noted as I walked around. I had on regular skinny jeans right before (wax jeans size 3 - 26" 27" waist) and they are really similar at the waist. Band feels nice and secure and it didn't seem to give me the weird crotch. I will see how it wears after a wash but so far so good.

I got the medium charcoal grey pants and they fit great! I’m 4’11” so of course they were too long but I like having them rolled up so it’s fine. The only thing I wasn’t expecting is that they are a little sparkly. It’s really only noticeable in the sun but besides that, they’re perfect!

I got the olive ones mainly because in the ad photo they looked like they had a subtle vertical stripe texture. In person, they do have that texture and it's very pretty, enhancing a long silhouette. But guess what? As I removed them I saw that they have more pronounced striping of varying widths inside. So I turned them inside out and put them on, AND THEY ARE SO CUTE! Yes, I'm totally going to wear them this way. The details from the outside of the pants (stitching around real back and faux front pockets, waistband, fly, rivets) all show on the inside as well, I honestly don't think anyone could tell they aren't supposed to be worn inside out. OMG, are other colors of this legging striped inside? I have been looking everywhere for striped leggings. Most designers seem to think that legging stripes should be horizontal--you know, so that you look as much like a chubby toddler as possible. OK, so, standard features that other people who aren't as stripe-obsessed as I am might care about: 75% cotton very long-- I got the One Size; I'm 5'8", curvy muscular 140lbs, but there is plenty of length, scrunching at my ankles beautiful shading so that it's not just a bland olive smooth- and sleek-looking super-soft and comfortable stylish design great value for the price did I mention that they're striped on the inside and reversible?!

I got the one size leggings! My waist is about 36 inches, and the thickest area around my butt is 42 inches. They fit great and there was room to spare :) I’m a size 8 in jeans. I’m 5’9 and 165 lbs, and my inseam is a solid 30 inches. I have the leggings all the way over my belly button and there was still about an inch of room at the bottom so :) Notice the underwear line tho...

I got the pink/blue in a size large, and they fit great. I am a little worried about them shrinking in length after wash/dry, so I may opt to air dry. That said, I could have even gone with an XL to get more length and a more baggy look. (I am 5'7'' 155lbs) These are fun to wear around the house, and maybe I will get brave and wear them out of the house!

I got the plum colored pants in size medium. I am 5'6" and I typically wear a size 4-6. The length on these pants was just right. I'm unsure how I feel about the fit of the pants on me, but I'd say these are comparable to a 2-4 sized pant. I wanted these to look like skinny jeans, but feel like leggings. For the most part, they accomplish that. The fabric is thin, soft, and stretchy, which is good for physical comfort, but also means that the fabric really conforms to your body and doesn't feel like it's hiding anything. For this reason, I'd personally stick to darker colors that might not show every dimple off.

I got the XL in one of the black colors. I'm 5'4" and about 180 lbs. Approximately 36" waist, 43" hips, 40" bust. I'm gonna list the cons first, then the pros, because the cons outweigh the pros. Cons: - Missing button on the right side, loose button was nowhere to be found - Tons of extra fabric on crotch/lower stomach area, making it look weird when I bend the slightest bit - These were most likely returned and resold, as they came with the legs already rolled up (surely from a previous customer) and they had a strong scent of laundry detergent. They were probably returned due to the missing button. Who can blame 'em. - Packaged in just a clear plastic bag, making the contents more susceptible to stealing and damage - Fabric is pretty thin Pros: - Good stretch - Cute, if you squint hard enough - Mostly good fit - Good price, but... y'know. I would not recommend these. Sketchy. Go figure. Maybe I'll bring them to a tailor which will end up costing me in total the same amount I would've paid if I'd just spent a bit more on a legit brand. I'd return them but who can be bothered. Hopefully if I do keep them they'll hold up, but I don't trust it. Yayyy

I got these blue “high waisted” shorts during a flash sale for like $18 and I’m SOO glad I did! They fit wonderfully! I’m 5’1 142lbs and ordered the M, could get a S for a more snug fit but I love how comfy the M is. I haven’t washed them yet so I don’t know how they hold up in the wash, I’ll come back and edit once I do. They aren’t high rise really, definitely more mid rise which usually cuts into me weird but these don’t. The cut is extremely flattering. I have a leftover apron belly overhang from losing 86lbs this year, and the shorts hide it very well. So if I want to tuck in shirts, I can! Or maybe I’ll even try some crop tops, who knows! They make me feel so confident, I haven’t been able to wear jean shorts in years that actually look cute on me. I’m definitely ordering the black which they have a few variations to try. Maybe even white. They are also very flattering in the butt, which I have ZERO of since losing all this weight. The pockets unfortunately do poke out some, even though these are in no way tight. I might sew it a little to keep it from bulging out. I’ll come back when I get more to show what they look like. Ignore my Christmas shirt and dirty mirror - I really need to do laundry and I have a 2 year old who like to pretend she’s wa window washer.

I got these capris because I needed some brown capris to wear this summer. SIZE & FIT: I ordered the capris in size XXL based on the size chart. That’s also my usual size. They fit quite comfortably (I am 5”6’, ~220 lbs, with very wide hips). The design of the capris is casual, so if you’re looking for a tighter fit and you’re on the cusp of two sizes, you may want to size down. The fabric has slight elasticity. The bottom hem falls just under my knees. FABRIC: As I said, the fabric is just slightly elastic. I’m not a big fan of the texture; it’s neither coarse nor soft, but rather somewhere in between. I could see this fabric being nice and breathable in the heat of summer, though. The fabric is also prone to wrinkling. These qualities of the fabric are the main reason I’m giving the pants 4 stars; I was hoping for something a little better for the price of $32.99 (at the time of this review). APPEARANCE: The capris look nice enough on me, except when they’re very wrinkled. I could see myself wearing them in business or casual settings. I like the shade of brown; it's a nice warm brown. BELT: The belt is weird because it doesn’t have any holes. It doesn’t offer much support, but then, it’s not very constricting, so it adds to the comfort of the pants. Overall, I’d recommend these pants in terms of their comfort, but maybe not so much in terms of the fabric.

I got these capris pants so that I'd have some casual summer capris in a fun color. SIZE/FIT: I got an XXL in the olive green, and they fit as expected (the waist is a little snug on me, but I knew that going in, since I have a 42" waist - but they're pretty comfortable and stretchy regardless). I'm 5"6', about 220 lbs. The capris pant stops about 5-6 inches above my ankle. APPEARANCE: I love the color of the fabric; it's a lovely, light, slightly muted green. I like how the capris look from the front and sides. On the backside, however, the fabric kept getting caught up on my behind, giving my a slight wedgie appearance. For that reason, I took off a star. FEATURES: These pants have nice sized, open pockets, big enough for a phone or wallet. I like the wide, elastic waist. FUNCTION/FABRIC: The fabric of these pants is light and kind of crinkled; they would be great for the beach or on a hot summer day. Overall, I'm very happy with these pants, aside from the wedgie issue.

I got these jeans for my daughter & they look adorable on her. She especially loves how stretchy & super comfy they are. Sizing might run a bit small. She’s a size 6, which the description equates to a "small," but I decided to order up 1 size as a precaution & opted for the "medium." Thankfully, that was the right decision. They fit her perfectly. The high waist is flattering. They’re fitted on the hips & backside without being too snug. Although they’re wide-leg, they don’t look baggy, maintaining a stylish shape. These jeans have a sturdy zipper, metal button, & back pockets, giving them that classic denim jeans look. However, the material is thinner than your average denim & even thinner than other stretchy denim I’m used to. Despite the thinner fabric, you can’t see through them in the white color, so it’s not a major concern. My daughter is 5'7", & the bottom hem lands just beneath her heel, which makes them look long with flat sandals. However, they pair well with shoes that have a raised sole or heel, like boots & wedges. She did mention they’re a bit long below the zipper, causing a slight bunching when she sits. Fortunately, this improved after washing, as I expected since stretchy denim tends to draw up a bit. Although the care instructions advise against tumble drying, I did toss them in the dryer on a low, wrinkle-free setting & they held up just fine. Overall, these jeans are stylish & fit great. I’d recommend getting precise measurements & comparing them to the size chart, as you may need to size up. The denim is decent quality, though I’m a bit concerned about how long they’ll last given the thinner material. But mostly, she’s thrilled with how comfortable they are.

These are parachute pants to a tee - big and baggy and any time you move around they are crazy noisy like plastic rubbing together. Overall, I'm happy with these lightweight, ankle length pants that add a fun flair to my wardrobe!

I got this pair first, and then also the white one. First you see what they look like with straight bottom. They become longer after being ironed, out of the package. I'm 5'10", so it is an acceptable length. However, It's still a little short for me, when I walk and move around and I found them not very flattering. I had this idea. Purchased a pair of shoelaces in similar color; cut a small hole on the 'wrong' side of the bottom of each pant leg so that it is between the very bottom and the horizontal seam, and then I threaded a shoelace in each, to tie them around the ankle. And these pants become what you see on the last picture, with a tie on the outer side of the leg. This style is more attractive to me, and works better with this garment for my height. I did the same with the white pair. And I wear both frequently. I haven't had a chance to wash them yet, since I wear them gently and mostly for walks. I heard from other reviews that these pants shrink and pill. Shrinkage is the bigger worry. I have other linen pants that shrink and stretch with washes and wear. My plan with THESE pants is to iron them while wet after the wash, and stretch them out, then hang-dry. In my experience, this works with clothes that are prone to shrinking. At least for a while. For pilling/fuzz, I keep a fabric-shaving machine. It's been a great help for many years, and there's a great selection of them right here on Amazon. Hope this helps.

I guess I didn’t realize these are ankle pants, and boy are they ankle pants! With the waist o the pants pulled up to my true waist (now they are supposed to fit) they fall at about 4 inches above my ankle. If you pull them down so that they are not ankle pants, you have a strange “sagging” look and crotch line (second pic). I am 5’4” and bought a medium at first based on the pant measurements on this site. Those were too big, so I bought the small. Those fit right, but I’m not sure I like them. I’m a “pear” shape (more weight in my bottom half than my top half) and these pants REALLY add some weight to your hips and butt. It doesn’t look bad in pics, but makes me look very disproportioned in person. They are so comfy, but I’m not sure the bare ankles and chunky rear end are worth the comfort. Just not for me.

I had a pair of jeans like this back in high school and I think I wore them all the time. These will be no different! They fit true to size thank goodness! I ordered a 14 thinking maybe they’d run small, but it ended up being the opposite. These were very loose and I will need to return and order a smaller size. They are very stretchy which I honestly like a more structured jean, but if you like stretch then these would be great. They’re soft and comfy material. The buttons worked well. They’re not a thick, structured jean so if you lean more towards liking jeggings or like room for movement/ comfortable sitting, etc then you would like these. For reference I am 30 years old, 5’7” and 225lbs. I carry weight in my stomach, arms and back the most. I feel with how stretchy these fit, I could have gotten away with a size 12 or maybe even a 10. Other jeans I own and fit into are 12-14.

I had high hopes for these jeans but they felt short of my expectations. I’m 5’7” 157 lbs and ordered a size 8. I could’ve sized down one size but decided the stretch and loose fit is comfortable enough to keep them. The major issue is that these jeans fit more like a petite size. While I usually like to order a size long so that my ankles are covered, a regular fit usually hits just above my ankles. These jeans came at least 2” above my ankles so I decided to roll them and wear them like capris for warmer weather. They may work with boots when it gets cooler out but the length is just disappointing.

I had high hopes for these jeans! Sadly, I will be returning them. I’m 5’6” and currently 190 lbs - ordered a size large. Out of the bag, they had a super strong chemical smell. Not a deal breaker, as I’m sure it would go away with washing. The jeans themself are really cute! Great length and I like the style/color. Unfortunately, where the jean top meets the belly band is at a weird spot on my stomach and creates an unflattering bulge. The band doesn’t come up as high as I thought it would. I have a feeling I would be pulling it up often while wearing.

I had high hopes for these pants but my goodness, they look awful on me. The material is nice--lightweight and cool, 100% cotton--and the style is cute. Unfortunately, they're terribly unflattering. They even make my legs look short and I'm 5'10" with a 34" inseam. They make me look weirdly proportioned and make my booty look nonexistent (and it's definitely in existence!). My husband was like "uh uh no way--not even just here at home"😆. Normally the dress code at home is pretty relaxed (😉), so for him to say they look so bad that I shouldn't even wear them at home is pretty bad. They wash easily (I used cold, delicate, and tumble dry low). I wouldn't order these again, but not because there's anything wrong with the quality (from what I can see just by looking); rather, it would be because they look awful on my frame. For reference, I'm 5'10", 190 lbs, 38dd, 32 jeans, inverted triangle/apple shape. I ordered the XL, but they say 2x, so if that's the case I would definitely say they're too big.

I had high hopes for these pants. They arrived pretty well packaged, and the color is nice. The snaps on the cargo pockets work well. However, the pants were a bit too long for my wife. She is 4'11". I know they are supposed to balloon out a bit but these are too long even when lifting them up and pulling the draw strings. I suppose if you're a bit taller or have long legs then it's fine. These are really basic pants though. They have no branding or tags on them. I couldn't even find a size/care tag inside the pants. There's a torn piece of fabric hanging on a draw string on the right pants leg but I couldn't figure out where it came from. It could have happened when it was manufactured. The material is thin and my wife said it did feel comfortable. Overall, I wouldn't really recommend these pants if you can spring for similar pants with a bit higher price tag but also better quality.

I had high hopes for these shorts, but they fell flat. I bought a size small and medium. I’m 5’1” for reference. The small was pretty snug around the waist, but fit fine throughout the leg and length. The medium was just right around the waist, but was oversized throughout the leg. These shorts aren’t flattering at all, if anything they kind of make your bottom side look flatter. Both pairs felt a bit constricted when just walking, really odd, like no big allowance for stride. Not to mention the picture of the product, the denim variance is why I bought it to begin with. I loved the contrast, but the product I received was just plain and flat. Not worth the price and I’ll likely return both.

I had high hopes for these. Im a size 4 and 5'7". The fabric is soft, cut is perfect, even length which is hard to get right, but the claw like wash marks on front ruin the whole jean. I wish it was more blended in. Looking at the product photo now, it looks like they photoshopped it and blended in the weird marks. Almost perfect jean. Back to the search.
I had high hopes for these. They are cute but not soft or stretchy. Very stiff and I found them too short. For reference I’m 5’ 7” 145 lbs wearing a medium

I had higher hopes for this one. I’m 192 pounds and 5’6. I got a size large. What I like about it: the color is beautiful. The fabric is almost silky and super soft. It has pockets. What I don’t love about it: the fit was a bit weird. Because of the waist band, I expected it to hold in your tummy a bit but it doesn’t. The pants are not see through but the fabric was thin enough that if I pulled up the waist band to be flattering on my waist, you could see my cellulite on my thighs. If you’re smaller and don’t have cellulite, these would probably work great for you! The problem is if I would have sized down one, the waist band probably would have been more flattering but then the pants would have been too tight for my liking on my thighs. All together: for me personally: they’re okay, but not super flattering. I probably won’t be wearing them out of the house, but they’re super comfortable for at home, they’re loose on the legs and it’s a silkyish stretchy material.

I had low expectations for the price, but these are surprisingly flattering and comfortable. One caveat is that you need to size up. I usually wear a size 4 jeans so I ordered a size 6. They fit comfortably but are tighter in the waist and hips than my barrel jeans from Anthro. So if you want more of a baggy look at the top, you'll want to go up two sizes. I am 5'2" and the length is just right for me, so for a taller person they might be too short. The fabric is super stretchy and comfortable, color is good. They are cheaper looking than the Antro/Free People versions, but also $125 less. :-) Updated: after a couple of washings and frequent wear, the jeans are pilling. I have never seen denim pill before, so it must be from the quantity of viscose that makes them soft? I'm glad I have a pill shaver but just thought I'd mention it.

I had low expectations. Usually stuff like this looks good but when it arrives I’m so disappointed. These are well-made, good material, comfy, you literally cannot tell that all the items are printed on. No button, no belt loops, no itchy stitching, no rips. All printed on the material and they are sooo comfortable. The inside truly feels like my old ratty sweatpants but they’re cute as a button. I am 5 foot seven, 160 pounds. If I lost 10 pounds I still think they would fit. If I gained 10 pounds probably not

I had not purchased this specific style of Levi before. I checked everything online and all the sizing sounded correct. It is not as loose fitting in the thigh as I hoped, but it is not to snug. Hopefully it will relax a little. I was disappointed that the regular is sold as a 32' inseam, but it actually is 30'. I have attached a picture of the tag. I am trying to decide if I want to keep them or not, as I have black jegging's already that are 30'. Unfortunately I bought this on Prime days and the price is much higher today. Tip: If it is on sale order more than one size and return one. :-0 I am 5'4 128lbs.

I had ordered the navy pair of these leggings in M/L a week ago & absolutely loved them! They fit perfectly & are so warm & cozy. So I decided to order another pair in the skin color & they are COMPLETELY different! Different style, thickness & sizing. The skin has seams down the inside of the legs. Navy are seamless. Skin has 5" waistband, navy is 3". Skin is 8% spandex, navy is 15%. Skin has no front or back seam going up the front & back. Skin has regular leg openings at ankles, navy has sort of a stirrup. The skin pair did still fit me (5'3", 130lbs), but with less stretch than the navy pair. The fleece inside the skin pair is much thinner & not plush like the navy ones. However, the shade of this skin pair is a much better shade of "skin" than the shade shown on the website. Because I liked the shade so much I contemplated keeping them (they actually match my skin), but they were too thin to wear as pants & although they fit more like tights, the seams running down the inside of the legs made them unacceptable to me to wear as tights with my sweater dress. These had to go back & I reordered the skin colored ones from a different vendor. I'm hoping the other vendor actually sends the product advertised & doesn't pull a bait & switch as this vendor did.

I had originally ordered these jeans to wear as part of my Halloween costume. I had hoped they would have a little more flare on the leg, but they are just fine. I am 5’9” and weigh 265lb and have a mom bod. The length is definitely longer than average, but are the perfect length to wear with boots or heels. I wear anywhere between a 20-22. These are a size 20 and are snug, but definitely wearable. The denim is lightweight. The buttons are plastic, as I had read in other reviews, but seem sturdy enough. All in all, I’d say this was a good purchase!

I had originally ordered these jeans to wear as part of my Halloween costume. I had hoped they would have a little more flare on the leg, but they are just fine. I had read reviews before buying and opted to choose the smaller size than I have been buying for jeans with these (I have been getting size 8). My current waist line is 31 inches and my hips are 38 inches. I went with the size 6 (28 inch) and they fit perfectly with some wiggle room but none that make it appear they are baggy in areas. I don’t even have tightness issues around the waist when sitting down. I really love these jeans and will be buying another pair soon. If height helps to know too when comparing sizes to buy, I am 5’4”.
I had so much hope for these however they’re not cute of flattering. They also are slightly short. I am 5’6 135 lbs and am returning these.

I had such high hope for these pants. After reading reviews and measuring and comparing to size chart, I ordered the 12 (I’m 5’-2” and 158lbs). They fit perfect in the thigh and calf, I have bigger thighs) but the waist was gaping. I’m afraid that a 10 would be to tight in the thigh area and based on how much these gaped at the waist I think 10’s would gap too.

I had such high hopes for these pants. Today was the first day I wore them, so here's my review. I ordered my regular size that I would order at any other store...the fit is okay - a bit snug in the thigh/rear area. They are also quite long. I can't remember if there is a 'long/tall' size, but I just ordered the regular inseam and found that they were quite long (I am 5'7" for reference). If you normally wear your pants with heels, then this may not be an issue for you. The material is thin, so they may not keep you very warm this winter, but I don't find that it looks too cheap or anything. The problem with these pants is that I found a small hole in the leg with the threading already coming undone (see photo). I know you get what you pay for but I was expecting them to hold up for at least a few wears. Would not order again.

I had super low expectations for random brand Amazon jeans but wow, I am impressed! They have their flaws for sure, but they are WAYYYY better than expected and super comfortable. Some thoughts: - Run a bit large. I am 5'7" and 140lbs. I usually wear a 27/28 waist but went down to a 27 because they are oversized and I didn't want them TOO large. The fit is PERFECT, however, they are tighter on my legs than shown in the photos, I wonder if I had went with a 28 if they would fit more "boyfriend" like. - Unbelievably comfortable. They are so stretchy and so soft but they maintain their shape. - A bit shorter on me than the model. Again, I am 5'7" and they fit above my ankle. - Rips all look cute and are well placed - Butt pockets.....thats the biggest yuck. They are placed poorly for me and are not flattering on the booty. This is the reason I dropped them a star! Overall, I have already ordered another style from this brand! They're so comfy and cute! Very very happy! Definitely consider sizing down if you between sizes though!

I had these in my cart a while and continued to read mixed reviews. I was undecided on size because I've read that they are long. I'm a young seamstress and decided I could fix them. I finally ordered them and they fit fantastic. The reason they may seem long to others is because they're designed to be worn with boots. When I tried them with my sneakers at first, they obviously were long. But as with other bootcuts, they fit much better once you stretch them over a boot. I'm actually pleasantly surprised and I really hope they don't shrink because they fit just right.It's not easy to find good bootcut in my size. For reference I'm 5'1 with a butt and normally wear a size 5 in jeans or size small in sweats. Other than that, they seem good quality and the embroidery is flawless.

I had these on my Amazon Wish List, and my sister bought them for me as a gift. They are even better than I expected! I have a curvy figure with a small waist and larger hips/bottom (plus post-menopause belly), and these fit perfectly. I chose medium, my usual size in pants. The waistband is snug without being tight, and the length grazes the floor with shoes. (For reference, I am just under 5'5", 152 lbs.) These pants are comfortable enough for me to wear when teaching yoga but also dressy enough looking for me to wear to work. I'm thinking about getting more colors! UPDATE, 8/8/24 I've now owned these for about 8 months; I've probably worn them 6-8x. I am still really happy with the comfort and the fit. However, I've noticed that after washing, there is some pilling to the fabric - I can feel it when I run my hands down the front. I had thought about getting these in gray as well, but now I'm hesitant because I'm not sure how they will hold up.

I had to leave a review!! I was very nervous ordering denim shorts online but these are adorable!! I’m a curvy 51 YO, 5’1, 153 lb mama. I opted for a large and they work great! Comfy, thinner fabric, nice fit. I will be ordering more! Give them a try!

I had to leave a review!! I was very nervous ordering denim shorts online but these are adorable!! I’m a curvy 51 YO, 5’1, 153 lb mama. I opted for a large and they work great! Comfy, thinner fabric, nice fit. I will be ordering more! Give them a try!
I had to order a size down. Normally buy a S but ordered an XS due to some of the reviews saying they run large. Got the XS and they fit perfect!! I weigh 127 and I’m 5’5

I had to order these 3 times to get my correct size. First I bought large since another review said they were very stretchy I decided to look at them as leggings and L is my size. These were extremely small. So going by that size I sized up to 3XL short. These where extremely big. Tried one last time and did a 1XL short. These fit. But I could probably do an XL short. I did not like that they are a bit wider at the ankle than I would like. I’m hoping they fit a bit better after I wash them and some shrinkage. I’m also hoping they don’t fade in the wash. I will update after I have worn them. But they are very comfortable and I’m very happy with the color and stretchy material. Just what I was searching for. Price is awesome too. For reference I’m 5ft 3inch 199lb

I had wanted a pair of these jeans for the longest time but didn't want to shell out the money for the Fre* P*ople version. I ordered some from a different seller but those measurements were way off and I ended up returning. These are so perfect!!! I paid attention to the reviews and sized up. I would normally wear a 10 or sometimes an 8. I wanted these to be baggy so I went with a 12. I could probably have gotten away with a 10 but these had the baggy fit I was wanting without being too big. The material is a great weight. And, I am tall so I was worried about the length but these were perfect. They will be my go-to jeans for awhile!For reference I am about 5 feet 7 1/2 inches all and about 155 pounds.

I had wanted a pair of these jeans for the longest time but didn't want to shell out the money for the Fre* P*ople version. I had zero expectations for these, but I had them recommended by an Amazon influencer and decided to give them a try! First of all, I sized UP. I wanted a super baggy look, so after reading reviews I went with a Large instead of my usual Small. I’m glad I did! They’re super high rise, baggy in the legs but tighter in the waist. However, I can still pull them down lower and wear them slouchy. There is no stretch in the fabric, FYI. Length is long on me at 5’2” but I kinda like it. Print and fabric quality is actually really nice. But please note: they smell AWFUL right out of the bag. Like, truly so bad. I had to air them out overnight in my garage, then wash them inside out with detergent and laundry sanitizer. That seems to have helped tremendously. That’s really my only complaint. Still keeping them! :)

I hardly ever write product reviews, but as a chubby girl sometimes clothing items are impossible to buy based on size charts and reviews from people who do not have the same body type. I’m 5’3” and about 220 with a mom belly from having 2 kids back to back. They hit me at the perfect length and could be easily adjusted based on shoe type. These overalls are such a nice material of denim and have a crazy amount of stretch. The distressed look is super cute and the pockets actually function, since sometimes they don’t always have real pockets. All the buttons seem really well attached and stitching is great. They are incredibly comfortable and will work perfect for all seasons!

I hardly ever write reviews, but thought I would because I love these pants! I am a pear shape, and have a hard time finding flowy pants to fit my larger hips and smaller waist. These fit my curves so well, but also fit my waist in a flattering way. The material is light and flowy too. I hate wearing shorts in the summer, so these are a great fun option! For reference, I am 5’7” and about 185 pounds. I ordered an XL.

I hate buying clothes. I hate trying on clothes at the store. I have two pairs of solid jeans that are the exact same from Walmart. Every other pair I had busted from the thigh chub rubs! I needed a new outfit for blink 182 concert next week! These jeans are seriously perfect! I usually wear size 14 in jeans (at least from wmart) and that was how I determined I would wing it and get 16 since they were the closest in measurements. They fit like a glove! PERFECT! I love them and will definitely be getting more in other colors! 😍 I am always up and down between 185-200, 5”5 with most of the fat in my stomach area and 16 fit great!

I hate buying jeans online, but these jeans really surprised Me. For reference, I’m 5’6”, 150lbs, 28” waist, 39” hips (hourglass figure). The fit is perfect and so comfortable to wear. I like a high waisted jeans to hide my rice belly and these jeans do just that. Best part is inseam is spot on so I didn’t need to get the length altered. I will be ordering more in different washes.

i hate jean shopping, so finding good jeans on amazon is a miracle. these fit me PERFECT. i’m 5’3 and these hit me at the perfect point right at/below my belly button and are the perfect length when i wear my platform sneakers. LOVE!! i’m on the left on the pic and wear a size 25

I hate ordering pants of any kind online bc they are either too big or small in the waist, or too short in length. These are absolutely perfect in length and fit snug (but not too tight) at the waist. I feel like of I wear them a few hours they will loosen up some. I'm also very happy that they are not baggy around the butt or thighs. I actually like the way they look on me. I'm 5'8" and 130lbs. Ordered a 7 long

I HATE pants that ride up at the ankles, especially when I sit down. I want mine touching the ground (although preferably not dragging). I got these because of the high waist, stretch, and long option. I ordered the longest inseam, and I love the length. It’s size 6, and I’m about 5’6”, 130 lbs. They’re honestly a smidge big, but they fit my waist without feeling like they’ll fall off, and I don’t like my pants to be too snug anyways. I don’t think I’ll exchange for a size 4, even though that’s probably a better fit. I also don’t think these stretch a ton, but that’s probably better since they’re already a little large. That said, the smell really is terrible. I haven’t made up my mind if I’ll keep them. If they shrink and come out crisp from the wash, it’s worth it, but if they don’t, it’s a waste of money.

I HATE shopping for pants. I have long legs (normally a 32-34" inseam), so I either end up getting pants that fit my legs and are huge in the waist, or it fits my waist and doesn't quite fit my legs. I normally wear a 10-12, based on the other reviews that said they run large, I bought a size 10 and they fit perfect in the waist. They do sit a little low, so I have to wear a belt but they are super comfy. Definitely going to buy a pair of the brown ones as well. I got these to quail hunt in Coastal NC where we have gnarly car briars and sand Spurs. The double duck front works well so far.
I have 29 inch waist, 36 inch hips, these fit like a glove, very comfortable.

I have 35" waist and 48" Hips. I wear typically 14W or 16/17 jeans. I got XXL as I don't like painted on pants. These fit comfortably. My ankles have blood flow to my feet with this size. The waist isn't digging in and they are warm. The fleece is something new I've never had pants with that before but come snowy days that sounds spectacular.

I have 35" waist and 48" Hips. I wear typically 14W or 16/17 jeans. I got XXL as I don't like painted on pants. These fit comfortably. My ankles have blood flow to my feet with this size. The waist isn't digging in and they are warm. The fleece is something new I've never had pants with that before but come snowy days that sounds spectacular.

I have 41" hips and a 37" waist. Ordered the Large in black. The fit well. A little baggy around the front fly area and the knee and lower leg, so I might have even been able to go MD but didn't want to risk that "too stretched out" look - where you can see the glimmer of spandex - not a good look. These are very very long. As you know, all women are supposed to have the same length legs, so that's why we don't have different length pants, but I unfortunately have very short legs. I'll have to have about a good 6" chopped off of these, but that's nothing new. These are nice quality, thick, REAL BACK POCKETS!, nice stitching that looks like real jeans (deep yellow thread). 100x better than ones I had purchased at Walmart (Faded Glory brand maybe?) where they almost always have that stretched out look where you can totally tell they're not jeans.The black is borderline black - could almost pass for just a really dark wash denim, which is fine by me.Absolutely worth the price!

I have 44” hips and got the XL. The fit was good on the grey so I got two more in black and lilac. Stretch was good. But warning, it’s really warm after a while. If you do hot yoga or workout In it, you may find it too warm. If you need to stay warm in winter, they’ll be good for under long skirts.

I have 7 pairs of these in 4x and 5x. Navy blue and medium grey are lovely colors I favor. Fabric content (95% cotton) and short-pile furry lining are fabulous. Fit is great. Nice deep pockets. They're a little too long for me at 5'5" with average leg length and an 31" ideal/preferred inseam, but the ankle cuffs mean they still work for me. These are definitely go-to pieces in the casual/relaxation part of my wardrobe. The ONLY thing I did not like at all was the actual color I received for the Army Green pair -- see photo which accurately represents what I received and returned the same day it arrived. It is not Forest Green as shown in the listing pics, it's not even Army Green which is the official name of this color in the listing. As the photo shows is a dull, brown-green that looks like some kind of unfortunate vomit. The waist band is Army Green but the majority of the pants are this very ugly brownish green.

I have a 26 inch waist and 40 inch hips. I usually can wear a 28 in Levi’s but went with 29. These are only slightly stretchy mostly denim. They’re nice and sturdy denim but flexible not stiff. I had about 1-2 inches of a waist gap but I almost always do with Levi’s or non curvy clothing. I absolutely love the length as I hate my cellulite on the back of my upper thighs so this conceals it. I am 5’3” for reference. It is a button up vs zipper. Holds the mom pooch in well. They do bag out a bit after wearing 5-6 hours I noticed they were more loose and baggy than when I first put them on but they stayed at that level of being bagged out for the rest of the day.

I have a 26” waist and hourglass figure and purchased size 2 short. Denim is on the thin side but it’s also lightweight and forgiving. Love the straight cut. Very comfortable, perfect rise, great length and drapes well but need to wear a belt as there is a waist gap on me.

I have a 26" waist and, for where I like my belts to sit, this was a bit big. After adding two holes, it fit perfect. The triangle buckle is long making it a bit of a hassle to put on and remove. Keep in mind that the buckle prong won't sit correctly if the belt isn't a tighter fit (see last image). Even with the quirks, this is super duper cute and I will be wearing it lots :)

I have a 26" waist and, for where I like my belts to sit, this was a bit big. After adding two holes, it fit perfect. The triangle buckle is long making it a bit of a hassle to put on and remove. Keep in mind that the buckle prong won't sit correctly if the belt isn't a tighter fit (see last image). Even with the quirks, this is super duper cute and I will be wearing it lots :)

I have a 27” waist, and based on reviews, I sized up one size since they were said to fit small. The pants are a little long which I expected since the 32” inseam is a little long for me, but what I did not expect was the huge waist gap.. way more than just one extra inch to account for me sizing up. There is no stretch to this denim at all, so the other measurements are 100% true to size. Don’t buy unless you resemble the shape of plywood.
I have a 29” waist and 40” hips, and the medium fit me perfectly! They feel and look high quality. Good stretch. Comfortable. Excellent jeans!
I have a 32 inch inseam and they were perfect. Got them in white. Wore them to work. Not a soul knew they were yoga pants. Going to purchase more in different colors.

I have a 32" waist and 36" hips and the small fits nicely. The color is almost exactly the same color as my skin though so I wouldn't call it a khaki, it's more of a peachy tan. Aside from that the pants will be fine once steamed and they are very comfortable bc of the full elastic waist across the sides and back. I should have ordered beige as that seems to be the classic linen pant color that I'm used to.

I have a 35" inseam. I wear 12/14 jeans. I ordered the XXL so they'd fit loosely, but an XL would've worked as well. The fabric is comfy for a full workday. I love that they reach the soles of my feet, so whether I wear flats at work or stilletoes afterwards the length is perfect! The seperate sash is cute, too! And the belt loops can hold a wide belt if I want to jazz it up!

I have a lot of good things to say about these jeans! First off, the high waist is just perfect, it cinches the waist beautifully, creating a flattering silhouette. I also love the wide-leg style; it gives a lengthening effect that makes my legs look longer and adds a touch of retro chic to my look. The square pockets are a unique and also have a bit of a vintage vibe, which I adore. The classic denim is another highlight. It’s thick and stretchy, very comfortable,great for cooler autumn and winter weather. Plus, they’re super easy to style. I find they pair especially well with crop tops, creating a trendy, well-put-together outfit. Despite the quality and style, though, I’m not entirely satisfied with the Fit. I always order a Medium, but these jeans run large. I need a belt to keep them up. Also, the length is a bit much for me, given that all sizes have the same 30" inseam. At 5’2” and 116 pounds, they’re too long for my frame. If you’re on the shorter side and don’t want to hem them, keep in mind that they might be a bit lengthy. Overall, for style and quality, these jeans deserve a solid 5/5. If I were taller, the small size would have fit perfectly. Just be mindful of the sizing if you’re petite!

I have a male body, 6’3 and 220lbs. 30/34 pant size with a low torso and athletic legs. I never thought to cross the aisle and try these jeans but I am so happy that I did!! I got a large low rise stretch jean and they fit perfectly. Rides wonderfully on my thighs and there is very little snug on my calves. Hips are great and even the butt lift support gives the illusion that I actually have a butt!

I have a pair of 721's (High Rise Skinny) in Soft Black that I wear all the time, and the fit is perfect, so I figured the same size in 724 (also Soft Black) would be a slam dunk! But they are at least 3-4 inches longer! Why, Levi's? Why? The material is exactly the same, the cut is just waaaay longer. For reference, I'm 5'6 145 lbs and size 30 fits great- snug, but stretchy! In Soft Black at least. I've found every wash is a different amount of stretch so this review is for Soft Black only! Update- Chelsea Hour Dark Indigo had a similar fit to Soft Black, although a tad shorter. Even though they're cropped, they're way too long. Even the 10S hit the top of my foot and bunched slightly. They might shrink a bit after washing. Verdict: returning. Update 2: Chelsea Pier Dark Indigo - pics in the ad are super misleading. These are a thin extra stretchy denim - like super stretchy jegging material - and the 10S/30S brushed the top of my feet. The model for this style must be 12' tall bc they look like crops of her! The color is a very cute blue but only shoe I could wear these with is a flip flops or they'll bunch up

I have always bought my pants in a box store. After having four kids, and now going through the beginnings of menopause, buying clothes is not my favorite thing to do. In fact, I downright loathe it. All my jeans have become too tight now, so I’ve been wearing a lot of sweatpants and t-shirts which makes me feel sloppy and like a dirtbag when I’m out and about. When you look good and are dressed decent, you just feel good. I am sooo glad I found these jeans!! I have come to terms that I will never be a size 5 ever again, and so finding the right fit and comfortable jeans have just been almost impossible until now!! These are so dang comfortable, but look good and do a good job being flattering to all my lumps and bumps! I washed these first thing, and they are just so soft and feel beautifully already broke in! I’m 5’5 and these are the perfect length. Not too short not too long. I would call these more straight legged though. Not a super taper which I hate the struggle of taking super tapered pants off. I am just so ecstatic to take a chance on these jeans and now I feel like a million bucks! So much , I bought 3 more pairs!! Update- back to buy 2 more pairs, they are that good! Wash great and just get softer Another update- here to buy some more of these jeans. I’ve read a couple of negative reviews, but for most people they are going to love these jeans. I haven’t found a more comfortable jean that moves with you like these do and fits so well. I’d say for pple 5’3 to 5’5 just order regular sizes, not petite. Anything taller than 5’5 order tall or long.

I have been looking for some cropped wide leg jeans for a while and haven’t liked any of them. So I went with the 16 short and liked them for the most part. The issue was that they were a little baggy in the crotch and had a habit of sliding down. I can't even tell that I'm wearing jeans, they fit as comfortably as my leggings. This actually caused me to forget to undo them when I took them off. I included a squatting picture just to emphasize how stretchy and comfortable they are. The pictures with the green shirt are size 16 short, the purple shirts are the size 15 short. I would one hundred percent recommend these pants. I intend to get another pair later.

I have big time love for these pants, I have three different pairs: green camo, black, and the cow print ones. I have a size 6 in the green camo and the cow ones, and a size 4 in the black ones. The fit isn't super consistent, which would be my only complaint about these pants, because the two pairs I have that are 6's fit me differently. The waistband has some elastic in it, so they're stretchy enough to be super comfy without that elastic being worn out and too loose by the end of the day. I can wear these for 14+ hours and still be just as comfortable as anything, I've worn them to travel in, and I work in them constantly. The pockets!! There are 9 of them, all completely functional and very spaciously sized. Like the front pockets are deep enough to get my whole hand down to my forearm in them. I've never used the little pockets towards the bottom of the pants, but I like the way they look anyway. The side pockets are big enough for a ten inch tablet to easily slide into them, and the bottoms of the pants have ties on them so you can wear them loose or tie them and wear them more like a pair of joggers. I think they're versatile enough that they can be cute, or they can just be your new favorite work pants. I. Love. These. Pants.

I have come back twice for these shorts already this year, and so long as Levis doesn't change them, I will keep coming back! These shorts are incredibly comfortable and quite flattering. They have just enough stretch and don't loosen with wear. I am 5'8"/5'9"ish and sit between a 12 and 14 so I ordered 14 to keep it comfy and they are perfect! I could probably get away with a 12, but I didn't want to chance a muffin top look. With my rear end, I usually end up with a gap at the back of a lot of jeans/shorts, but these stay flat across my lower back.I DO cut the stitches with a seam ripper and unroll the hem to make them a bit longer. I have diabetes, and am insulin resistant. Because of this, my stomach bulges like a pregnant lady. It’s hard to find pants that fit right because they are always too tight in the waist and loose on the legs. If I get them to fit my waist right, the legs will be very loose and look so bad and frumpy. I am 5’7 and 175lbs, much of it carried in my stomach area. I chose 12Long but may have been able to size down 1 size, but they would be very snug. If you are my size and body type, I’d say stick with 12L, but if you prefer tight pants go down 1. If you have a big booty, go up 1. These pants fit my stomach AND legs properly. I have longer legs and the length covered my ankles very well and did not rise throughout the day like high waters, even with me bending and stooping down a lot at work. They were very soft and comfortable while still holding me in and giving me a nice shape overall. Made me look like a woman instead of an Oompa Loompa. I have only worn them once, but I have a cat and chinchilla so my black clothes always hold hair, but the hair did not stick to these. 5 stars! Will buy again! FYI: The back pockets are not real pockets.

I have never taken any time to review anything everrr until now. For reference, I am 5’1, 125lbs, and ordered a Small. I am not exaggerating when I say I’ve tried on countless of cargo pants only to be disappointed. They’re either too baggy, fit weird, or fit loosely around my waist leaving that gap in the back. These are NOT that. They are the perfect length for someone my height, not too baggy, and the elastic waistband makes them fit just right. The material is soft and very comfortable and you can tie the bottom of the pants for a tighter look (I tied the left leg so you can see with and without). Definitely ordering other colors!

I have never warn overalls but I’m already running to get more colors. I’m 5’8 150lbs and I got a M. I’m typically a size 6 tall in jeans but I opted to size up according to the chart for length. These are the perfect loose, comfy fit for a SAHM running around after my toddler. I could have probably gone with a small and been ok since they are adjustable with the arm straps and a looser fit. They feel like a light weight denim - ideal for spring/summer. Cannot wait to wear these with all my floral printed shoes and basic ts.
I have not one complaint about these pants. I love everything about them. I’m 5’1 & I weigh in @ 190 I ordered an Xl. Listen you will not be disappointed.

I have not worn jeans for 5 years, but these are comfortable and cute. The reasons why are-1) I have Interstitial Cystitis which means I cannot endure any pressure on my pelvic floor, and 2) I gained a lot of weight during CoVid lockdown so nothing fit. I’m finally down a little. Currently I am 5’7”, 178pounds, and hips measure 45”. I think that makes me size 14-16 depending on fit. I got the XL. They are not at all tight, even a bit loose around my hips, but I have not washed them-just tried them on. These are the Classic Blue and they have good color, some contrasting lightening in strategic areas, and zero smell-which I only mention because other reviews had concerns about chemical scent. You could probably get away with a heeled sandal if wearing in late Spring, Summer, or early Fall. They will look like capris on tall people. I’m a regular 30” inseam and wouldn’t be able to wear any closed-toe shoes except flat tennis shoes (think converse low tops). Love that they’re machine washable, and I don’t think they’ll shrink too much. They seem to be pre washed. Hope that’s to you all!

I have other colors from this Lee style & wanted the tan color. Despite the material being the same these are a heavier feel. The other colors are more lightweight. Due to weight gain I opted for a size 12 regular rather than a 10. The sizing chart doesn’t fit my body shape at all. Based on their sizing chart with my hips being 45” & my waist being 39” I should’ve ordered a 14 or 16 so take that into account. I’m also 5’6” & weigh 175 pounds. The size 12 regular fits me perfectly as I attached pictures to reflect. The material is 98% cotton & 2% spandex. There is very little stretch to the pants. The material is soft. I launder in cold water on the delicate cycle. I don’t dry these out of fear these will shrink due to the cotton. These do come out of the washer extremely wrinkled despite taking them out immediately. In hanging them to dry the wrinkles don’t come out. These definitely need to be ironed each time. I feel these pants are of quality & have longevity as my other pants truly have. The price is well worth what I paid. I do recommend just be aware of the sizing.

I have owned the fleece lined legging version of this pant for 3 years and loved them but it irks me to walk in the cold with no pocket for my phone. Shoving it in the front of my pants always cause my shirt to get tangled in it and I looked like a crazy person trying to keep my pace and wrestle it in the front of the pants. Enter the pocketed version. I ordered exact same size. Fit like a glove, right under my belly button, though the pocket is tapered and my phone is tight, I didn't think much of it and went on my walk this morning. I only did 1.75mi because I was so disgusted with how far these pants had fallen while walking that I turned around. I attached pics of before and after walk. I pulled them up once while walking and they resumed hip position again almost immediately. Also, due to the tapered pocket, only the top portion of your phone fits in the top, so you have to push, wiggle, wiggle, push, wiggle wiggle to get it far enough in, so it won't fall out...all while trying to keep pace. Within half a mile, I only push, wiggle, wiggled once and it fell right out and hit the ground...in the dark. Whomever designed this pocket, had zero thought about functionality while walking or running. Sure, the pocket looks sleek but it's awful. For reference, I have the S21 which is only exactly 3 inches wide. Do not buy these pants if you have an S10 or the giant Iphone because they won't fit. The only plus about these pants is that the fabric is very lint and pet hair resistant. I'm 5'5"/160# and ordered the large. I'm super pissed that I just wasted over $60 for fleece leggings that are pure caca

These jeans definitely have a much more flattering fit and the wash I bought is all one uniform color, which I like. The pants have a good amount of stretch and feel comfortable. I’m 5’5” and 128lbs and size small fits well. The fleece is MUCH thinner than the other pants I previously purchased and I am not super confident they’ll keep me much warmer than my normal jeans when outside.

user_comment: I have quite a few CIDER items and this quality is not different. It’s like a chunky sweater but in pants -HA!!! Super warm! Definitely a year round staple for someone who is always cold. I work from home (WFH) so this is perfect to lounge in. Very flattering and comfortable. I got a XL which is larger than my normal size. I am 5’1, 183 lbs and they are long on me but I wouldn’t say they are tall girl friendly. Plenty of stretch you can order your normal size. The waist band is stretchy. The fact that it’s high waisted is the best part! I could pair this with a Hoodie or sweater and it would be a cute outfit.

I have seen influencers use these pants on ads and have been keeping an eye on them to go on sale. So happy I bought them. I am in the small and with a wedge shoe they are the perfect length (I am 5’1”). They are lightweight and so flattering. All in all super happy with this purchase.

I have spent WAY too much money and time trying to find the perfect non-jean comfortable versatile pants, and my journey is over. Just bought two more colors. Lightweight, cozy, good quality, great hang, perfect length (5’5”) with heels and without. Might just switch out the drawstring for a more professional look for work.

I have struggled finding a belt that fits my tint waist and doesn’t fling out of the loops from having so much excess belt at the end. This belt has two loops which helps keep the end in place incase it falls out of the pant loop. I’m a size 0-1, 120lbs and the last hole is the perfect size. I only have it 4 stars because my $8 Walmart belt that lasted me 6 years seems to be better/thicker material than this. We’ll see how well it holds up!

I have such a hard time finding jean shorts that fit. I have bigger legs and a bigger booty but a small waist and shorts just don’t ever fit right. However, These shorts fit like a charm and were SO comfy!! 10/10 recommend!! I’m 5’1 and 130 pounds. I got a M!

I have such a hard time finding jeans that fit right. My waist is smaller than my hips so most pants either won’t go over my hips or if they do they are too big on my waist. I love pull on pants but usually I only find them in skinny pants. These are perfect! They went over my hips easily and hug my waist just the right amount. I find them to be very flattering hugging my curves in all the right places without being tight and constrictive. I am 5’2” and a half (haha) and the shorts length hit just right for me. I am about 215 pounds and went with a size 16. I typically buy pants in either a 16 or 18 so I would say they are pretty true to size. I did order the 18s as well to try and they were a looser fit but I felt the 16s helped wrangle in my trouble spots. I wish they had more variety of colors I would buy them all!

I have such a hard time finding sweatpants are long enough for me and these are perfect. I am 5 7 and 125 lbs. They are comfortable, fit perfectly, great quality, can’t see any wrinkles, and they look like they’re more expensive than they are. Definitely recommend!!! (I bought a medium)

I have such a hard time with shorter dresses being long enough for me. I’m 5’9” and about 210lbs and curvy, this fit me exactly like I had hoped! I bought an XL. I will say if you’re a bit larger chested the chest seam may sit a bit high but that didn’t bother me!

I have them in the blue and the brown. So dang comfortable and flattering. I am a 6'1” 60 y/o female sized between a 14 and 16. The XL is perfect. My only complaint is the pockets gap open at the top when you sit. You can see the lining. Easily fixed by stitching down the tops of the pockets using the top stitching as your guide. Plenty of room for hands to go in and out even after the correction.

I have these in black coffee (thick), beige (thin), and black (thick), all ordered in XS short. I’m 4’10” and had to get the black coffee hemmed 2” and the other two hemmed 1.5” to get them to the height seen in attached photos. My waist is 26-27” and hips are 36-37”. These fit me really well. The back has stretch to help with fitting. For reference, I’m wearing slingback pumps with a 2.5” heel and new balance 530s (~2” heel). The pants go well with a variety of looks. You can dress up and down as you want. Really good value for money. When I took the pants to get tailored the tailor complimented the quality and asked where they’re from! As for the difference between thin and thick - yes, the thin version is thinner, unsurprisingly. The thinner pants feel more like spring/summer pants and are a bit more breathable, while the thick pants have more weight to them. They both feel pretty soft to the touch. The thin pants have a metal clasp and the thick pants have a button closure.
