I have these in both the navy and the black. Purchased xs in both, I'm 115 lbs 5'5" for reference. Fit true to size. I've worn them running, biking, hiking, lounging, and running errands. Sooooo soft and cozy on the inside, pockets on either leg large enough to fit my Samsung Note (good sized phone), plus a small pocket on the waist band for card, key, etc. They been washed several times, turned inside out, and hung to dry with no noticeable wear. Hold their shape well. And, are more flattering than my more expensive cold weather leggings that cost 3 x what these did! A definite staple in my northeastern winter wardrobe! I plan to purchase more colors. Oh, and they're high-waisted, sit right at the smallest part of your waist to nip it right in! Perfect!

I have these jeans in two colors and I love love love the style and fit. They are comfy and doesn’t feel like you are wearing jeans if you are not a denim girly like me. I ordered the Caviar color but forewarning it is corduroy fabric if you are not into that! Otherwise still so soft. I am also 5’7’’ and the standard length is good on me. I recommend!

I have these pants in 3 patterns, and I've bought more patterns for my friends too because I just love these comfy cozy pants! At 5'4" and 150lbs, the large size fits me comfortably. The medium was a little snug with less freedom of movement. The pockets are an okay size, not too shallow but definitely not very deep. The fabric material is sooo soft and has held up to being washed regularly. Since I've bought so many of them, I noticed the cut of the print is not the same each time, so you do run the risk of having a giant bullseye print right on your crotch or butt, or having the pattern repeat in a weird way also drawing attention to the crotch. So, I've had to return a few pairs as well for bad cuts of the patterns, or for having holes in the pockets or ankle cuffs. These pants are cheap, afterall. But I can't get enough of 'em!

I have these pants in blue and black. I never wear the tie that came with them. They're slightly on the cheaply made size but still hold up well during wear and washing. They're comfortable to wear to the office. I am 5'3" and 130 lbs.

I have these pants in rust and apricot and I really love them. For reference: I am 5' 6" and about 125lbs. Pros: drawstring waist, lightweight, pockets, flattering for a flatter figure despite being a more flowy cut. They can be dressed up or down - I can wear them with almost any crop top...so great for travelling if you want to pack light but still have options. I could wear flats without tripping on the hem. Cons: I read reviews where they shrunk in the wash - I have avoided this potential issue by doing the lazy thing and not washing them. The lighter pair IS see-through, but I just wore nude spandex to remedy this issue.

I have to agree with other reviewers who referenced these as a Halara dupe 👍🏽. Every thing I need them to be: cute ✅ comfy ✅ perfect for sitting at a computer and getting work done ✅ running errands ✅ going to brunch ✅ lounging and relaxing ✅ not suddenly uncomfortable after eating a meal ✅. I just bought 4 more pairs in different colors as these will def be in my regular rotation of outfits. The material drapes well and doesn’t look too casual if you pair it with a dressier top and accessories. I have a 33 inch waist and a wear variable pant sizes depending upon the brand. I got a large in these and they fit perfectly! They were wrinkled on arrival, but I just sprayed them with wrinkle release and put them in the dryer for a few minutes and now they are free of wrinkles. Great addition to my wardrobe 👍🏽.

I have to say I have always loved this brand. Their jeans are so comfortable and only get better the more you wear them. These have such a flattering fit! I am also LOVING a cuffed jean lately. I have to start off by saying, just go ahead and add this dress to cart! It’s comfortable, lightweight, cute, and great quality. I wore this to the Houston rodeo at 25 weeks pregnant and received a lot of compliments. For reference I’m 5’7”, 180 lbs, a size 10/12 and ordered the large. It fit perfect! I’ll even be able to wear this postpartum which is nice. Definitely recommend for spring and summer!

I have tried on a lot of wide leg pants and these are my favorite. Very flowy material that drapes well. Comfortable, roomy, and elegant. I am 5’3 179 lbs with big tummy. Amazon recommended XXL. Technically it fit but was stylishly baggy, but too long unless I wear heels. I ordered M, L and XL to compare. Medium fit best because it was shorter length so I can wear it with flats. Can feel it is tighter at waist but still stretchy and comfortable. L feels like my correct size in waist but a little long, so am sticking with medium. L would be good with heels or I can hem them. I feel sizing is accurate overall since I am petite enough to wear size 8-10 M in oversized styles but tend to wear size 10-12 jeans or dresses. My recommendation is to choose your usual size if you are tall or wear heels. Size down if you are petite or short, because the style is so loose and flowy that it is better to get the length rightMy photos: XXL in khaki, M in brown and L in light blue

I have trouble with pants because I have wide set hips, a tummy, and Itty bitty chicken legs with no butt. These are a great solution because they're so stretchy!!! They fit comfortably around waist but aren't super baggy in the butt and legs. Still a bit bigger in those areas than I'd like but in a not quite tight enough way not a baggy way. They also cover my ankles even though they're high wasted and pulled up past my belly button! I'm almost 5'10 so pants always look like high waters on me. The most impressive thing about these was the fabric quality. Normally if I find jeans with amazing stretch like this then they're super super thin or like jegings. It doesn't bother me I like thin pants but then they wear and tear very fast. These are very thick sturdy durable denimaterial but SO stretchy and comfortable!!! An awesome pair of flattering jeans! Definitely a good order!

I have two pairs of these pants in black and they’re amazing. They’re a cross between jeans and jeggings. The problem is that they have a lot of elastic fibers woven in which makes for a really comfortable wear but over time and washing the elastic fibers break through the fabric and you’ll see little white strings all over your pant legs. BUT they’re so comfortable that I’m willing to overlook it and just buy more when that happens (hence the four stars). My main problem is that after purchasing two of these in black, I purchased the gray plaid pair and they fit completely different than the black pair! I’m 5’7’’ and 203lbs (16) and the XL was more than roomy for me (maybe even too roomy after a full day of wear). So when I ordered the gray plaid XL you can imagine I was more than surprised when I couldn’t even get them over my butt! (even lost a couple lbs recently, so that’s not the culprit). So beware that not all colors fit the same (for some reason?) Update: I took another star off, because I bought another black XL pair this week and it fits the same as the gray plaid pair. BE ADVISED: I’m assuming this company has changed the sizing for their pants (without indicating so)- size up!

I have very long skinny legs and have a really hard time finding stretch jeans that fit my legs and small butt. These fit perfectly, I ordered a 4 long, I have a 29” waist, with skinny legs and I have a 33” inseam and I absolutely love the fit. They have a good amount of stretch but not too much that they lose their shape, comfortable and well made.

I have wanted a denim jacket for years now, but never wanted to pay the price of one. I was a little skeptical of this because of the price, but I LOVE IT! I ordered the 3x. and My weight is 260 lbs. It fits perfect! The denim is not tight at all! It feels as if you have leggins on lol. Definetly didnt expect it. The little added details at the top makes it even better to. Such a good buy!

I'm 5'4", 187 lbs and the jeans are a size large. I plan on ordering the white and blue pair net.

I have worn and washed these pants a number of times before this review. I really like the cargo pockets for my phone - it keep the phone secure and flush with your thigh. The fabric is soft and stretchy and very comfortable. I am about 5'9 and these feel a little bit short for me, would have loved an extra inch of length. The waistband is high and doesn't dig in. Great pants for when you want to comfort of yoga pants, warmth of fleece, but still want them to look a bit more like slacks.

I haven’t worn jeans in many years, but I thought I should have a pair as they are useful. Mostly I wander about in some form of loose lounge pants. I like that these are made of a stretchable fabric so are comfortable. They really are comfortable, and I'm very particular about that these days. The fabric yields but look like jeans. The stitching and the sewing is very well done and I don't see any flaws--the quality is exceptional. The zipper slides up and down just fine. The button hole is a little tight but that will loosen. I'm definitely going to use these. I ordered an extra large size "24 short" figuring there would be shrinkage and I like things loose. I’m 5’3” around 180. Probably I'm normally a size 18 to 20. But I really don't like feeling the constriction of thick materials. The legs have plenty of room. For my height I have to roll them up about an inch and a half (see photo; sorry, am by myself and don't use mirrors, so these shots are the best I can do.) Jeans size 24 Short: 45.5" waist, 55.5" hips, and 18" bottom of the leg circumference (leg opening) My measurements: 47.5"waist, 52" hip Five pockets: A front pocket will hold my phone fine; I may add Velcro to secure it. The back pockets are very large, good for gardening gloves and a trowel. I have a wide waist and the jeans' waist fits me well and is not tight or constrictive, being a loose larger size than I normally wear. There's lots of room, and I may even use a belt. Happy to find some quality plus-size clothing.

Good thing I am not a gymnast or flexible because I could see them tearing right open if you did the splits. Would buy again if they keep the price low/affordable since they are obviously not meant for long term use. Such a bummer since they seemed great otherwise. Would only buy again if priced under $20. Wouldn't pay more since you cannot guarantee more than one use.

I just tried these on and I’m wondering what the raves are about in hiding the lower belly that is stretched from having kids. As I sit, muffin top is real. I’m 5’3 188 pounds so I ordered a 16 short. I received a size 16 but had to roll the pants up to fit my height so didn’t receive short. On the model the pants seem to hug the legs to show great legs but on me the knees are super baggy which is very disappointing. I utterly hate the process of returns and wish Amazon offered a doorstep return. These cost too much to be disappointed. To avoid the whole return process I’ll just chalk them up to yard pants and take this as this brand isn’t providing the look the model is wearing bc of her knee coverage. I just wanna preface this written review and say that yes, I’m a dude, and believe that this is a great 90’s look jeans purchase regardless of your gender. The men’s ones out there are just horrendous and I’m tired of buying and then returning (Amazon and in-store like Levi’s or GAP), and the ones from Temu never fits me because of the horrible sizing. I’m 5’9 with a size 30 waist and an athletic build (I wear size L or XL depending on brand for men’s shirt). After numerous research, I find that there some dudes out there still that still swears by women’s jeans only. Since why not? I used to wear tight skinny women’s jeans back in my emo-phase days in the early 2000’s anyways, so I decided to check out this baggy version that is in-style nowadays as I really like the style. I ordered a large and they fit okay, I could’ve sized down to a medium though, but the large is still good with a belt on. These are stretchy, lightweight, and comfortable. However, just like the women’s jeans back in the day when I used to wear the skinny ones, I’m still disappointed that women’s jeans doesn’t have a big pocket like the men’s jeans do. Why is that? Oof. I wish that would change as some of the ladies out there have these mega tablet-like size phone, and with my iPhone Pro Max, it was sticking out and when I sit down, or shift to a weird angle, they slide off. Same thing for my wallet. Overall though, these are the exact jeans I wanted and highly recommend.

I just want to saw that I really do genuinely like these pants! I purchased the size small (5'5 & 135) and they fit me really well. Loved the pockets & they looked great with a few different pair of heels. However, I returned because the belt is only held in place by those 2 loops on the hips. Just while trying them on the belt was moving all over in the back and exposing the zipper. When I sat down the belt would rise above the pants and onto my lower back. Please please add some extra loops in the back or sew the belt in place and I will happily repurchase!

I kept eyeing this jumpsuit and was excited to see it went on sale so I bought it right away. I’m 5’7” about 220lbs and 40D and carry most of my weight in my belly. The XXL is a perfect fit. The fabric is thin so it’s a great summer outfit. The legs are wide and flowy so super comfortable. It’s more comfortable than wearing pajamas. I WFH so it will certainly be one of my go to outfits during the week. The top area is more comfortable than I thought it would be considering it’s a scrunchy material. I’m not wearing a bra in the photos so I’d say you don’t really need one but I do recommend wearing nipple covers only because the scrunch material irritates that area. If you are wearing it for a short period of time then you can probably bypass the nip covers. At my height, the crotch is at the perfect area - not too short and not too long. The pants are an inch or two too long but I plan on mostly wearing this around the house but a platform sandal will fix that for you. The mustard color is more of a burnt orange than mustard. Doesn’t look like the color advertised but I’m completely in love and will be buying it again in black.

I kept seeing these on my insta feed and finally caved and purchased them, and honestly, I kinda love them. I am a small and 5’1 and they are long on me but works with my chunky sandals. The material is a thin jean so it works for summer months. 10/10 recommend if you’re looking for a fun style of overalls.

I kept seeing these pants come up in a reel for a Flamingal ad on FB and I loved them but the model was so tiny and in a little cropped top and I thought they wouldn’t look as good on me and I didn’t know or trust an FB ad but after a few weeks of seeing them I decided to order them but I looked for them on Amazon instead of ordering through FB and there they were! I had them in two days and I’m in love with them! I ordered a M in khaki. I’m 5’3 and weigh about 160lbs and they fit perfectly. I kept seeing reviews saying they're too big so that’s why I ordered a medium instead of a large so maybe go down a size. They’re so comfortable and so stylish! I wore them from 7am to 6pm and they looked exactly like they did when I put them on that morning. They are stretchy but they do NOT bag out at all. The quality of these pants is excellent. They do not feel cheap at all and that’s coming from someone who only wears $300 Mother Denim jeans and thousand dollar shoes so trust me when I tell you that these pants are nice and not cheap. I’m actually ordering another color right now. I think the dark grey and also the charcoal color. I highly recommend them!

I kid you not - these overalls are AH-MAZING! I'm 5'4" and 160lbs of muscle and finding pants that fit is like trying to find a needle in a haystack. If they fit my hips, I can't zip or button. If they fit my tree trunks of a leg, then they're much too big in the hips. These are a PERFECT fit. A beautiful and comfortable mix of loose and hugging in just the right places. I knew that there was no risk in ordering Aeropostale clothes without trying them on first. For reference, I am 5 feet 5 inches tall and weigh 130 pounds. I usually wear medium-sized clothing, and I got these pants in medium size, which is US sizes 6-8 according to the measurement chart provided by the seller. The pants are a bit loose on me. They are more of a size 8 than 6. My medium is more of a size 6. I do not know if they will shrink a little after a wash. I also got a regular inseam, which is 32 inches, which is too long for me. I will have to make them shorter by at least an inch. Other than that, the pants fit my body nicely. There is nothing weird about their cut or shape. I like the fabric. It is very soft and has a little bit of stretch to it. The pants are very comfortable. The craftsmanship is impeccable. So I would highly recommend these pants if you were looking for a pair of casual ones.

I like how comfortable the pants fit and I received many compliments from males and females. The only con that I could say about these pants is I had to keep fixing them in my crouch area. I'm 5'8 and weigh 170lbs. Purchased a medium.

I like how stretchy the material is. Thick enough. I love the flare as well. For your reference: I am 5'2", US Medium. 160 lbs. Somethings can be improved in the design. Adding some side pockets would be really nice because it would really make it look like real pants instead of jeggings with elastic waistband. I kind of like to tuck my shirt in my pockets. The waistband is a bit too tight. These are high-waist pants and it being so tight around the waist is restricting because I have GERD it messes up my stomach because it pushes my stomach too much. For that reason I can't wear it as much as I want to. If the waist band is at least 2 inches loose, these would be comfortable. I like that the hemline is raw so I was able to cut these to match my height and not worry about sewing. Because I am short, the hole is not hitting me at the knees, but it doesn't bother me. The only thing I really would say are not very good at not having pockets and the waistline being super tight. Other than that, I would have give these 5 stars because they are cute! If you are tall and skinny, you would love these!

I like how the Dickies look on my wife and can't detect any obvious issues, but my wife feels these jeans are oddly sized. When I looked more closely with a discernible eye, I concur that it's an imperfect fit. The basic design suggests form fitting comfort and in some areas of the body, it delivers on that point. But where some areas fit right, other spots are incongruous and are not a good match on my curvy wife. First off, the size 10 R is long. My 5'5 ½" wife wore 2 inch heeled boots (see photos) and the leg hem is to the floor. In all fairness, my wife wears size 8-10 pants and perhaps the 8 would work better. These definitely run large. She needs to keep pulling them up as they slide down, even over her curvy hips. She will need to wear a belt and high heels to make them work. They look good around the legs and derriere, but are too wide on the waist and feel loose and bunchy around the hips and crotch. The actual material feels like value fabric. These are not super smooth jeans. They feel and look a wee vintage and in that regard seem like old fashioned jeans of the 80s but with a little more stretch than pure cotton pants of the past. The stitching and weave is decent and the button and zipper work great. They appear to be sturdy, breathable pants that will last a long time. The pockets are nice and deep and there is a slight stretch throughout, but almost too much stretch to the point of slippage and instability. My wife isn't sure if she will wear the Dickies much as she has other shape jeans that look and feel better. But all things considered, it's a good value for a basic classic bootcut jean for the right body type. Just bear in mind that they run long and large. ~ Kort

I picked up the medium in the reef blue and I am happy with the color and the fit. It is a good length on me. My calves aren't bad, but above the knee things take a turn for the worse. This dress hits me just right, short enough to be cool and comfortable in the summer, but long enough to fully hide my thighs. The empire waist is flattering and gives me shape without overwhelming me with ruffles. It is an interesting design, as the tiers are only in the back. The front is flat with just the one seam at the waist. The sleeve is nice as well, decent in length to hide my upper arms. Made in China from 85% cotton, 15% polyester, this dress appears to be well made, the buttons are well sewn and all the seams and hems are finished nicely. Despite the cotton/polyester blend, the fabric is not stretchy at all, but I went with my usual size and it fits well. I just wanted to point out that I am not at all busty, I carry my weight in the bottom, so this style dress works well for me. If you have a large chest or are very broad across your back, you may find this dress too restricting. Overall, I am very happy with this dress and think it will serve me well this summer, even into the fall. This can be worn very casually with a pair of Converse sneakers to a back yard picnic, or dressed up a bit with a sexy strappy sandal and a great chunky bracelet for dinner out. It would even work well as a bathing suit cover up. I could also see this with boots and a little cardigan for fall. It washed up well, I did a cold water wash and hung it to dry. There was minimal shrinkage and no color fading, it didn't wrinkle too badly either. Bottom line, a great wardrobe basic that you will reach for often!

I like the look of the Joe's Jeans The Trixie Trousers, but they have are a higher high rise than I was expecting and aren't the most comfortable for me. They totally cover my belly button. This important information isn't in the listing, but I found it on the brand website: Rise: 11.5", Back Rise: 15.125", Leg Opening: 22.5", and Inseam: 28" *cuffed*. I also found that some other sites say these run small, and I agree. I have a 29-inch waist and 36 hips, and I got a 28. They fit me snugly, but I prefer some extra breathing room, so I probably should have sized up to the 29 in these. I wear a 28 or 6 in other jean brands and have worn a 28 in Joe's in the past. I was thinking these might be a bit bigger, because they have some stretch. They do stretch out a little and feel more comfortable after I've been wearing them a while, but it's not a big difference. I'm 5'5", and the height is perfect for me when I'm standing -- they are just above the top of my feet. But, when I sit, they ride up a bit, I guess because of the cuffs and high rise. These won't be favorite jeans, but they are fun and trendy. I recommend them. Just make note of the 11.5" inseam and size up if you're between sizes.

It is labeled as a size 12 on both the fabric label inside and the paper label outside, but it is not a 12. I’m currently in a size 10 (used to be in a size 12 a few months ago) and this pair fits me like a tight pair of yoga pants. It is really, really tight. I compared it to other pants in my closet (none of them Lee) and it narrower in the hips and waist then the size 8 that I keep around just in case I lose some more weight. Another sizing issue was the length. I’m 5’9” and these pants were actually a little long on me.So nice quality, but I think I got a size 6/8 and not the size 12 that I ordered.In the picture below the brown pants at the bottom of the stack are a size 12 by Investment, the white pair is a size 10 (a generous size 10) by Kasper, and the black stipples pair is a size 8 by Raffella. You can see that the size 12 Lee is smaller than even the size 8.

I like the style of these mid rise jeans and they are flattering on my hourglass figure. I usually wear a womens 10 in pants and ordered a large based on the size chart. The fit me perfectly when I tried them on; however, they shank when I washed them. I washed these jeans in cold water and tumble dried them on a low temperature setting (the tag recommends hand washing and line drying, but who has time for that?). The length is still ok on me after drying them. I'm 5'6" and the length is good to wear with sneakers or flats, but the jeans would probably be a little too short to wear with heels. After washing, the stomach area is now a little tight on me. I could still wear them if it wasn't for the fact that the light weight denim fabric pulls out of shape at the zipper. I know these are mid-rise jeans, but the rise feels a little short. With the light weight fabric and the rise, there is a very subtle camel toe situation going on for me. There must have been an adhesive label on the back side of the waistband that I didn't notice before I washed them because there is now a spot of gummed adhesive that will probably never come out. The style of the pants is cute and the quality is fine, though they seem a little over priced at $39.99. If they hadn't shrunk, I would have been happy with them. If you plan to tumble dry these and wash in any temperature other than cold I would recommend sizing up at least a size.

I’m 5’ 8”, 180 and ordered size 18 according to the size chart. They fit well everywhere except the waist is too big. I can still wear them, but I think I probably could’ve ordered the 16. These are a nice pair of jeans for the price.

I like these Dokotoo cargo-style hiking shorts more than I thought I would. They fit me pretty well, and I like that the waistband is tie-style, it’s stretchy and adjustable. The material itself has some stretch and give, it isn’t completely stiff but don’t expect it to stretch a whole size or anything. For reference, I’m about 130lbs at 5’5”, and my measurements are 36”/28”/38”. I usually wear a size 8 in pants, though I recently lost some weight so I might fit a 6. Medium works well on me! The pant legs are a little larger than my thighs, but I’d rather have them be a bit too big over too small. The only things I’m not as thrilled about is the fact the pockets on the back aren’t real (but the front pockets are and nice and roomy!), and that the care instructions seem difficult. The label says it must be hand-washed cold, and can’t be dry cleaned or tumble dried, and to cool iron if needed. I’m not sure who really has time to hand wash their clothes, but it isn’t me! However I did throw them in my washer on a regular cycle with the rest of my laundry (on cool temp water) and they seemed to come out just fine. I hung them up to dry and it did seem like they wrinkled more than I would like, but that’s nothing an iron can’t handle. Overall I’m liking these shorts and will continue to test and wear them over the summer! So far they’re very comfortable and trendy. I recommend them and hope they will last me at least for the next few seasons!

I like these due to the bootcut flare bottoms and stretch waist. I have purchased the black and the blue and they fit the same. I ordered the XL and they are a tad snug in the waist but a comfortable snug if you know what I mean. I am 175lbs, 5’5” and wear a size 12 in jeans and a medium/large too. With a 1” heal shoe on, these almost hit the floor and sometimes do. Hope this helps, have a great day!!

I like these jeans quite a bit. I broke my foot and wanted something that would fit over my orthopedic boot, and these did great. But I also found them to be very flattering. I'm 5'7" and about 200 lbs, usually a size 14-16, with a slight pear-shaped body and I carry most of my weight in my belly (Mother of six, any surprise?) The 16 fit me nicely, and I don't think I would have been comfortable in a 14. The jeans are fitted through my thigh and knee and open up below that. They just skim the floor when I wear low sneakers or flats, so they can easily be worn with some heels and boots as well. The waist is the definition of mid-rise, coming just above my hips and about to my belly button. After a couple days of wear, they loosened just the tiniest bit, which added to the comfort but didn't have to be hitched up constantly. (That's important to me as a mom!) I got a darker wash, which I like but they kind of turned my hands blue before I washed them. I definitely recommend washing these separately the first time. These aren't "Oh my gosh! Best jeans ever!" But they are very good jeans, and I don't regret the purchase at all, especially at this price, and with the option of Amazon wardrobe. I also tried the Amazon Essentialls mid-rise authentic bootcut jean and preferred these because of the fitted upper leg, which the "authentic" style lacked.

I like these jeans they are very comfortable and they are long enough for me. I always have a hard time finding pants because I don’t like wearing short ones. These fall a little past my ankle. I am 5’6 and weigh 185. I got size 14. I think they gave my flat inverted shaped butt a little bit of a lift.

I’m not saying these are going to be my favorite jeans that compliment my body super well but I needed a lighter wash jean with no rips. Btw I am 5’2” and fluctuate between 125-130lbs with a more athletic build (thicker thighs and butt) and I originally bought the size 28 after reading reviews when I’m normally a 27. 28 was too big on my waist and gave me a weird V shape in the front, wasn’t a camel toe but in my third pic you can see what I mean. Just be aware that there’s almost no stretch but I assume they’ll break down after wearing and washing them a few times.

I like these jeans. They are inexpensive, and light stretchy fabric. I normally wear a medium, but it was huge! Small in the light blue fits great. Small in black is a little big, but I think I may have received a defective pair of small black jeans. For reference, I have 29” waist, 38” hips. 5’6”, 135 lbs. Photos are 2 smalls and 2 XS pairs. As you can see in the photos, the bottom 2 pairs were both a small, but the black pair is significantly bigger. The XS were more consistent, but due to stretchiness of the fabric, I can squeeze into the light blue pair, but not the black. Second photo is length - the small black pair is significantly shorter than the XS, even though they are both supposed to be regular length - which is why I think I got a defective pair. Im debating ordering a new pair of black Small because I think this pair may have just been sewn wrong, but I hesitate because of the cost of shipping and the length of time to receive the order.

I like these jeans. They are soft and have the elastic waist band. The waist is higher, so they don't fall down when I bend over or sit. I also like the shorter length. I am 5'1", so it's hard to find jeans that aren't too long! I do feel they are a little on the thin side, but I do not see any wear so far. The waist band does not stretch as much as other jeans I have bought of this same style from Levi. Overall, they are a great pair of jeans for the price.

I like these Levis. I'm 5'2" & weigh 112 & ordered a size 27 short & they fit comfortably. (Could have worn a size 26 b/c they are stretchy, so you might want to size down.) The waist goes to right @ my belly button. Happy that flares are back again & would recommend these Levi's jeans.

I like these pants a lot, however they are baggier than I thoughy they would be but I like it. If you’re not into baggy pants then don’t get these. I like the material and feel of them and there’s some stretch to them (95% Polyester, 5% Spandex), and I love the color. I’m tall (5’9”) so I thought about ordering the medium-tall size, but something told me these would be long enough in the regular size so I went with that and they are perfect, hitting right below my ankles. These pants are so cute with a fitted top and heels, but I wouldn’t wear anything that’s flowy or baggy on top with these. Overall I really like them and I will definitely get wear out of them. I like these pants more than I thought I would. The fabric is soft and drapey, and I really like the pockets on the front. The buttons are just for decoration, not functional. For reference, I am 5 foot two, approximately 110 pounds. These fit me perfectly, more akin to a size 4.

I like, they are comfy, fit at waist, stretchy and cute for casual look Size 10, 5’7”, 155-160#

I liked almost everything about these lounge pants--the velvet-like fabric, the way they fit, the fact they were actually long enough for my 5'7" height. The only problem was that the photo of the color I selected, brown, was pretty far off from the actual color. The photo makes the pants appear as a dark chocolate brown--but in real life, they ere a much lighter brighter almost rust brown. I don't know if this will be true for the other colors but it is a problem in the brown pair.
I liked everything about these pants I plan on ordering more in different colors. I'm 5'5 ,150lb the medium fit well with wiggle room .

I liked the color of the faded black and the length was good for me at almost 5'8". What I didn't like: The fabric was not comfortable at all. Maybe it would soften over time? The fabric sort of creased or puckered right under the patch pockets as you can see in the photo. It's very noticeable when I walked in them and I'm worried by the end of the day I'd be left with a baggy crotch look. Have to return and try other options. Also the rise does not reach my belly button. I measured the rise at 10.5".

I liked the dark denim. True to size. I’m 5’4, 200+ lbs. This waist band is sturdy but comfy & goes up enough. I liked I could fold at bottom for that casual look. Thick enough not to be flimsy, but thin enough to be comfy. I was cautious & bought 1 for now. I’d buy another👍🏼

I liked the fabric which was flattering on the hips and from behind, but the crotch was a really weird fit that I would not wear in public. I am surprised because of all the very happy reviews. These pants clearly showed belly fat, even in black, and then had a strange tent of fabric in the crotch. I followed sizing I found on another website because Amazon didn't have the plus sizes in their size chart. I will not be keeping these pants. If you do not have belly fat/rolls, these are probably lovely. Also, I am 5'2" and they were too long. This was expected as the inseam is 30", so I would have hemming or in heels for these.

I liked the fit and the feel. It's hard to find pants that fit well because of my height. I'm 5ft and even pants that are labeled petite or short, can be too long. These fit great and are comfortable, but unfortunately they are too see-through for me. Even with nude underwear, you can see the lines and the white pockets stand out under the fabric.

I liked the fit in the front and the color. The material is really light but not sheer. The fit is perfect. I'm a standard large in most things and this fit just right, not too snug or loose. There are two complaints I have with these pants through. One is the length but can't blame the company completely because I am only 5'3 however, the extra length I would need to hem is over 3 inches which is a lot! If you're 5'6 and above then you wont need to hem. The other issue which is the biggest is that this is just not flattering on your behind. Not sure if you can see in pictures but in person it made my butt look out of place. Hard to describe, perhaps the scrunchy waistband is giving it a weird look, not sure.

I liked the fit. It’s a loose fit. It’s a big long but nothing too bad. I didn’t want it to be tight and show my underwear lines so I got a medium. A small would fit tighter if that’s what you are looking for. A medium is a loose fit. I’m 5’2,118 lbs.
I liked the jeans but they were a little to snug for me. I normally wear an 8 or 10 and I ordered the 8. I’m 5’6” 143 lbs with a 33in waist. I’m going to return and try a size 10.

I liked the material and flow of the pants. They are well stitched and are stretchable. The only thing is they are very long for me I’m 5’3 and ordered medium which is the usual size for me but still they are long. I wish they had reasonable length to wear them easily.

I liked the pants and the blue color was very beautiful. The fabric was syntethic and they are made well, however these run small. I wish I'd known so I could size up. One thing to notice is I am.only 5' 1" and I did not need to hem them, so these may not fit tall people.
I liked the pants, but they looked and felt too small. After checking the tags, I realized they were not the size I ordered. I'm 5'5 feet tall and a size small.

If I had ordered my normal size they would have bagged in the seat. Biggest issue I had was the waist was very small compared to the hips, thus the photo. I'm pretty straight. Waist is tight but because it is very stretchy I kept it. I would order more if the waist was more comfortable. It could be a fluke and maybe the pair I bought was defective?
I liked them first but I was wrong—they were ripped off when I tried on three times. (By the way, I am 5.4 and 118 pounds.) It was embarrassing because I was in public!! Very disappointing.

I liked these jeans for the retro look and especially for the slit that’s at the bottom of the Pant leg, I thought they looked really cute and would be really Good with some cute shoes,, However, I got my jeans and they do not have a slit on either leg. They are completely sewn up like regular jeans. I don’t know if this was a defect for mine that I received or if The picture they advertise is not the correct picture of the jeans they send.. So just keep that in mind. However, they do fit very well. They are a little bit thinner material with some stretch. For reference, I am 5’7 ,180 and I got the 33/32.

I liked these jeans. I am 150 pounds and 5 feet 2 inches and I needed a size 10. They are a true palazzo pant, which I like, but buyers should be aware that they are "flowy." I am 5'6" and weigh 155 lbs. I normally wear an XL but got the XXL as prompted by Amazon. They are very long and almost touch the ground. They fit well but I think the XL would have been fine as they are stretchy. They don't look like a pajama bottom and you could easily wear them to run errands, grab lunch etc. Highly recommend!

I liked these pants, they are a good fabric to me personally, but they were a bit big in the legs. But I’m also super skinny and wear a 000-00 in jeans (hopefully that helps give you an idea) and they fit nicely, but not as snug as in the photos. I’d get them if there was an xs size, but idk. THEY ARE ALSO SUPER SUOER LONG. In those photos I have 5-6 inch heels on and they make me 5’4-5’5 so that should also give you a little idea. I’m also pretty short so they probably look weird on me, I’m originally 4’11 btw, but if you get them, just know they are very long, but they are cute and stretchy and worth the price I paid

First time I wore them I was in an upscale mall and I was stopped twice to be asked where I got them. I have not had to wash them yet so can't advise on that. Highly recommend.

I liked these trousers even more than I thought I would. With white/cream, it is always a concern that they’ll be thin or see-through, which is not the case at all here. The fabric is silky and light, but not chintzy or cheap feeling. The draping is perfect for a lightly pleated front, Creating a clean line that doesn’t hug or accentuate my belly, which is also a concern for me, as I am not as flat in the stomach area. I ordered an XL, with the intention of having a looser fit, I normally wear a 12/14, for reference and am 5’3”. They were comfortable and loose, but not clown baggy. Love the pockets and extra elastic on back, which allows for more comfortable fit at the waist. I wore them to work and got a ton of compliments. I may need to buy in all colors now.

I live for leopard print! The first time I ordered this jacket Amazon had suggested that the large would fit me best, so I ordered up from my normal size medium. I assumed that maybe this piece ran small. Nope…it was WAY too big for me, and not in a cute, oversized way. So I returned it and ordered a medium. Still oversized, but it actually looked adorable. I think I definitely could’ve sized down and still had plenty of room to move around in. It’s meant to run a little large though, so if you’re looking for a snug fit then order a size (or two) down. Otherwise go with your regular size and you’re golden. One thing I noticed is that the print is clear and defined. Sometimes the images/prints on clothing are a bit blurry, but this one looks pretty pristine. The price point isn’t too hefty…especially for denim, and the material isn’t itchy or incredibly stiff. I wouldn’t suggest wearing it in cooler weather unless you really plan on layering up. I love that it can be styled in so many different ways…with jeans, leggings, skirts, trousers, tees, sweaters, dresses, button-ups, etc. I feel like the possibilities are endless. For reference I’m 5’3”, 152lbs, 36C and ordered a medium. The only reason for the 4 stars rather than 5 is because the first medium I received had a broken button.

I tried a size 25 based off the size chart and found them way too big, so I exchanged for a 24 and they are perfect. I line dry most of my clothes so shrinkage is unlikely, but if they do happen to shrink, I still have some space all around to account for that without being too baggy. *If you dry these, they will shrink.* 501’s are not pre shrunk because they are designed to be “broke in” by sitting in a tub of water with the jeans on, and wearing them until they dry. They are not meant to be washed every wear or even every two wears, so keep that in mind! I’m gonna put my measurements at the bottom of this so hopefully it will help some pick the correct size. Anyways, get these, you’ll love them! I find them very flattering on everyone I see wearing them:) • My measurements in inches: Waist-25 Hip-31.5 Widest point of hips(butt)-35 Thigh at widest-20.5

The first is made in Jordan, the second in Kenya. I did buy two other pairs in the two year interim, and I returned them as the “fits at natural waist” was uncomfortably well above my belly button. The pairs I kept both needed to be hemmed, but I’m used to that. I’m 5’4” with a 29” inseam. The Short length is too short at 28”, and the Regular length is too long at 30”. I love the cut and fit of these high waisted jeans! They’re super flattering. I’m 5’ 5” and 125lbs and the size small fits me perfectly. I love the cut and style of these stretchy pants! I bought a size up due to the potential for shrinkage if someone else did the laundry. I also didn't want a "muffin top" created by a tight waist band. They fit great--and if they shrank--they'd still fit. They have a wide waist band with belt loops. Sit nicely at at the hip (I'm short waisted).All the pockets are fake, but nicely detailed. I am 5'4", weigh about 145-150, and I ordered a size 12. Yes, I could've ordered a 10 and feel confident they would fit even better, but I can't risk the potential for shrinkage and muffin top. They were long, but I just folded them under the exact width of the existing hem and hemmed them myself. I wash in cold water and hang dry. The ONLY drawback (but worth it in my opinion) is they do need to be ironed on LOW (synthetic). If it's not a low heat, you risk melting your pants. The back pockets look puckered after washing, but once you iron them and wear them, you don't see the puckers. Also, the pants stretch back into form, meaning they don't stretch out and look saggy. These pants are light weight, perfect for warm/hot climates. Very slimming. Who doesn't like that?

I love the design of this 2 pc denim set by Fisoew. The 100% cotton denim is soft, breathable with the perfect amount of stretch and weight. The color is a crisp white with no yellow, which automatically cheapens anything white in my experience. The halter vest top is cool without being too revealing and the wide legged pants are everything. I don’t often buy a lot of clothing online, but I must say this is one of my favorite designs. I love the shape of both the vest and bottoms and was hoping the fit would be exactly like it fits the model in the photo. For size references, I’m 5’6 120 lbs, usually a small, and probably pretty close to the size of the model. The problem is, I referred to the reviews for size guidance. Now at the time I ordered, the majority of the reviews stated concerns about the set being totally too small. So guess what, I sized up! Now I don’t remember ordering a large, but I probably did and that’s what I received. Well, it’s huge and based on the large, even the medium would have been too large. If I had stuck to my regular size small, it would have been perfect. I should have relied on my body type and the fact that both pieces were loose fitting. Nonetheless, I appreciate the ease and comfort of this set. It’s a great look, value and is well crafted ……..🌱Happy to recommend …

I love the lightweight fabric and the relaxed fit of these pants. I have several pairs of pants in this style. These are slightly more fitted than my other wide leg pants, with a narrower leg, which I like. The pockets are fabulous, and the detachable ribbon belt is a nice feature. Because of the wide elastic waistband, these could be worn with or without the belt. These are easy care, as they can be tossed in the washer and dryer, or hung to dry. The only problem for me is that these pants are too short for me (at 5'6") to wear them with a heel of any height. Unfortunately, there is no real hem to let down for an extra couple of inches, so these will become lounge pants for me. For a shorter gal, they would be perfect, as they are comfortable and versatile pants.

I love the look and feel of these pants. They are super cute. I felt like I needed to break up the black though, so I added a gold chain belt. It actually made a huge difference, in my opinion. I guess you could achieve the same result by wearing a different color blouse. I wanted to wear the black blouse shown in my picture, so the belt helped break it up a little. The only change I would have liked to make in the pants was for them to be just a bit shorter. It might just be that I ordered the large and really needed the medium, but they seem to fit every place else. Looking at the size options, I now see that there is a short option. I might try that the next time around, even thought I considered "average" at 5'5", and not "short." Regardless, I really love these pants and can think of so many great ways to wear them.

I love the material and cut, but it is almost too full oversize cut. The fabric hangs well though and I had to try on with 4 in heels and it still hit the floor so I may have to take the seam up. (I am a long legged 5'4" so generally wear regular length jeans as slightly long while petite lengths are too short bt more than 1.5 inches) Material is very much like summer suit materials. These pants are very similar to a higher priced name brand business (more formal professional) wear pants I have from decades ago. So very pleased. And although a little full, the waist is comfortably sized only about 1" larger than a loose cut small would be, so with a belt these pants will be very look great professionally, almost a men's wear style. I will either wear with higher heels or will take it up. These are good for most fall through summer weather; may not be as warm in extreme cold winter weather. So overall.. really love this!

I love the material. It has just enough stretch. It doesn’t feel like it will slip off or like it’s too structured. I have two issues with these pants 1) way too long and 2) makes my butt look flat. I usually only wear Paige Jeans size 26, but I must say that I like the material on these jeans more than Paige. The Paige jeans are even softer than these and hug my body without feeling at all uncomfortable or tight; however, they don’t maintain their shape very well after a bunch of washes. The bottoms start to roll up and the fabric doesn’t look as good (almost like how wool starts to get fuzzy). Also, I like fit at the waist better on these jeans than any of my Paige jeans. I like that these give me a little more support in the stomach area without feeling tight and I don’t need to worry about bending down and exposing myself. I might just be getting older and prefer a sexy but covered up look over the Paige jeans which have a lower rise and are less forgiving. I’m giving these three stars because I love the material, but they just don’t look on my petite body frame. For reference, I’m 5’2 and 125 pounds.

I love the throwback style of these jeans and was looking forward to trying them. I have really mixed feelings about them though. First, the fabric has some stretch to it which is great EXCEPT the waist doesn't give much at all and despite ordering a large based on the size chart provided, these were torture to get on. The waist itself was okay once I finally got them past my hips but it was a STRUGGLE! Note that my hips are not disproportionately large compared to my other measurements. If they had a zipper, they would be perfect or if the waist band had more stretch to them, that would work as well. In terms of the length, they are a little bit long on me at 5'4" but they work with a chunky heel. They just fit as longer jeans and not ankle length as suggested in the pictures. Otherwise, the fabric is really comfortable but I'm not sure they will last very long. It's a little hard to see from my picture but the seam that goes directly down the front of the leg seems a little fragile and I don't think it would take much to tear apart. In summary, I will wear these jeans when I'm feeling motivated to do the work of getting them on but personally, I wouldn't order again if given the chance with the way the sizing is.

If I decide to hem the jeans it should not be too difficult as the hem is a 1 inch hem sewn all around the leg. They are supposed to have tummy control. I don’t think they do. To be tummy control they need a control panel sewn from the pockets to the zipper. Other than this oversight, they are terrific jeans, I love the style, the softness of the fabric and the high waist with wide, but not baggy legs.I took off one star because they are so long and the missing panel in the tummy area.

Honestly, you would not see a difference in a photo of me wearing either the small or the large size. The large is a little longer in the legs and comes up above my bellybutton, but other than that I don't feel much of a difference. It definitely feels more comfortable pulling it up so it aligns with the top of my legs to look like jeans rather than tights (and I'm short!). Although I could see this stretching to fit a heavier person, I wouldn't consider it xl sized. I usually wear small or medium in other stores, and I'd consider this more of a medium size.

I love these linen flowy pants, however they do run extremely large. I love these lounge pants! I'm currently 36 weeks pregnant, and with just 2 weeks to go until my C section, I've been living in these. I originally ordered 2 pairs, but I just ordered a 3rd because after my surgery I know I'll need loose pants that don't constrict my incision, and I have a feeling I'll only be wearing these at home for the first few weeks of recovery. I wear a size medium before and during pregnancy. I'm only 5' 3", so these do run a bit long on me. But I only wear them around the house with slippers so I don't mind one bit. With how loose fitting these are, I have no doubt I could wear a size small but I'm fine with them. They're loose enough that they don't constrict my waist, but I can also pull them about halfway up my preggo belly and they'll fit there comfortably, which I really need for after my C section. I wish I'd bought these for my first baby during my surgery recovery, and i highly recommend them before, during, and after having a baby even if you don't plan to have a C section. The weight I've gained during this pregnancy seems to be all baby, so I know I'll still fit comfortably in these after the baby comes. I may have to roll up the waist though. Highly recommend!

The stitching and hems are well-done, and I can tell these jeans will last a long time with proper care. I highly recommend them.

I love these pajamas! They are cute, cozy, and warm. I’m typically a women’s small (5’6” 130Ibs) but I like my pajamas a little loose so I ordered a medium and large. I ordered from the same seller and they even arrived in the same bag but they were made with different fabric and were wildly different in size. The large pants fit me like a glove and the top was loose but not overly so. I pulled the pants up because I like a high waisted fit and they still sit at my ankles. The fabric is stretchy and very plush. I was surprised they looked so similar to the stock photos. The buttons are on tight and there were only a few loose threads. I wish the arms were a little longer so the elastic could sit at my wrist instead of my arm but it’s still comfy. The inside of the pockets is a little scratchy and I’d prefer fabric covering the elastic on the ankle and wrist ruffles. Overall, the large pajamas were 5 stars. The medium pajama pants were too small to get on and too short. The top fit fine but the fabric on both is rougher, cheaper and less plush feeling. I would not have kept the medium even if they weren’t too small.

I LOVE these pants! Enough to deserve a thoughtful review. That said, let's start with the fit! The pants are advertised as butt lifting. I cannot personally confirm this, as i do not have a problem in that area, but i CAN confirm that the pants almost contour to your body. There's no bunching or baggy spots. The stretch is enough to provide a perfect fit but not too much that they become loose. I love the rise - I'm a sucker for high waisted pants 😍 These come right up about up to your belly button (which is my favorite height for high waisted pants). Super cute rise! Just make sure to measure yourself properly with this type of rise - there isn't gonna be a lot of wiggle room. I know my measurements well but i also try to keep in mind that a lot of women dont so i just want to put that reminder. Measure your waist at your belly button. Measure your hips at your thickest part. As for the length: I'm a little short - not quite 5'3 - so the pants are a tad long on me. However, if you're average height or like me and always wear heels, this wont be a problem. Plus, they're easily rolled at the bottom if needed. Over all these are very cute, comfortable and everything i waned. Black skinny jeans are a staple for me so I think I will be ordering another pair. I got a lot of complements on these (usually you don't get compliments on plain black pants lol).

I love these pants!!! I ordered them for a trip to Amsterdam next month and I wanted pants other than jeans. I figured I'd give these a try because they seemed to get really great reviews and I found a pattern I love so why not give them a shot. When opened the package, the leg part looked a bit big and I thought they would look like Aladdin pants (LOL) but they don't seem so big when they're actually on. They're roomy but not too big and the material is light but comfy and soft-ish.I love how these come in a variety of prints/colors and are super easy to coordinate an outfit! I plan to wear these with a black tank or black tee and sandals (like I'm wearing in the photo) but I could totally see myself wearing a pair of flats or even heels to dress them up if I wanted to. I also think these would be comfy enough to wear all day while I'm sight-seeing, visiting museums, strolling around town, or going to a flea market.I typically dress in darker clothes but wanted something different, something funky for my trip to Amsterdam. I love bohemian, though I never thought I'd actually like these so much! It might be hard to tell in my photo but they are a perfect dark blue and black floral/mandala type pattern (the color is actually called "Mandala Blue"). The pattern looks exactly how I thought it would be (or is even better!) in person!For reference, I am 5'9" and about 180 pounds. The length of the pants is just right, a little above my ankles. Not too long and not too short even for a taller person. My only beef with these is that I think they look better high waisted however doing so gives me a little bit of camel toe which isn't ideal so I have to pull them down a bit more to my hips for them to be more comfortable (or maybe with a different tee or tank they could work better for me high waisted). I wish the waist part was a bit stretchier or longer, which is the only reason why I gave them 4 stars, but this is definitely not a deal breaker. I was more concerned about these being long enough for my liking and roomy in the leg/thigh area which they very much are.Aside from the high waisted fit thing (which is probably more on my body than the pants themselves), I am absolutely obsessed with these! I didn't expect to love them so much but I really do. They are effortless, super cute and versatile! I can't wait to wear these! I'm so glad I took a chance and tried something I might not have if it weren't for my trip. I will definitely be wearing these into the summer well after my trip to Europe! Thanks so much for a great item!Update: washed these once and I am noticing a few lines on these where the print has faded or the black bled through the fabric? Not sure whether I'm explaining right but they are definitely not wet spots from water as they're a straight line. I LOVE these pants. That may be an understatement. I am 5'5, 127lbs, and the mediums fit me nearly perfectly. I need to take in the waist line by just a tiiiiny bit for them to be perfect, but I also have a pretty defined hourglass figure (tiny waist, larger hips/butt), which is why I love wearing pants like these. I've spent years shopping worldwide for pants JUST like these, and these ones are among the most comfortable, durable, thick, and flattering that I've purchased. I have two pairs of the khaki and two of the black, and I'd like to buy more since I wear them daily, but... That's where my 4 stars instead of 5 come in. I've ordered from this company a few times, and the quality and color of the material is hit or miss and tends to change. My first pairs of khaki and black were thick, good-quality material, good and consistent color, but when I reordered another pair of khaki - this time in both medium and small to see if the small fit any differently - the fabric of the small was noticeably different. It was thinner and more yellow-toned than the medium pair I loved so much. I returned them. The mediums were fine; they were made of the same soft, thick linen my original pair had been made of, same soft khaki color, etc. A month later, I tried ordering another two. a medium pair of black and a medium pair of khaki, assuming the quality problem was perhaps linked to the small size only, but the mediums that just arrived are the color and quality I had returned before! In my photo, the pair on the left are the new pair that I'm returning. It's difficult to tell in a photo since you have to physically touch them to truly feel the difference, but it's absolutely noticeable in-person, so much so that I can't stand the feeling of them against my legs. The black was also the poorer-quality fabric. I decided to eat the cost and try washing them immediately to see if that helped, but even washed and air-dried, they're noticeably different quality from my first black pair I ordered two months ago. I'm disheartened - I want to buy so many of these so I never have to worry about ruining a pair, but I don't want to keep ordering and returning in hopes that the pair that arrives will be the quality and color I love so much. There's definitely a consistency problem, so be warned of that. If you end up receiving the better quality pants, you'll love them. I know I do.

I love these pants. Took them right out of the package and put them on. Okay, my review says "fit as expected." But that's because I read all of the other reviews and ordered a 2x. I am 5'6" and weigh about 180, with a bit of a tummy and some of that weight in the rear as well. Depending on the manufacturer and cut of garment, I can wear a Large, X-Large, or 2X. Around US size 16. About the color, and translucency, I ordered teal, and the color is perfect. The fabric is rayon, so it's very much like tightly-woven silk, actually, like sueded silk, and no more sheer than a silk blouse, for example. If I stand with the sun behind me, I am pretty sure you will vaguely see my legs silhouetted, but not the color of my underwear. Dark prints under light colors might show a little. The bottoms are hemmed and rest on the tops of my feet. A 1-inch heel will do the trick. UPDATE: I first saw them inside under incandescent light, which is very yellow, so they looked more teal. That is, a saturated greeny blue. In daylight, they look more blue. In the photo you can compare them to my t-shirt. In response to other reviews, I do plan to wear them to work. UPDATE: May 2017 I eventually got these pants in black, light green, and brown, in 1x, which are about a half-inch shorter. Otherwise I tend to step on the hem when going upstairs. The 1x waist still fits fine. The wide, wide legs are fun to walk in. They kind of flow and swing. They look great with tunics. Or a long vest, or one of the long, side-tail "cardigans" from Meaneor. The dye never ran, but I do wash the darks with other dark colors. I hang them to dry. I wore them a lot last summer and fall. I am updating my review because I am back for more!

I love these retro hippy jeans! I had to send a few pairs back, but now I have a size that fits just right. For reference I’m 5’7” 150lbs with a thick middle and thin legs. I’m 59 y/o but tend to shop in the juniors department because of my legs being thin. I’m a woman’s size 10 so that’s what I ordered at first. These jeans run large in the middle and they’re very stretchy. I sent them back and got a size 8. They were also a bit too roomy in the middle. They fit snug, but I could stretch the waist out several inches. Pushing my luck, I sent them back and got a size 6. This girl hasn’t been a size 6 since the 70’s! They fit and felt great. The waist is not too tight to expose a muffin top and actually still has a bit of room if I stretch it out. Posting photos to show measurements. If you have thicker legs, there is still plenty of stretch in these pants as long as you like them snug. The length was perfect even after I machine washed them in cold water. I hung to dry overnight then put in the dryer for 5 minutes just to soften up. Directions say hand wash, but I raised five kids so I never had time for such nonsense :)

I love these stretchy, pull-on skinny jeans! They're very comfortable, and the short size is a pretty good length for my petite 5'1" height. If I were 1" or 2" taller, they would fit perfectly, but they work totally fine as is. The supportive wide waistband is a wonderful design for tummy flattening. Dealing with a chronic pain illness makes me want to wear comfortable clothes whenever possible. I'm often at home wearing pajamas, but for being out, these jeans are an awesome choice that mix comfort, fashion and efficiency. These jeans are perfect for just about any occasion, whether it be work, play, a date, or anything in between. They're rugged enough for hiking and soft enough for lounging. The fabric is very smooth and stretchy. The only problem is that the material easily attracts lots of fur and dust if you have pets that shed. These jeans aren't the most butt-lifting like most regular tight denim jeans are. They're also not as tight as jeggings although they're still skinny. But these jeans are still very flattering and form-fitting, and you can't beat the comfort level. I like these a lot! I think you will too.

These jeans are perfect for just about any occasion, whether it be work, play, a date, or anything in between. They're rugged enough for hiking and soft enough for lounging. The fabric is very smooth and stretchy. The only problem is that the material easily attracts lots of fur and dust if you have pets that shed. These jeans aren't the most butt-lifting like most regular tight denim jeans are. They're also not as tight as jeggings although they're still skinny. But these jeans are still very flattering and form-fitting, and you can't beat the comfort level. I like these a lot! I think you will too.

I love these style of pants. This is my third pair of Willow’s. Unfortunately the previous two have ripped in about 6 months or less. While I wore these almost daily, it was not hard use. I used them mainly for office work so was disappointed when they ripped so fast. This new pair tag says they are 32 in length but are more like 34’s. It’s not a big deal but just slightly annoying. They are slightly darker than my previous pairs also. Otherwise they are really comfy pants with a slight stretch. I am 5”5’ and 175 wearing a 11-32(most likely miss marked 34’s).

I love these way these feel and fit. The cotton is really soft, seams appear solid. The color is bright and they are just comfy. I really could lounge in them, feel like pjs. I'm 5ft 200lbs and bought a size large. Although I liked and wanted the relaxed feel, I could've purchased a small and would still comfortably fit in them. I have a lot of wiggle room and stretch, even in the waistband, which has a drawstring. I would knot the ends to prevent string from going in hole when washed. The waistband stretches another 5-6 inches out for me and my belly is 41 inches. I prefer the gathering at the ankles because tight fits around the ankle aren't flattering on me but with that being said a tall person can rock these maybe up to 5'5 or 5'6. I prefer the roominess of the large as it hides my fupa. I bought four designs and only have to return one as the red plaid has a hole in the right pocket. Oh, it has pockets! Not shallow but not deep enough to hold cell phone unless you had a tighter fit, like if I had gotten the small the phone would fit snug. They are not transparent so no worry about underwear showing. They are lightweight. I'll update the breathability later as I live in Florida and it gets hot. They have found a repeat customer. I'll update after I do my first wash to see how it holds up. Good value for the money.

I must say, I am very pleased I bought these magical mom jeans in black and medium wash. My waist is 25.5", 33.5" hips. I ordered size 3. They fit beautifully. I have smallish hips and a wider waist so I was worried that the waist band would be too tight. It is snug, but it does have some stretch, so I think as long as I don't gain weight around my waist, they should be comfy. I have only tried these on so far, I haven't actually worn them anywhere or washed them. They are soft and stretchy. I thought the black might be more crispy and less stretchy, but they are about the same. The fit on both pair was identical. The jeans really make the most of my small butt, and they make my waist look smaller, which gives the illusion of more of a heart-shape, when in reality, my waist to hip transition is pretty pretty straight. I can do a deep squat in these jeans without having to adjust them. In the pictures I am wearing them with panties that create lines. I don't want these to shrink, so I will probably wash in cold and hang dry. The fabric looks like it has good structure and is unlikely to stretch out or sag, but time will tell. I paid less than $30 each, which I think is a great price for what I received. Plus I ordered them day before yesterday and they arrived this morning. Can't beat that!

I needed a new denim jacket to fit my current size. I liked this darker wash color and classic look. It is important to measure your bust, waist, and hips to determine your size; don't go off the standard sizes you buy in the stores. I saw some reviews from women who also bought an XL and claimed it was too small; and although their weight was slightly less than mine, they were also much shorter than me. I am an athletic body type with broad shoulders, but still have womanly thickness. In the photos I have on a wireless bra, but I dont think a push up would fill in the chest area too much more (38/40 C). Your true measurements are the key. If you get your correct size, your abdomen will have a straight line silhouette (like in my photo), but if you want to show your curves, a size down may do the trick. If you are hoping to wear thicker shirts or sweaters with the jacket, I suggest sizing up one or two sizes, because the arms are a form fit. The denim is light weight (perfect for TX), but not thin (great for "fall" and "winter" we have in our state). It has enough stretch in the fabric that you can unbutton the sleeve cuffs and roll them up as I did in my photos. I normally wear a size 4 or 6 in jeans but ordered these in size 10 due to their chart and I'm glad I did because these fit. These jeans are very soft and extremely stretchy--not a lot of structure if that makes sense. The tag says 1% spandex but I believe there's a lot more spandex than what their tag says. In addition to a regular set of belt loops these jeans have a narrower 2nd set of loops right beneath for the included decorative denim strap/belt. The denim strap/belt is easily removable (just looped through), which is good because you'll have to undo it each time you need to use the restroom. I don't have a very full behind and I find these jeans more flattering on me than most. At 5'4" these are a great length on me. I hope this information helps with your purchasing decision.

I love the jeans, but you never know with sizing, every jean is made from different material, so you really have to consider that when ordering. For the price and free shipping, I consider theses jeans a most reasonable deal. I would definitely buy these WallFlower jeans again.

I normally wear my jeans with a straight leg that are too small to take off with your shoes on. I liked the way these jeans looked in the ad. They look curvy with a flare at the bottom, and I thought I'd give them a try. These are a good-quality pair of jeans with five pockets. The front pockets are pretty deep, with a lining that feels soft and silky. The zipper is silver-colored, although it's a little stiff. Upon receiving them and seeing their shape, I could tell that they didn't have the flared or "bell bottom" style. I tried them on right away and then took them off even quicker. Being plus-size most of my life, I've always hated jeans like this. It's not just the bottom that's flared, but actually the whole leg. I've always felt that plus-size jeans were made with the thought that just because you have a larger waist, you must have legs the size of an elephant. A bell bottom I can handle, but not elephant legs. I used to wear the largest size I could find, but now I wear anywhere from a size 12 to 16 depending on the pants and the amount of stretch. I ordered these in size 16, and they are too big; plus, they are very stretchy on top of that. They're just too uncomfortably big without any kind of curvy shape to them. Additionally, they are too long for me, but I'm 5'5", and I usually run into this problem. Currently, 25% off is a fair price, and they're a good pair of jeans for someone; just not for me.

I normally wear my jeans with a straight leg that are too small to take off with your shoes on. I liked the way these jeans looked in the ad. They look curvy with a flare at the bottom, and I thought I'd give them a try. These are a good-quality pair of jeans with five pockets. The front pockets are pretty deep, with a lining that feels soft and silky. The zipper is silver-colored, although it's a little stiff. Upon receiving them and seeing their shape, I could tell that they didn't have the flared or "bell bottom" style. I tried them on right away and then took them off even quicker. Being plus-size most of my life, I've always hated jeans like this. It's not just the bottom that's flared, but actually the whole leg. I've always felt that plus-size jeans were made with the thought that just because you have a larger waist, you must have legs the size of an elephant. A bell bottom I can handle, but not elephant legs. I used to wear the largest size I could find, but now I wear anywhere from a size 12 to 16 depending on the pants and the amount of stretch. I ordered these in size 16, and they are too big; plus, they are very stretchy on top of that. They're just too uncomfortably big without any kind of curvy shape to them. Additionally, they are too long for me, but I'm 5'5", and I usually run into this problem. Currently, 25% off is a fair price, and they're a good pair of jeans for someone; just not for me.

I normally wear size 12 and these fit true yo size. A little stretch in fabric which is great. High waisted which helps with little muffin top. 5ft5” 155 lbs and with my boots they are perfect. Pockets a little lower by design but look perfect when on.Would buy a different color

I normally wear size medium/29 jeans and the medium I ordered fits, however they are clearly short on me (5’7”). I don’t think they’re that flattering either. I ordered them because I wanted pants that were more comfortable than jeans, and they do feel that way, but since I’m not a fan of how they look on me, I probably won’t wear them out much. Especially since I’d have to steam/iron them.

They are long on me (I have them folded under in the picture) so I'll need to get them hemmed but that's no big deal. These jeans are awesome, highly recommend.

I order a size 2 in the Capetown waterless color. I usually wear a size 4 in jeans but lately I've noticed a lot of people have changed their sizing and I'm ordering more brands in a size 2. I'm 5'3" and 113 lbs, pear shaped. These weren't the best jeans I've ever bought but they are somewhat flattering compared to most brands I've bought as of late. They are slightly loose in the waist still but they don't gap nearly as bad as other jeans seem to do. I'm short but I don't buy " short" jeans because they end up looking like high waters. These hit at a decent length without bunching up too much and also looked good rolled at the bottom. I have a very short torso and these hit just below my belly button so if you have a longer torso it may not be high waisted on you. I'm not sure I'd order more but I'll be keeping these.

I ordered 1 pair of these to start and thought they were great! They fit true to size and felt very comfortable and stretchy (almost like a jeggings material/feel)! Although I don't normally wear jeans in the "long" length, I ordered it in these since the regular length was listed as an inch or so shorter than most regular length jeans. I am 5'5". The long length on the first pair seemed great. They were a little long, but I would rather that and make a small rolled cuff at the bottom than too short. Because I liked the first pair so much that I just got, I ordered 2 more. None of them are consistent. They are all made of different blends of material and have a different texture. The sizing and styles are not consistent. Each pair is a different length. The design on the back pockets of each is different. It does show this in the product photos, but I didn't expect the blends/textures and lengths to also be different. While each pair does fit (some longer than others), the fits do vary. The front pockets are also different. Two of the pairs have what I would consider "normal" front pockets for women's jeans. The 3rd pair has pockets less than 2 inches deep that they're basically pointless! I love the 1st pair I got but will likely send the other 2 back. Just don't expect them to be consistent.

I ordered 2 pairs of white jeans in same exact size (28/6) on Prime day in October. This pair and one more expensive by Liverpool. I tried this pair on first and was beside myself. I gained a few pounds over the summer but what stunned I could barely get these jeans over my hips and forget buttoning them. Then I tried on Liverpool and those fit just fine even with my extra 6-7lbs of summer snacking gain so thankfully I’m still a size 6. Sometimes you literally get what you pay for. These pants are definitely more than 1 size too small than a 28 waist. Definitely size up by 1 size…maybe more frankly. Also the hems looked like someone took a hacksaw to them - unwearable and I would have had to trim them and distress them again myself if I kept them.

I am 5'5 154lbs and usually wear 30/31 or size 10 pants. I have belly and hips but not really any booty or leg meat SO! that being said, I can't speak for other woman in the same height/weight class as I, but for me, these fit loosely with a bit of sag that just didn't look appealing. I'm 5'5" and found the inseem really worked nicely without being too short (they're not as short as they appear on the model, unless maybe you hike them up more?). I do like the fabric. Material is soft and stretchy. I would say it’s more of a light weight material.

I ordered a size up and it was perfect. I often struggle with pants being too short but these were pretty good, I'd say a 30" inseam. They would also be awesome if you needed them hemmed because it would add to the nice drape. The paper bag top is flattering and adds coverage. They would make a great travel pant.

I ordered a size xl. I'm 5'3 and 160 lbs. I usually wear a size15-16 jeans. So I decided to give these jeans a try because of the coloring which is different than regular ones. And now they are my new favorite jeans. I have gotten nothing but compliments, and wows lol. The 1 thing that sold me on these jeans is definitely the coloring The 2 different colors are absolutely amazing. And I feel great in them.

I ordered a small due to some reviews mentioning the stretch in the pants. There was in fact enough stretch to fit around my size 4/6 lower body. I am around 5’6 and immediately felt how short they were at the bottom. With heels, I was able to wear them a couple of times before giving up on the length. The material is so comfortable and worth the price, but the length could be longer for the size. A medium may have been too loose. The button also is very loose to begin with. They ended up fitting my mom perfectly who is a size 4 and 5’4.

I ordered a Small PETITE in indigo. I am 5 feet tall and wear a size 4. I was hoping they would fit shorter, however they do hit at ankle. Pants fit loosely, I would say closer to a 6 in the waist (chart says 4-6) but because of drawstring in waistline they can be pulled in. My hip measurement from chart is between a size XS and S but because of the cotton/rayon content I was fearful of shrinkage and did not want them to fit tight as these are a more casual look. They did not shrink to a noticeable degree. Material is on the lighter side for pants, I would consider these better for warmer seasons. Very comfortable and soft to wear. I disagree with some reviews that say pants have stretch. There is no elastic in material, and I don't consider cotton/rayon to be stretchy. Front pockets fit my entire hand (or cell phone), however back pocket is shorter.

I ordered a small tall. The tag says small 4-6, 32 inseam. I’m 5’2 and wear a 32 inch inseam so If you’re buying these for a tall woman you should ask their inseam first. There’s no elastic in the waistband so they should remain comfortable. The belly portion is soft, smooth & stretchy. The rest of the pant is a loose fit with a wide leg. The fabric is soft and has plenty of stretch for movement. The pockets aren’t super deep but my phone does fit.

I ordered according to the size chart, I wear a size 2, usually need long length but went with 30 because skinny need to be ankle usually. I tried them on immediately, and I could not be happier!! The dark red is more plum, even my picture looks red, but they seem more purple to me, and that's what I was hoping for! The green are also more muted, but still pretty. They are higher than many of my high waisted jeans, these hit right below my belly button, but I have a long torso so high is never high enough! I'm 5'7", 125ish, athletic build, with a booty. I get the reviews saying they run small, but I don't think they're quite right, they are a challenge to get on if you have any thighs and a butt, I mean very very hard to get on! But once up they're perfect, and I don't think more stretch would be as good for how these look. So just be ready for a battle every time you have to pee or dress/undress! These are so flattering and nice and a thick material. I'm so glad I took a chance!
